I have great Neurologists to help me with my auto-immune condition. They don't have dietary intervention on the radar, though. The advice is to eat healthy and exercise. Thats ok. I get that. I talk with other patients who are finding ways to understand what happened and what can help.

The system in Quebec must be similar to the one in Ontario. btw, a lot of Toronto families were without electricity as of recent: over 100,000 at one point.

I have not finished Dr Kruse's new blog yet.

The big plus, for me at least, is that Dr Kruse is able to put forth some kind of logical explanation as to why we are getting sick in record numbers. I wonder at what might be driving the increase in pathological conditions and disease numbers. When people get serious diseases despite healthy lifestyles, it becomes obvious that something is wrong. Dr Kruse is tracking a good number of factors that might be increasing our vulnerability to disease.

EMF, circadian mismatches, poor nutritional strategies: sometimes we think we are doing the right thing and we are not.

More-so than any other blog that I have seen, Dr Kruse is helping us to make sense of it all. Some ideas he puts forth are real keepers, some I am not sure about and some I don't understand. Its worth the effort though. And I am committed to keep reading and following the blog as much as time permits.

Have a pleasant day. Its morning in Australia is it not ?

### Ricardo — 2014-01-12T06:35:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105422>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105385)
>
> Did something change in your environment when you were 35? home, work? \[other than the candles on your birthday cake of course! introduction of non native EMF? moved/new house? mould? lyme disease?
>
> Is this common with auto-immune issues ... they just seemingly come out of nowhere?
>
> Do you didjeridu?

I can't recall anything out of the ordinary that happened at the time when my auto-immune issues started. The hypothesis is that it could have started by the residual effects of a bacterial or viral infection. Yes, as a matter of fact little is known about the true causes of many neurological problems. There are a lot of variables.

I, personally have found that ketones from MCT and Coconut fats are very helpful for my auto-immune disorder. Further, it makes sense to me that any condition that has impaired uptake of glucose to cells due to insulin resistance could benefit from ketones as fuel.
Ketones:
First, they lower inflammation.
Second, they have anti-oxydent effects.
Third, the human brain and organs, except for the liver, all perform very well on ketones.
Fourth... this is a hypothesis.. I believe they create less metabolic by-products. (not sure about that)

Our survival as a species may be largely due to the fact that we do well on ketones.

Consider this.

Grokky Grocardo gets hungry. He leaves the cave and goes hunting. His physical efforts to find prey and his mental alertness are fueled by ketones. He gets hungrier ( ketosis) and his mind and senses get sharper: "gotta find food to eat or my kids and wife will starve." ( I believe we are at our best while fueled by ketones ).
This quick and easy switch from glucose as fuel to ketones as fuel starts at Grokky's birth. As a newborn baby he won't get any milk from his mom for at least 36 hours. Being fat, babies are fat...he lives off that fat... his body is in ketosis until his mom has milk to feed him.

I believe we are made to be mentally sharper and to do better physically when we are fueled by ketones. Ketones made us better more effective hunters.

Then back at the cave after he feeds his family and himself a feast from his hunt and gatherings: his blood sugar goes up a bit and so does the available glucose. He begins to fuel with glucose again. His senses and reflexes are not as sharp after a big dinner. So he relaxes and thinks about tossing his boomerang.

Did I didjeridu ? hmm I think I dijerididn't. But thank you for reminding me.

PS: Is it true that women who move to Australia from Toronto dijeridu a lot ?

### Ricardo — 2014-01-12T16:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105442>

I was up late last night.

A dear friend's mother is getting symptoms of Alzheimer... not the best news. My friend remembered what I had mentioned to her about coconut fat and ketones. However, her mother refuses to get tested since she feels that the meds available are not very effective anyway. And when my friend asked her to try coconut oil she said she doesn't like it. So I was just figuring out a strategy. btw.. I think this is where ketone esters would be a potentially good solution.

Alzheimer is epidemic and the curve is on the upswing. 500,000 Canadians have Alzheimer. In the states over 5 million people have Alzheimer.

The best solution so far, I believe, is ketones. Small tests have shown it could be promising.

So getting back to Grokky. After hours, perhaps even days of pursuit, he sprints toward his prey and musters the strength to throw a spear with enough force and accuracy to kill his target of choice and thereby secure food for his family. All this is ketone powered. ( unless of course Lucy packed him a lunch ). The point is if his biology relied exclusively on glucose as fuel, then when glucose petered out it would have been "curtains". Instead, ketones made it possible for him to survive.

I mentioned that once I became efficient at fat as fuel I became determined never to go back to carbs ( glucose) as my main fuel. Fuggedaboudit ! For my biology..ketones are a superior fuel...by a country mile.

Wanna hear something exciting ? There is currently a hypothesis that a ketone fueled body may be more resistant to the big C. Thats not to say a person fueled by ketones 24/7 would absolutely not get cancer, it means that the increased resistance would be there. The reasoning seems sound enough. Research and tests need to be done.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-16T06:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-105865>

Caroline,

How have you been ?

Just a quick post.

I know I have been posting a lot about ketones: I believe their potential for providing healing, wellness and quality of life is extraordinary.

Because they have the potential to aide in healing diseases that are treated with expensive pharmaceuticals, I somehow doubt that there will be many, if any, large clinical trials. Funding for large trials may be a problem. However the anecdotal evidence will, I think, attain critical mass at some point and it will become known on a widespread basis as an potential alternative to Alzheimer meds for instance. I hope it does.

So to continue..Consider this:

When our friend Grokky is born, his mother's body is producing milk which will contain medium chain triglicerides (MCT). Its the only time our human bodies will produce MCT. Now that in itself tells me something. Nature, in its wisdom, provides newborns with MCTs to start life on the right foot. It gives them ketones to thrive on.

While Grokky is pursuing a food source he is getting exercise which also adds to the ketones he is getting from hunger.

In fact, ketone levels for exercise are in the same range as ketones levels from mcts or coconut oil.

Ketone levels
Exercise ............................0.3-0.5 mmol/l
Coconut oil / MCT oil ..........0.3-0.5 mmol/l

Both starvation and a ketogenic diet are about the same in terms of producing ketones .......... 2.5-5 mmol/l

As a matter of fact premmies (premature infants) were given drops of MCT by neonatologists in the 70's. They did this until the companies that produce baby formulas decided to include it in their products.

In addition, I know of at least one pediatric neurosurgeon who would insist that mct and coconut oil be included in the diets of hospitalized children with brain and/or neurological issues.. ( especially children having brain injuries resulting from cranial impact.. car accidents, for instance).

I left a message for my friend whose mom is starting to have symptoms of Alzheimers. I left her a bottle of MCT oil along with some suggestions. I believe coconut oil has its place, even with the addition to the diet of MCT oil. So since her mother does not like coconut oil, she could just rub it on her arms as she would a moisturizer. Coconut oil absorbs into the skin very easily. It gets to work quickly. Its considered a beauty aid in some cultures.

Her mother is 84. Statistics reveal that at age 85 we have a 50% chance of having Alzheimer.

Be well,

PS: I am not forgetting any topics you brought up: I will converse with you about them next week.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-20T02:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106252>

Hello Caroline ( <--- correct spelling  )

How are you ?

So a typical day ?.. I'll post in separate sections so its not too long.

I get up at 7 am.

I take probiotics of which I have a selection. I look to start the day with
probiotics then fats and electron rich foods for reasons previously mentioned.

Breakfast is 5 tbs of a sulfered protein such as quark cheese into which I
thoroughly blend
2 Tbs of cold pressed organic coconut oil
1 Tbs of extra virgin olive oil
1 heaping Tbs of freshly ground flax seeds.

I normally avoid dairy however the blending of these two substances (fat and
sulfered protein) creates a third substance which I can easily tolerate.

While blending, I add coconut milk until it is the consistancy of thick yogourt.
After the mix is thoroughly blended.. no sign of oil at all... I flavor it in one of
two ways. It can be flavored a number of ways: these two are my favorite.

The juice of a half of freshly squeezed lemon or lime plus a bit of stevia.

or

Finely ground green tea and a bit of stevia.

I am working toward the Epi-paleoRx recommended amount of protein and
carbs so now I will add a lettuce-wrap filled with salmon. I still need to consume
more carbs to attain to the recommended amount for breakfast... working on it.

This combination of fats is highly active.
In addition to being a nutritional powerhouse, it will counteract the accumulation of toxins in all tissues.
It will support the absorption of oxygen by the organism.
In addition, it will help regulate and optimize the
entire metabolic process.

Be well,

R. R.

PS: If I'm in a real hurry I will just take 2 Tbs of EVOO. I am comfortable with
this, and can function well until noon w/o hunger. However I am aware it is
not balanced as choices go.

" Lucy, how come you are so quiet ? "

Caroline thank you for the infos. I am interested in joining the Optimal Reset.

I'm kinda swamped with work these days and will come by as time permits. We have, among my friends, more or less divided the follow up on some important subjects such as food additives and the reliability of our food sources. At least this way we can get better information with less time invested.

Here in Quebec we often rent church basements at Christmas time for family reunions. Its been unsettling to hear in recent years who has cancer and who has Alzheimer or other diseases. It seems like more and more people are getting sick, more-so than in previous years. The statistics are not promising, especially for some diseases. We are looking for answers.

I like that the approach of people like Dr Beliveau and Dr Kruse is upbeat. After all, it should be fun and interesting to look after one's health and enjoy intelligent food and lifestyle choices.

Unfortunately, there is a lot of erroneous and questionable information even within the health movement. Personally, I look for some scientific basis when that is possible. In addition, what may make sense for one person, may not for another because of biological diversity.

At the end of the day, each of us has to determine what works best, what moves us toward homeostasis and what level of ketosis is optimal at any given time.

I think its best not to jolt the body, not to go too quickly. Steady progress is preferable and trumps occasional train wrecks from trying to go too fast.

I have a friend who works in medical equipment who told me "We are all athletes." Interesting thought, isn't it ?

Be well,

PS Please tell me what you did that was so fanatical: I'm trying to get up to speed.

PPS About protein powders.. I don't use them. Some amino acids can be converted to glucose. I prioritize ketones as fuel and less glucose is better for what my objectives are. I know its a trend to consume protein shakes: I see guys in gyms walking around with their protein shakes. I also know that some of them are taking protein shakes every 3 hours to promote anabolism. The kidneys are not meant to function that way. I suspect they are doing themselves more harm than good.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-23T15:40:36+0200

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106732)

Hello Caroline,

Interested yes.. but I just don't have time to follow the news.

I wondered if its all that important to follow the news: I asked a friend who teaches at a local university and he said he only reads the "Toronto Globe". So I only get a little (at best) international and national news from the newspaper.

I am always amazed at how the presence of ketones manifests itself in the human body.
They work at the cellular level and so a lot of good things can happen.

A women who is the wife of a friend tried some MCT oil: 2 Tbs a day as a "preventative".
After only 2 weeks the dark circles under her eyes ( she has had them for at least the 30 years I have known her ) disappeared !
Well, needless to say she is thrilled ! And I am thrilled for her.

I find it interesting to observe how electron rich fats can accelerate healing. I have seen people try this and that to resolve various issues. Things really start to turn around when they go with electron rich fats and eliminate bad fats.

Maintaining a negatively charged outer membrane is the basis of health for our cells. Nothing achieves this better than electron rich fats.

What I am very interested in these days is how the various good fats "play out " in my biology.

Do you remember the Canadian lipid specialist Udo ?
He wrote "Fats that Heal and Fats that Kill". He also has tried various good fats and noted how they "play out" in his biology. I'm keeping notes.

I am looking into grounding. It makes sense: the earth is a tremendous electron donor. As a kid I would observe how farm livestock would lay down on muddy areas to get better when they were sick or injured. Their instinct guided them. I thought it was the mineral content of the mud, however the mud might have acted as a better electron conductor.

If you didn't get to hear the 3 hour presentation by Dr Kruse it could be because of a shockwave flash problem on your PC. I'm not up on PC tech however it is explained on the net. It was a worthwhile presentation. I look forward to hearing more.

Be well,

### Ricardo — 2014-01-24T02:32:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106846>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106796)
>
> I have a dear friend whose DH has just been diagnosed with Dementia .... he hasn't been well for years - prostrate cancer etc.
> .... I suggested making him a smoothie using coconut cream and MCT oil and a banana and a bit of frozen raspberries .... any other suggestions?

Super suggestion !

MCT may be able to produce sufficient ketones to improve cognitive function in the Alzheimer brain. That insight has been around since the year 2000.

The knowledge that ketones could reduce the cerebral infarction in brain injury was already part of the body of knowledge at that time. Many other benefits have since been attributed to ketones.

Consider this : ketones, even at low levels, can increase cerebral blood flow by approximately 39% !

Ketones drive the production of ATP better than other alternative sources of fuels.

So, yes, coconut oil and mct oil should be added to your friend's husband's diet. In addition, getting at least 2 dosages a day would be better.

As we age, there is a shift in the types of fuel used by the brain.

When we are young it is 100:1 glucose to alternative fuels.
As we get older it becomes.... 29:1.
Those numbers radically change in the Alzheimer brain.... 2:1.

From these statistics it becomes clear that as we age we should access more ketones from dietary sources and strategies.

I think its great that you are suggesting quality ketogenic agents for your friend's husband ! <----Lucy knows best !

Be well,

R.R.

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106862)
>
> Is there a book you would recommend that I can suggest to my friend?

Sure.

" Alzheimer's Disease: What If There Was a Cure ? " By Mary Newport MD. 2nd Edition. Available at Amazon. In addition, it can be downloaded as a kindle book.

I read the entire first edition. A very compelling story: a triumph of the human spirit. They say love finds a way.

The author is a neonatologist whose husband had severe symptoms of Alzheimer. After research, she gave her husband some coconut oil in his cereal one morning. After just 4 days "we knew our lives had changed".

Details of her husband's recovery are encouraging and inspiring.

She is a delight to read. There are scientific explanations and illustrations as well as a complete lipid strategy.

Caroline, your energy is noticeable. You must be making some good choices.

Be well,

R.R.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-25T02:09:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-106947>

So to complete what I mentioned earlier..

As we age our brains are designed.. at this point in our evolution ...to begin to use alternate sources of fuel to a greater extent than when we are younger. It behooves us to choose the right fuel.

In addition, the human body is designed to switch to alternate sources of fuel after glucose stores become depleted.
Alternate sources could also be aminos (by way of gluconeogenesis), lactate or fatty acids. I say fuggedaboudit: Go with a clean burning fuel and forget the metabolic byproducts and potential problems associated with other alternate fuel sources.

Ketones are far superior as an alternate energy source... by a country mile.

My best academic sucesses in University took place in the year that I went to the swimming pool for lunch instead of eating. So as we saw earlier, exercise potentially can provide as high a level of ketones as MCT. In addition, since I was depleting glucose from an hour of swimming with no compensation from carbs, I probably was becoming ketogenic. I believe for most of that year I was in a state of ketosis. My mind was sharp.. in a good place.

I wanted to understand and duplicate this sharpness and resistance to fatigue. However, it wasn't until the late 90's that I started to understand how ketones could be advantageous as a fuel source. I have been following the research ever since.

Y'know at the IVIG unit, I get to meet with people who have MS. What an insidious disease ! I guess we all have dreams.. one of mine is that we live in a world where information is coherent and readily available in order to address diseases with treatments that work.

I believe MS can be resolved or, at the very least, stabilized, with dietary intervention. And I believe the basis of such a dietary intervention would be ketones.

Well, I will be away "cocooning" to get some work done.

Lucy, be well, and keep on "doin it the way you're doin it ".

### Ricardo — 2014-02-10T01:43:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-109350>

Hello Lucykins,

Howya been ?

Just been going through some data on ketones and came across something I wanted to take a minute to get out there. Dr. Mary Newport's husband Steve is apparently retaining his progress in overcoming Alzheimer's. She mentioned something that is important to know. Twice he missed his morning oil ( MCT + coconut oil ) due to fasting bloodwork or travel:

" He became “dazed and confused” with a full-blown tremor. He returned to normal shortly after receiving the oil. "
Mary Newport MD

My "take" from that is once on a program of recovery for AD patients it is therefore important to respect the dosages and timing.

I also want to add a quick word about some feedback I got from friends starting to consume MCT and coconut oil in appreciable amounts. I hear about periods of "euphoria". Well considering the comments coming from AD patients such as "its like someone turned on the lights" and " I feel like I got my life back" its probably just a normal reaction to the enhanced fuel source (ketones). In that case, I suggest just staying grounded: even some light physical activity should help.
The brain will soon get accustomed to the increased amounts of ketones. The euphoria is temporary, however, the increased mental clarity is usually experienced on an ongoing basis.

Be well,
R.R.

PS The Optimal Reset 2014 is great ! Lots of useful information !

guys, i do not know about you but in my case ketosis elevated my fasting glucose to 95+. I did some home tests with carb loading over a few days and i seem to respond well, giving I do not have 50g of sugar without few days of slow upping. in all the cases i had to leave my GP, she laughed at me and my article about physiological insulin resistance on ketogenic diet when I refused glucose load test and received prediabetic label. She as well said, that macronutrient ratios play NO role in your fasting glucise levels and your ability to do glucose load test. Will this be a steady feature of longterm high fat low carb? I have done it only Since November, but 2 years gives youa. different perspective. By the way, I tend to have lower glucose after BAB breakfast than before.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-06T21:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lipids-and-cortisol.9236/post-113827>

> [kovita said:](/goto/post?id=113825)
>
> guys, i do not know about you but in my case ketosis elevated my fasting glucose to 95+. I did some home tests with carb loading over a few days and i seem to respond well, giving I do not have 50g of sugar without few days of slow upping. in all the cases i had to leave my GP, she laughed at me and my article about physiological insulin resistance on ketogenic diet when I refused glucose load test and received prediabetic label. She as well said, that macronutrient ratios play NO role in your fasting glucise levels and your ability to do glucose load test. Will this be a steady feature of longterm high fat low carb? I have done it only Since November, but 2 years gives youa. different perspective. By the way, I tend to have lower glucose after BAB breakfast than before.

Kovita...had a similar experience this fall with my cardiologist...he is even an anti-aging guy.....promotes ketotic eating etc. etc. But when it came to the glucose tolerance test he was adamant that my CT, ketosis etc. would have no bearing on the outcome. I just refused to take it (did confirm with an ask Jack question too) on the grounds that I wasn't going to blast my body full of sugar after working so hard to get it out of my system (serious brain/addiction issues). I even brought articles to show him...oh well. We still work together in a kind of gingerly fashion.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-06T22:03:21+0200

Sean....have you read Dr. Kruse's material on the water/fat diet? Electrons not nutrients are what is operating here. You have to be in the right environment and have a good redox potential to take advantage of this. I know you are having a terrible time with your bloating, but asking what other people do can steer you in the wrong direction as much as it might actually help. Do you know what I mean?? I wish I could give you the answer, but I can't...sounds like working on your environment with the magnetico etc. is a good way to go for now.

@[SeaHorse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/562/) Yea I've read that post on 90% water diet, thats what got me thinking. I'm just trying to find out if other people can handle more fat and I just need to adapt or if I'm actually having too much and that's why I can't really handle it.
I think it's more of an adaption thing.
Yea my main plan is to save up for a stronger magnetico, and get more sun/artificial light electrons. The more electrons I can get in my body without going through my stomach the better.

### tellmisty — 2014-01-03T02:54:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ecourse-biohacking-101.9241/post-104631>

Hey everyone,

We've just launched a new FREE eCourse on the site called BioHacking 101. We've had a couple hundred of you sign up so far ... you can sign up using the opt-in on the blog, or go here: [http://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-101/](http://www.jackkruse.com/biohacking-101/)

With Dr. Kruse's help, I wrote this course for us to help navigate the world of biohacking ... this course is a work in progress, and I really would like us all to come together around the pathway that's emerging and share our ideas, success stories, and strategies for navigating the world of labwork and the path to optimal. Special thanks to Gretchen and Dr. Tim for their invaluable contributions to this course! I mean ... you all know it already, but those two are bah-rilliant and we are really lucky that they're part of the JK.com community.

I'll try to be better about stopping by and participating in this forum in particular ... I am really committed to growing the course and making it better for ALL of us! Please share your thoughts liberally ... and if you're really committed to becoming a good biohacker, I highly recommend setting up an Optimal Journal: [http://forum.jackkruse.com/forumdisplay.php?17-My-Optimal-Journal](http://forum.jackkruse.com/forumdisplay.php?17-My-Optimal-Journal). This community is FULL of brilliant members who've got lots of experience on this journey; we all lift the boats together!

Love you guys! Keep your eyes peeled for the 2014 Optimal Reset ... OMG, it's going to be BANANAS!!! We're gonna get optimal together!

xo
Misty

Thank you, I've found it. I'm beginning to think that after 60 years of less than optimal living and diet and reading just how much can go wrong that I'm never likely to be able to turn back the clock and put absolutely everything right. I'll settle for carrying less weight (if I can ever achieve that) and hope that by following a Paleo lifestyle as much as I can that anything else out of whack sorts itself along the way.

### caroline — 2014-01-08T23:42:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-ecourse-biohacking-101.9241/post-105110>

There is always high value in reading and following along - even if you can't do the tests for now. You never know when something will click for you.

This is a journey to optimal for me .... and I wouldn't dream of settling ... but then - I love everything about it. I love all the things I am learning and I love all the interaction with everyone here. I just love trying to figure all this out.

I would never have dreamt that I would love reading books about Einstein .... and reading Dr. Becker and Dr. Merino and Richard Feynman etc.

Every time I go to the pool at the ocean .... I always wind up talking about epi-paleo and C/T .... and I have been wondering - why does that keep happening? I have finally come to realise that people always eventually ask me what I am doing and have lots of questions and then we get into great discussions.

This journey is so individual .... we are all so different. At the end of the day - We all definitely need to do what is right for us.

# Thread: Chronic Eczema hacking for years & no cure! EMF or Methylation?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 22
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LaurenR — 2014-01-04T14:58:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104719>

Long post...
I have been hacking my chronic eczema all over for 3 years, tried many things...

Diagnosed as infant with eczema, asthma etc. Flare ups age 17yrs through to mid twenties, also IBS, allergies, chornic sinusitis (all fixed now).

Aug 2010- Chronic eczema on inside of forearms- dermatologist UV B treatment, helps a little.

Sept 2011- Still eczema, saw naturopath- started herbal meds & herbal cleanse, followed by probiotics (standard weed, seed stuff) .
Also stopped asthma meds and pill at same time = MASSIVE flare up! Face, neck, arms, upper body- lasts for months!
CDSA-4 stool- says no parasites, extreme dysbiosis, little to no good bacteria in gut.

Mar 2012- Series of 4 colonics & stop anti-depressant meds results in complete body flare up, EXTREME odema- legs,ankles, Extreme flaking of skin all over, in car, in bed, all day and night.

Next 3 months- extreme itching and burning sensation- like having acid poured on me, also little to no sleep at night- fall to sleep usually around 3am-5:30am, impacted life, ability to work etc.
Jul 2012- start a cortisone cream so I can sleep, function, work etc, helps greatly, continue use of cream.

Jul 2012- Jan 2014- continued chronic eczema, rash. Better, but not healed, some flare ups. Eczema forearm/elbow, behind knees-weeps, bleeds.
But also a general all over rash on back, outside of arms.

I have been Paleo (a little dairy) for 3 years, Dairy-free for 2 years, STRICT epi-paleo for >1 year.
My diet is tight: seafood/fish daily, grass fed/ free range red meat, some pork belly, coconut oil, coconut milk, limited veg (broc, cauli, brussels etc ), herbs, turmeric, ginger, garlic, onion, some 90% chocolate. I don't eat any of my IgG intolerance foods.

Starting again and simplifying: Currently redoing on a gut healing protocol- glutamine, aloe vera. Also bottled spring water as only water I drink now and reducing EMF where in my control (no sleep pad yet).
I still have "standard" eczema symptoms- sun makes it better. Showers, wool clothes make it worse.

Why am I posting in here? I AM GOING TO HEAL THIS IN 2014! But it's time to look DIG DEEPER!
I'm wondering about the impact of EMF (emf 7). I'm wondering about methylation.
It is more than a leaky gut!!!

I'm am wondering where to from here, as I begin an new year, try new labs etc.

I thank you for your time taken to read this. I appreciate your help to finally overcoming this issue.

### HoneyChild — 2014-01-04T16:55:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104721>

Maybe this thread might be of interest to you...

[http://forums.phoenixrising.me/inde...ogens-link-to-cfs-fibromyalgia-success.17874/](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?threads/xeno-estrogens-link-to-cfs-fibromyalgia-success.17874/)

Also, I'm sure I remember reading that LDN was a good treatment for all sorts of things including skin problems.

There was also a TV show where it was shown that soaking your skin in water with oats in it can help. They used a machine to analyse the skin to prove that it wasn't just a placebo effect, I think there was something like a 10% improvement as opposed to 0% with the placebo group. You simply place oats in an old pair of tights or muslin bag and let it sit in the bath.

### LaurenR — 2014-01-04T17:09:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104722>

Thanks, will take a look. I have done the oat bath thing, didn't help.

I should add I have not had any change in body composition with paleo or epi-paleo. I weigh around 52kgs / 114lbs (i don't weigh myself anymore).
I have 2-3kgs or 5-6lbs of fat to shift. Doesn't happen.
Only in last month or two seeing a slight change in body composition (rib cage, waist) with very very heavy lifting.

### LaurenR — 2014-01-04T17:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104725>

So from that link: gene coding olymorphins impacting estrogen metabolism.
Supplements of I3C, DIM, calcium-D-glucarate

Is the current 23andme test for ancestry reports and raw genetic data useful?
Or do I need the health related tests?

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104730>

### GoldFishie — 2014-01-05T16:40:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-eczema-hacking-for-years-no-cure-emf-or-methylation.9249/post-104801>

Lauren honey, I am SO sorry for the misery you are going through! I had horrible eczema up until the age of 35 and was on steroids for 10 years as a child until they pulled me off of them at the age of 17. Then I went from the proverbial frying pan into the fire.

I certainly don't have 'the' answer for you but I will tell you this - get off of all pork period. Oh if I would have known this as a child growing up on a farm! I am clear now, and seldom eat out, but one time I ate some chicken that had been cooked on the same grill they cooked pork on and I broke out for 6 weeks. This piece should be huge for you.

Secondly, make sure that you get a good balance on your essential fatty acids. You need more than the average person. I know that you are in Oz and don't know if you have access to Genova labs but they have a blood spot test for this.

Lastly, the sun makes a tremendous difference for my skin. I live in the southern US and am outside butt naked as much as possible and use a tanning bed during the winter.

Hope this helps~

### Sammy — 2014-01-25T03:23:57+0200

I can actually attest to this. When my eczema was really bad (when I was younger) I visited one doctor who told me to take like 6 capsules of evening primrose oil a day. It really did help to improve my skin then. I have no idea how or why it works though. But I did read somewhere that dry flaky skin was due to an Omega 6 deficiency. I don't understand the pathophysiology of eczema......so there is something wrong with the Vit A cycle in the skin.....affecting the immune response...but what does that have to do with Omega 6? Maybe the connection has something to do with Omega 6 -> inflammatory prostaglandins?

I would really like to know more about eczema......I know so many people who have it and although I can tell them what I did to get rid of it.....what worked for me might not necessarily work for them. But I think people with eczema should really focus on light cycles more than anything(including diet). Adhering to light cycles would be a high yield area..... Lots of sunbathing, the more skin exposed the better, during the day and strict avoidance of light(especially blue light at night) would benefit your skin more than any diet could.

### QuantumRadical — 2014-01-05T01:18:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104747>

The "tourists" stuck in the ice in Antarctica are actually a scientific expedition in Antarctica to study global warming. **_How ironic._** Yet, the media continues to ignore this part of the story and continues to describe the lot as just tourists. Now, with at least two icebreakers stuck in the ice, and the coldest air mass in twenty years descending from the Arctic into mid-America, you've got to wonder about computer models and theories. I put my money on a coming ice-age rather than a balmy beach vacation at the poles.

### sooperb — 2014-01-05T14:53:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/global-warming.9252/post-104795>

I don't buy into global warming either, there's no real evidence. Anyone remember the hole in the ozone layer? Hasn't it gone quiet about that now. How they could determine that it was manmade I don't know, they didn't know how long it's been there, what caused it, if anything, whether it's benign or dangerous, if it's bigger or not, all speculation with very little to back it up.

There's always been weather, weather changes, it has cycles, it moves stuff about, but all in all the world goes on despite it or even because of it. Here in the UK we were losing a lot of trees and in Scandinavia too, it was acid rain created by too much industrial pollution, oh my god we were killing the planet. It turned out to be a virus that was killing them. QED.

### prAna303 — 2014-01-06T23:22:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-its-cold-outside.9255/post-104921>

Still can not stop wonder if we have done a little to much to control our world.

We have created citys, highways and filled our world with tar and cement. The way of thinking, that logistics is our main goal, perhaps is getting a kick in the butt. Every time nature makes a move, these modern things get hurt and us together with them. # Thread: Want to help your kids........teach them about harmonics in everything.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 52
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:45:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104905>

I am not going to elaborate but lets just say something clicked at the Louvre.............

Most people associate harmonics with music........

I dont.

It is about life. All of it.

After I saw DaVinci's St. John the Baptiste live for my first time it struck me.

Listen to the key four notes in Pink Floyd's Part of Shine on your crazy diamond. 4 notes struck together........just do something you did not expect.

This was the last of DaVinci's works I had seen for myself and it completed my bucket list I wrote when I was 15.

On the flight home I wrote a new list................It's a list of melodies of harmonics. They are everywhere and we just do not see them. I saw them in ceilings. buildings, walls, and stone sculpture. I now see it in my own field. Blind once, but now I have new vision where the darkness roamed before.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:47:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104906>

same four notes in PF song above.........pattern the human heart beat. I did the acoustics check today.........amazing. Different octaves but spot on. Never saw it until today.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-06T22:48:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-104908>

### Chrysanthemama — 2014-02-17T01:50:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-110683>

Pink Floyd has always been one of my most loved bands. I came to love and appreciate them while exploring facets of reality as a young teen. As I type this, I have the song playing while my daughter uses her ear to find the notes on the piano. When I first learned to play music, my band instructor didn't take the time to ensure that each of us was learning how to read music properly. I learned to play by ear for my first year of music, and created my own way of reading music that was definitely not the correct way! I learned that the hard way when we moved to Vegas my 7th grade year and I had to audition for chair position. Out of 24 flautists, I was dead last because I could not read the instructor's hand-written music since I'd learned to read in such an ass-backwards fashion. Eventually, I passed each one of those flute players and advanced to the higher band, once I learned to read properly. But playing by ear has always been the way I play music best…along with improv. Harmonics have always been one of my favorite things to play around with. I can see how the visual harmonics along with the musical harmonics create a perfect symphony. Love this!

Classical music in general is suppose to be on point with heart beats, hence why its so powerful for healing. People love rocking in rocking chairs, espically when feeding babies, it flows with our heart beat.

Study: Group put 3 plants in different rooms...One listened to classical music...other one was rock music...other one was no music.

Rock music, plant died.
No music...plant grew normal
Classical...plant doubled their growth rate..

)))

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T03:25:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-171045>

**From FB tying music to magnetism:** "A teaching lesson about numbers tied to magnetism: Numbers explain physics better via colors to scientists, at least that is their idea now. Colors in all things are all about frequency of light they reflect and absorb. Colors for me, and hopefully for you, is not about what we see, it is about what they are fundamentally. they are unique packets of vibrational energy in the form of waves/particles. Consider red light: has a frequency of 436,803,079,680,000 oscillations per second. That equates to the same energy in a high C note in music. That note has an oscillation frequency of 4,096 oscillations. per second. Xrays, microwaves, cosmic and gamma rays all have these same type of numbers and colors tied to them but when you read these numbers they make no sense to you. However, the color has a qualia you do understand fully. You know what red and purple means to you. You know what frequency/color/numbers means to me? Magnetism, one part of the electromagnetic wave of light, has the HIGHEST oscillatory rate we know of in nature. Few people know this fact. It is 18,446,744,073,709,551,616 oscillations per second.........It is hard for the mind to grasp that many things can be simultaneouslyvibrating that many times in a second, but guess what.......last week on the cruise while everyone else did there own thing in Playa Del Carmen I made sure I took a person who needed to feel that vibration to a cent. I went into the belly of the crater where those oscillations have existed and been echoing in rain water for 65 million yrs.........Patrick asked me how I expended all the energy I did every day and night on the cruise.......and not get tired. I remained ketotic and at once and day and drank a ton of water........why? Because that water assimilated all those oscillations from the crater to renew me.........I knew I could give a lot because I was going to get a lot more in my time in the crater. For those of you bad at math: That number above is 18 quintillion, 446 quadrillion, 744 trillion, 73 billion, 709 million, 551 thousand, 6 hundred, and 16 oscillations per second of vibrational energy. That my dear is physics 101 of water. Remember water is a magnetic dipole that reacts to those magnetic moments in life. Get some.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-23T15:51:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-210938>

Music and light both share waves forms...........The video begins with the Sarasota school experiment that GE tried to bury in the 1970's. But the most important part of the video is about our noctural friends, the mice. Do you think any nutrition researcher controls for the lights in their labs? Nope. Do you think the results of their experiemts would varying if they were done in darkness and sunlight only? I do. Why do I think this? Mice are nocturnal and not diurnal like humans. Can mice still teach humans something about sunlight? What kind of mouse would you like to be?

### drezy — 2017-03-27T19:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-211230>

Don't forget that kids need to be educated in "bad" harmonics as well.

Jack's warning of nnEMF extends all the way to anything digital. Digital signals, on and off square waves, don't really exists outside of theory.

I spent a decade and a half working with high speed signals for computer ports and cell tower chips. It was a favorite pastime to pick on fresh engineers and ask them to explain why their "square" wave was broken on the scope when it wasn't broken but reality just doesn't match textbook theory.
{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Jack ain't shitting about nnEMF. What this shows is what actual "digital" signals always looks like. Your 1 Ghz port/chip isn't just spewing out 1GHz signals. It's got harmonics at 3x,5x,7x,9x,... frequencies.
For math heads:

For music heads:

When you just want C2 also force yourself to play G3 (1/3 the volume) E4-ish(1/5 volume) A#4-ish(1/7 volume) D5ish (1/9 volume)... Yuck right?

Bottom line for me:
Think you and your little darlings are just exposed to 2.4Ghz with wifi? Nope, with harmonics it's 2.4G, 7.2G, 12G.... It's like expecting silk when you reach out but instead putting your hand into 25 year old shag carpet from the back of a weirdo's van. Total hand sanitizer moment.

Consequently, one big challenge in producing high speed digital semiconductors is getting(designing in) enough electron flow and getting enough grounding... Gee, Sound familiar ? It sure does to me. I've tracked down this electron & ground issue on processor memory interfaces and high speed graphics ports.

The power grid also has to account for digital devices
[http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/5173191/](http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/5173191/)

### Jack Kruse — 2017-03-27T21:02:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-211236>

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse?published=t)

### drezy — 2017-03-27T22:26:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-211243>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=211236)
>
> [https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse?published=t](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/flicker-effect-light-jack-kruse?published=t)

@[Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)
I'm glad you're out there pushing. I'm only able to influence my own family's journey at this point and have noticed benefits across the boards. Momma, Poppa, and baby bear now all CT!

FWIW I spent 7+ years working under "flickering" light and 6 feet from a large pice of equipment that could supply high end network semiconductors with large amounts of "instantaneous" power. There sure were harmonics and numerous other effects there.

About 10 years later, after having left, I attended a party with the old crew and was told "You haven't aged at all in 10 years, what are you doing?" I didn't have all the pieces then but I'm filling in the picture more now thanks to this site.

**All** basic electronics relies on storing(or inadvertently leaking) energy into capacitance(chemical storage) and inductance (magnetic field storage or leakage \*read nnEMF\*). Fiber optics can be raised as an exception, but the optic line always terminates at an electrical transceiver so same issues.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-01T16:04:04+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2017-04-01T16:09:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/want-to-help-your-kids-teach-them-about-harmonics-in-everything.9268/post-211531>

Drezy you said, "The optical line may always end in an electric receiver".........in the tech world but if you look at the human biologic world the DC electric current generated by NLO chips (non linear optical= NLO) in us are rather unique and very different than what technology uses to build things. I have a sense that is the key we're misunderstanding. I actualy think the Quantlet is going to help me figure this out. This is why I think tinitus is instructive to clinicians.......it tells us something else is in action and we are missing something very fundamental. I think one thing we might be missing is that all optical systems must end in acoustic DC electric sensors. I think phonons and solitons have an inherit advantage in a DC system from a physics perspective.

> Don't forget that kids need to be educated in "bad" harmonics as well.
>
> Jack's warning of nnEMF extends all the way to anything digital. Digital signals, on and off square waves, don't really exists outside of theory.
>
> I spent a decade and a half working with high speed signals for computer ports and cell tower chips. It was a favorite pastime to pick on fresh engineers and ask them to explain why their "square" wave was broken on the scope when it wasn't broken but reality just doesn't match textbook theory.
> [View attachment 4584](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/4584/)
>
> Jack ain't shitting about nnEMF. What this shows is what actual "digital" signals always looks like. Your 1 Ghz port/chip isn't just spewing out 1GHz signals. It's got harmonics at 3x,5x,7x,9x,... frequencies.
> For math heads:
>
> [View attachment 4585](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/4585/)
>
> For music heads:
>
> When you just want C2 also force yourself to play G3 (1/3 the volume) E4-ish(1/5 volume) A#4-ish(1/7 volume) D5ish (1/9 volume)... Yuck right?
>
> Bottom line for me:
> Think you and your little darlings are just exposed to 2.4Ghz with wifi? Nope, with harmonics it's 2.4G, 7.2G, 12G.... It's like expecting silk when you reach out but instead putting your hand into 25 year old shag carpet from the back of a weirdo's van. Total hand sanitizer moment.
>
> Consequently, one big challenge in producing high speed digital semiconductors is getting(designing in) enough electron flow and getting enough grounding... Gee, Sound familiar ? It sure does to me. I've tracked down this electron & ground issue on processor memory interfaces and high speed graphics ports.
>
> The power grid also has to account for digital devices
> [http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/5173191/](http://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/5173191/)

The wave form in the picture above is the key.......that picture is generated by a semiconductor that does not use true optical signaling. It is digital electronic and makes very unusally shaped wave forms. Those shapes mean that electron flow is inconsistent and lead to energy (electric and magnetic leak) leakage..........when energy is leached this effects the wave form and the optical power that is possible.

Biologic semiconduction is all about photoelectric semiconduction. The sun has to exicte those electrons and because the sun's light is so chaotic (see the Fraunhoffer Lines) electrons excitation is the first way to make the optical information in them less chaotic. The most important part of the Rosetta stone cipher of life is found in the DNA code. Why is it that DNA only codes for proteins and very little else?

My belief is simple........the DNA code is all about our semiconductor fab plant. Our nucleic acids specialize in in making non linear optical chips to make more sense of light using non linear optics.

[In 1961, Franken](https://scholar.google.com/scholar_lookup?title=Generation%20of%20optical%20harmonics&author=Franken,+P.A.&author=Weinreich,+G.&author=Peters,+C.W.&author=Hill,+A.E.&publication_year=1961&journal=Phys.+Rev.+Lett.&volume=7&pages=118&doi=10.1103/PhysRevLett.7.118) and his co-workers observed the phenomenon of second-harmonic generation (SHG) in quartz. This was seminal to understanding how optics differs from electronics. Quartz also has a very special ability: It allows passage of UV light. And it turns that UV light into very special wave forms to make sense of the information in light. Quartz is a crystal that participates in non linear optics because of its own crystalline lattice. I think biologic proteins all have NLO factors that make them highly conserved by evolution in DNA by magnetic photolithography. Second-harmonic generation is but one aspect of non linear optics and I did a webinar several months ago. Our brain is made out of liquid crystaline NLO materials that are all carbon based and hydrated that also participate in non linear optical changes. Today nonlinear optics has attracted much attention in electric engineering and has developed into one of the key branches of modern optics.

Without exception, nonlinear optical (NLO) crystals, have become the most important optoelectronic functional material types in semiconductor designs that human engineers know about. Those same himans have no idea how to really use these characteristics. If you observe thigns life cand do as I do you see it clearly. In electronics, NLO's are now widely used in laser wavelength-converting technology.

Our brain has been doing this to wake life up now for 600 million years. **Non linear optical crystals allow for a physicochemical phenomenon when they interact with light.** These phenomena manifests often by leeching onto the non linear crystal materials as the carrier. This is why DNA codes for them. They decipher the signals in sunlight further photo-electrically. This means light programs the NLO to do things we can hardly believe............ In turn, evolution has usually found a practical application of NLO's via evolutionary history. Life, cells, and tissues have continued to benefiting from the attached property that the sun has given our biologic NLO's. This is critical to a quantum evolution. That attached property in biology is pretty varied..........across tissues. This is how animals evolved wakefulness from the unconscious sleep state. Once awake, more energy was made from our hydrogen fuel cells called mitochondria, and that phontonic light energy was used with more evolved NLO crystals cells innovated to provide us the ability to create how sensations acquire characteristics, such as colors, pitch, and tastes.

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: Light vs. Temperature
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 25
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### TheKid — 2014-01-06T23:52:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104930>

We talk a lot here about the importance of light mismatches. But I am wondering about temperature mismatches as well.

We all know the role of cold. But could it be that in order to really benefit from it in a true mismatch-avoiding way, one should move to where it is cold rather than recreate cold?

The reason I ask is that right about now, there are places in and around the 37th parallel that are balmy and producing tons of citrus. In fact, in California, winter is the season for citrus. And if you were a premodern human who stumbled upon a citrus tree, you'd eat your fill.

And we know that the earth has been much warmer in past ages, so much so that vineyards thrived in England at one time. And had you lived back then, I'm assuming it would not have been a mismatch to eat grapes although it is now. And it's also seems the only reason it is a mismatch to eat grapes in England now is that we have a changed climate not changed seasonal light periods.

I live near Turkey and right now Turkey is spitting out mandarins and all sorts of citrus. Spain has seaaonal pomegranates. I'm seeing seasonal kiwis as well. Lots of vitamin C for the taking.

So I am wondering if we need to be more mindful about what temperature is signaling to us while still minding light cycles. And if you live in a warmer, northern, non-tropical place where climate would allow you to eat fruit during shorter light periods, should you?

### prAna303 — 2014-01-07T00:00:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104931>

> And if you live in a warmer, northern, non-tropical place where climate would allow you to eat fruit during shorter light periods, should you?

I live in Sweden... From my point of view, nothing would grow even if the winters would be warm... The sun do not provide enough power to make that kind of life explode during the period from october to june. We have a short span here with plants that gives us fruits, three months i think and after that they get rotten.

So i would say no, and because how the world look like today, NO!

Just my way of thinking.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-07T00:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104932>

Great question Jason... Love it. I think the answer is going to depend on your particular health issues. If you're healthy no AI issues etc, and you had access to seasonal fruits that grow in your region - why not?
but I think for me the answer is no, at least given my current situation. That doesn't mean once I've fixed myself the answer won't become yes.

I associate ruby red grapefruits with Jan/Feb. and when I was sick this weekend I asked DH to get pick one up for me, b/c it was about the only thing other than gelatin, and sweet potatoes that I could eat. so I ate it. I really want another now. I've been very good at eating only fruit in season... I will say that when "pomegranates" were in season at the store I ate the greadliy - b/c to me they are a winter fruit - we'd get them all the time when I lived in CA. probably not ideal....

### TheKid — 2014-01-07T00:32:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104934>

I think it's important to think in both directions. We tend to give a lot of consideration to the consequences of eating XYZ out of season.

But maybe we should be also as careful about the consequences of NOT eating PDQ when IN season.

And we should remember that the ability for flora to thrive is more a function of temperature than light. Just look at all the micro climates created by the Gulf Stream from France to even north of England

### nicld — 2014-01-07T04:34:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-104943>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=104931)
>

Since we no longer migrate for food, I am still trying to figure out this whole seasonal eating and how far my range is. I have had wild frozen local berries in winter too and I did not felt any bad effects.. but they are very concentrated and very little carbs. Almost all fruits we can buy are heavily cultivated for sweetness and to be bigger... and are having way more fructose than the wild fruits. I am a fan of wild food. They make me feel the best. They make me THRIVE

So for me, even cultivated fruits when in season are practically out. My own N=1

I do not even eat much cultivated veggies. I prefer wild greens and herbs. They have a different effect on me. They too makes me thrive

So really this question is more me just looking out at the world and wondering about why we see some of the things we do. And having lived in California, I remember what came in season there and when; and it got me thinking about the various aspects of our human biology that are still not well understood by the masses.

What I was hoping Jack, or someone else, might clarify in a little more depth, is the relative importance of temperature (all temps, not just cold), in signalling to humans vs. light; and how they work together or, possibly work against one another. Because as I noted, we have the Medieval Warm Period, when grapes apparently flourished in Britain. So what do we make of the changes in climate that come and go versus the changes in light periods, which remain consistent?

We have talked before about how the light period leads to literal energetic differences in fruits/veggies that grow in certain seasons. And Jack has used the example of eating a banana in Canada being a complete energetic mismatch. I assume using the same reasoning, an orange in Louisville, KY would be a mismatch as well, because it's now winter. But hold on a second. Louisville is essentially at the same latitude as Palo Alto, CA, where, right now, oranges are fully in season.

Or you might think about New Orleans. I remember being there and seeing banana trees. And apparently, bananas can be in season into like December. I wouldn't eat on in Ohio. But do you eat one if you live in New Orleans and are otherwise healthy?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T22:08:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-vs-temperature.9271/post-105008>

Life is all about charge separation. In photosynthesis we use sunlight to separated charges. In animals we use hydrophillic semiconductors to do the same thing. What is the fluid of charge separation? Water. Water next to a hydrophilic semiconductor acts like a N type semiconductor. This makes the water adjacent next to the semiconductor negative. The further you go away from it the charge is positive. What helps energize this simple battery? More sunlight.........its energizes can alter that battery function positively or negatively. In the zone next to the semiconductor Becker found something called the DC current. This is a negatively charged current. He had no idea where it came from. Pauling, Ling, and Szent Georgyi did. It came from the separation of water into hydrogen oxygen and electrons. In the exclusion zone of water physicists like Mae Wan Ho have showed electrons carry the charge. It also turns out these electrons reside in oxygen molecules in the water. They are usually distributed throughout the water lattice adjacent to the hydrophilic semiconductor. The number of oxygen atoms dictates the DC current. The larger oxygen content the larger the electron density. This is why cold water water carries more Oxygen. It is more electron dense because cold water has more dissolved o2 in it........This is at the core of why CT is powerful. It allows for more electrons to be dissolved in the major fluid of your cellular matrix to deliver more energy by liberation of these electrons when charge separation happens....................This is where temperature sculpts. Physics.......and physical chemistry showing you why chemistry and biology left quite a few lessons out of their bags of tricks. Water chemistry is the biggy they forgot.

axons are gels who are extremely hydrophilic. # Thread: How do you protect yourself from all of this?!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tanya — 2014-01-07T16:42:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/post-104985>

2014 consumer electronics show is here now with the emphasis on â€œinternet of things - any object that is connected or run via the Internet.

I must admit that I used to look so much forward to seeing all these new gadgets and some of them even made into my wish list, but this time I have a quite different view of that thanks to this blog.... You can read the whole article, but this is what sent chills down my spine:

By 2050, analysts project, there will be 50 billion Internet-connected devices, or five gadgets for every man, woman and child.
"It transforms things in pretty significant ways," said Scott McGregor, president and chief executive of Broadcom, an Irvine company that hopes its semiconductors will power this trend. "You're going to see an explosion of devices."

Thanks to smaller, cheaper sensors, speedier wireless connections and the explosion of smartphones and tablets, it's becoming easier and more cost-effective to link just about any object to the Internet.
Gadget by gadget, people are coming to expect that even the most common things will be more useful when they are connected. Pets. Livestock. Sports equipment. Watches. Power meters. Light bulbs. Washing machines. Thermostats. If you can think of it, someone has probably stuck a sensor on it and connected it to the Internet.
"I think a lot of this is going to start with the smart home," said Kelly Davis-Felner, a representative of the Wi-Fi Alliance, whose members have played a key role in enabling this connected world. "The smart home is going to be built brick by brick until 10 years from now we're going to be looking back and saying, 'Wow, I can't believe there used to be a time when everything wasn't connected.'"

[http://www.latimes.com/business/la-fi-ces-internet-things-20140105,0,3796601.story#ixzz2pittZDwW](http://www.latimes.com/business/la-fi-ces-internet-things-20140105,0,3796601.story#ixzz2pittZDwW)

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T18:37:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-do-you-protect-yourself-from-all-of-this.9276/post-104991>

# Thread: Carbs and Vitamin D - no problem if there is no inflamation and according to seasons?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/>

### marcus — 2014-01-07T21:38:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/post-105006>

I am trying to get the picture regarding vitamin D. After reading several blog posts here (science behind leptin reset, hormones 101, hormones 102, Vitamin D: The Sunshine of Your Life, Quantum Biology 7: Vitamin D) vitamin D levels in humans seem to be influenced by several factors:

If there is inflammation present, vitamin d levels fall because of 'prenenolone steal syndrome'.

Another issue is VDR polymorphisms. It's an issue especially of people suffering from autoimmune diseases.

In QuantumBiology blog post series Vitamin D is the connection between ('binds') the brain and the skin.

So far so good.

But how is this quote meant: "The circadian rhythm of vitamin D levels is also accounted for to judge light levels as well. Those closer to the equator would have much higher levels of Vitamin D and would be able to account for a higher carb level they face. Many current day leptin resistant folks find out their vitamin D levels are low when they finally test for it."? ('science behind leptin reset' blog post).

How are vitamin D and carbs in humans yoked metabolically? Why do I need high levels of vit D to stay healthy while eating carbs?

It can be deduced that eating no carbs in winter (when sun levels are low) is no problem to have a low level of vitamin D?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-07T21:58:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/carbs-and-vitamin-d-no-problem-if-there-is-no-inflamation-and-according-to-seasons.9279/post-105007>

How are Vitamin D and carbs yoked? Via the photoelectric effect. Vitamin D is made below the power of natural light of 520 nm. 200-380 nm range. Carbs grow in longer light cycles where photons and electrons always carry higher energies from the sun. They are naturally absent from places on this planet when these metrics are absent. Vitamin D is also provided for at the same time because UV light has a lower wavelength than the visible spectrum therefore UV light will always be present when the 520 nm light is p\[resent to grow carbs. Vitamin D is made with this light source in skin. This is how two parts of the spectrum are used by two different sets of Maxwell demons to catch photons and electrons of different powers. The difference in energies creates an "opportunity for the brain's receptors". If you carefully read CT 6 what did I say about Vitamin D in winter time? We have a metabolic trap door.......we dont need it if we can generate NOS. Normally we can when our redox potential is good........but when we cant..........shit hits the fan.

    Messages:
    [655](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?search/member&user_id=2835)
    Likes:
    267
    [↑](http://forums.phoenixrising.me/index.php?goto/post&id=347920#post-347920)

    Dbkita,
    thank you for airing these suspicions and discussing the validity og SNP interpretation by Yasko. Very interesting.

    I've been around for half a century. I have no background from informatics or genetics (linguistics and anthropology) as the informatics came too late for me, but I'm a systems thinker by nature. I've suffered series of collapses throughout life and recently rehabilitated myself from dysautonomia. I came into this forum backwards; only after finding help through supplements that worked, did I start to question the genetic underpinnings of DNA methylation.

    Mine is a long and complex story. I am self diagnosed and self treated for the past 16 years. I came to this forum through recently discovering Richard van Konynenburg's methylation hypothesis on Dr. Sarah Myhill's website. I only learned of Amy Yasko through this forum, and of Genetic Genie through a rare haplotype group I share with 18 people that may or may not cause lowered tetrahydrobiopterin. It's been a very steep learning curve!

    While I in general consider Amy Yasko a credible pioneer, trying to help primarily children with autism and their parents, I wonder if she has taken too much upon herself, and therefore simplified the whole approach, contrary to her proclaimed intentions of individualised interpretation. She does state that the genetic profile is just one of the many factors in multifactorial disease. However, her methylation and detox profile seem to contradict that, as one predictive value seems to be given to all SNPs. I don't know if that's her fault, or if the people using her data misinterpret.

    I read your posts here on the topic of VDR Taq with great interest, as I share your confusion and suspicion (without intending to diminish her work). Based on what little I have grasped about polymorphism variation, I find it simplicistic to assign a +/- value to variation that may be differently distributed in different populations, gender, age or in correlation with other SNPs. Both the COMT 158 Val/ Met and the VDR Taq seem to be population specific, according to scientific studies. However, even the scientific studies on genetic polymorphisms are not a measure of anything. The work on the COMT 158 Val/Met polymorphism reads like a horoscope (you're either 'a warrior' or 'a worrier'), but that SNP itself is significant. I prefer to focus on the biochemistry of its function, rather than on the personality traits.

    I cringe when I read "being positive for a mutation". Genotyping is not like testing positive for a viral or a bacterial infection, but in most peoples' minds it may sound like that! Although the genetic variation technically is a mutation - a mutation that many people carry - it is not a mutation in the sense of the gene defect. From the established genetics point of view, ie current knowledge about the inborn errors of metabolism, only the proven defects are caused by gene _mutation_. What a single polynucleotide variation does in any individual, is to be discovered, not simply charted.

    The SNP variation does not cause anything directly, but indirectly, by affecting a synthesis of an important vitamin or coenzyme, or by inability to metabolise common drugs and environmental toxins. Here I'm in agreement with Yasko.

    The only thing I can think of, in her defence, is that autism may be a differently organised system altogether. I am on the opposite side of that spectrum (gifted and not a trace of developmental problems). I too am a fast oxidiser and despite having genotypes that enable me to deal with stress more successfully than most people, my HPA axis broke down. I've experienced first hand dysautonomia (a total neuro-endocrine-immune derangement) so I know how a deranged metabolism is not like a normal metabolism. That's why I wonder if maybe Yasko's interpretation of methylation in autists is based on her clinical experience that won't be matched by the usual genetic studies.

    Just thinking out loud. If you'd care to comment, I'd appreciate it very much.

    If you do reply, could you also point me to the posts you wrote about immune dysfunction, as I find that the most difficult area of all I had to deal with. Thanks in advance.

    Sonja

    I would agree with you except in the case of VDR, Dr Yasko was labeling the baT haplotype as "defective". While in her practice autistic children with baT may be able to tolerate more methyl donors the idea that the baT haplotype in the general population is defective and means less dopamine is plain wrong. It is the dominant haplotype. Especially in certain ethnic groups.

    Sorry but I have made so many posts. Try the search feature with "dbkita" and "immunne". Adreno has been on a lot of those threads as well. Good luck!​

### NeilBB — 2018-03-01T21:17:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-podcast-out-with-dave-asprey.9281/post-236090>

One of the major advantages of an establishment with an "official dogma" is its ubiquity, united front, and strength in numbers. This is a powerful socializing force and gives propaganda its juice.

Conversely, the reason most anti-establishment movements ultimately fail historically is because the various opponents of the establishment rarely agree with each other and schisms invariably multiply over details, spoils, and mechanics of implementation. It's always been true across all disciplines.

I personally think that opponents of the establishment should band together over the "bigger picture outlook" to defeat the mutual common enemy and wait to battle out the details later, once the establishment is fully discredited. As the US, Europe and Russia came together in WW2 to defeat the Nazi's and Facsist's. But that's just my view and I know it's a pipedream.

# Thread: Lauren's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LaurenR — 2014-01-08T14:18:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-105063>

I thought I might join in the fun and start a journal!

Righto so here is a brief history, of diagnosis, medications, progress:
Age 2- Asthma - put on steroid inhalers serotide and ventolin- took age 2 to 29
Age 5- Eczema - begin steroid creams on & off
Age 12- Digestive issues begin, cramping, diahorrea
Age 13- Elimination diets for digestive
Age 15- Severe allergy and hayfever symptoms
Age 16- Chronic sinusitis begins
Age 16- De-sensitization Injections for garden mold, following skin prick tests
Age 17- Acne and increased hair growth- put on the Pill
Age 18- Sinusitis continues
Age 19- Surgery on sinuses FESS & Septoplasty
Age 20- Blood tests and investigation into fatigue-nothing found
Age 20- Colonoscopy & Endoscopy for chronic cramping & diahorrea- IBS diagnosis
Age 20- Anxiety & Panic attacks developed following car crash
Age 21- Acne put on antibiotics for 3 months at a time...
IBS continues to plague daily life
Eczema continues on and off in patches
Age 24- Mild depression
IBS continues
Eczema continues on and off in patches
Age 27- 10months backpacking Sth America alone.
Both amazing and traumatic experiences.

Aug 2010- Return from overseas and diagnosed with Depression, PTSD ( trauma in Sth America) & exhaustion. Most likely adrenal fatigue too.
Eczema returns (from constant summer to winter )
Begin grain free, dairy free- and finally digestive relief and heal IBS and hayfever!

Sept 2011- 5 day skin allergy test
Oct 2011 - Say goodbye to GP's and visit Naturopath- poop test-high dysbiosis, low good bactieria;
Stop contraceptive pill & asthma inhalers.
Begin parasite cleanse, liver detox, mag, vit c, herbal meds, chlorella.
Experience major detox, vomiting a few days & rash extreme flare up face, neck, arms, chest.

Mar 2012- Stop anti-depressants (after 1 year) and do 4 colonics and a clay colon cleanse
Extreme eczema flare up, oedema, detox, anti-depressan detox?
Spend the next 3 months in burning, itching agony, don't sleep, painful to dress, move.
Bleed everywhere, ruin many sheets, weeping and shed more skin than humanly possible!!!
Try acupuncture.
Cycle regulates

Vit D= low, begin supp; B12= low
ASI Test:
7am- 22nmol/L (target 25)
12pm- 10 nmol/L (target 15)
6pm- 7 nmol/L (target 5-10)
10pm- 6 nmol/L (target<5)

DHEA-S= 7AM 9.8nmol/L (ref range 0.7-8.7)

Liver Function Test= fast p1, slow p2
Hair metals Test= multiple toxicities- i realise not useful.
IgG t=Test- cow, sheep, goat milk, wheat, egg white, kidney beans, soy, almonds, brazil nuts, peanut

June 2012- Start using a cream for rash, skin. Supps.
Sept 2012- Infrared saunas, start kinestiology (combo of muscle testing, EFT, craniosacral)
Dec 2012- Begin Leptin Rx, epi-paleo. Supps.
Jan 2013: Adrenal fatigue RX, Leaky gut RX. Supps.
March 2013: Begin CT (stop in May due to move). more supps...
July 2013: Move O.S

So where does that leave me...
Well I have just moved from Australia to London...that puts me out of the donut hole frying pan and into the EMF fire of London

Food Goals:
My diet is tight and seasonal, but food is not as good here.
I want to start eating seafood/oily fish twice a day not just once.
I also want to eat more types of shellfish. I accidently poisoned myself on mussels, so haven't been eating them. Not a huge fan of scallops or oysters, but will get back into them.
Also eating more raw protein is a goal too, when I feel comfortable on the source and process etc.

Body Composition Goals:
I don't weigh myself, haven't for a couple of years.
So I weigh around 52kgs = 114lbs and I am 165cm = 5'3".
I have not done a DEXA scan, so could be around 20% at a very rough guess.

I want to lose 3-4kgs (um what is that 5-7lbs) of body fat and a couple of %'s.
Mostly stored on my love handles, belly fat, my adductors (inner thigh) and back.
I am not overweight, but I am not lean or optimal in the midsection!
I also want to increase muscles mass and squat some booty into my butt

Optimal Goals:
Get optimal...obviously!  Not just health, but in mind, career, relationships, connection.
This is about an optimal life not just health!
Heal skin, leaky gut, adrenals, brain, clock

RX Goals:
Do my best with EMF in this place!
Get magnetico sleep pad
Tighten on bluelight and circadian cycle, I have blue blockers and recently got amber bulbs too and candles.
CT again! (I think I packed too many shoes not enough cold packs). I found the ice packs much much easier than a bath of cold water.

Retest usual and Test thyroid, sex hormones, ASI, 23andme

Activity Goals:
Increase muscle mass through lifting; increase PB's for deadlift, front and back squat, chin, pull ups; dance more; more fun!

Continue daily meditation, EFT, kinesiology.
