Lauren, welcome and wow, what a story you have to share!! I find it fascinating that you didn't notice any digestive issues till you were 12. I wonder if they were brewing all along, and if your mom's health didn't contribute. What was/is her health like?

Also, I wonder if you have read at all about mold/biotoxin issues? Reading your list it popped right out at me. I don't know where you live but is it possible you were ever exposed to a water damaged building? Or anywhere for more than 10 minutes where it smelled musty? I just wonder.

Here is the life-changing blog that I had to come across twice before I realized, mold/biotoxins are likely part of my story:

Congratulations on finding this place and doing so much to get optimal!!!!!

### LaurenR — 2014-01-19T13:59:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-106193>

I am about 10 days in with NO steroid cream for eczema, as I have run out.
This happens every 4-6 months. I run out of steroid cream and I test to see what happens etc....inevitably the same thing!!!

My arms-inside & out, neck, chest, breasts and parts of back and rib cage are COVERED in eczema. Red, hot, itchy, acid burn feeling and very very dry.
It's flaking a lot this time.
I haven't slept properly for days. And this is ultimately what happens each time I have done this.
I end up using the cortisone cream again so I can start functioning properly, quality of life etc.

This time it has really got to me. I have been using this cream since July 2012, (I went for 9 months prior without a prescription cream, bad idea!).
I have a feeling if I left it longer it would continue to creep down my torso, thighs, calves, ankles...

I am no better than I was before I started using it!!! I am a strong woman, but years of itchy, hot burning, sometimes takes it's toll on my mental & emotional wellbeing....

I need to fix brain, photoperiod reader, vit a cycle. I want to understand this completely.
The Rx: circadian cycle & sleep, along with the other kruse rx's.

The catch: I face and have faced for years now- the eczema, the heat, the itchiness impacts my ability to fall asleep.
EVEN when using a prescription cream, I still have trouble falling asleep. And here in lies a hurdle.
I meditate but that does not help with this, other things but not this.
Anyway that is where I'm at.

### sooperb — 2014-01-19T17:17:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-106197>

I think my daughter would sympathise Lauren. She has had asthma since she was 2.5 and at 41 is still a sufferer. She thrives physically when she takes her medication but it is steroid based and she finds her mood sinks and her temper frays alarmingly. She has tried to live without the meds many times but while she may get some months symptom free (usually in the summer) she always seems to relapse. At present she has been trying to do without the meds but is then needing to use a nebuliser in order to be breathe. It's no fun and she hates it. She is an allergic asthmatic so there can be many things that spark it off and of course she doesn't know until she hits an allergen by which time it's too late.

Have you ever bio-hacked yourself to see if there are any particular irritants that set your eczema off or is it something more internal that causes it? According to what I've read about paleo and via some of Dr. Kruse's posts, eczema and asthma are classed as neolithic diseases that one should be able to heal by living optimally. I'd love to believe this is true.

Sooperb, not to hijack, but this sounds like you were talking about me, the way I used to be. I also had some relief from asthma during summer. And I was allergic to a host of things, including some foods. All of it went away, including the various allergies (even to poison ivy) when I dropped the wheat. I think the wheat got my system all riled up so that the smallest irritant, like cedar pollen or nuts or a cat or whatever, would set off my asthma. I dropped wheat and after a while I would get only a very mild reaction to things that used to set me off. Later, I stopped having the mild reactions with the addition of CT. It blows my mind how one thing messed up my system so badly. No more ear infections, sinus infections, asthma.

### LaurenR — 2014-03-07T16:24:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-113997>

I pulled three little black things out of the same spot in my arm, which I took to the infectious disease clinic. I did a series of parasitology and microbiology tests which cam back negative. They said the things in my arm were not parasites, but I have had another in my ankle!!!

So update...I was then tested to for strongaloydis, which I have not heard back, which I think will mean I do NOT have it.
Wanted to be clear of this, as I have had this rash since returning from 10 months in South America.

I am also doing a parasite cleanse with tincture drops, in case there are parasites that were not detectable by their testing.
Have not really had any symptoms. Keep checking poop, but hard to tell if I am seeing parasites lol.
(I remember being told in my Invertebrates class at University that if you removed most matter from the world but left nematodes, that you would still be able to "see" most things or something along the lines. Gross! Never forgotten that!).

My new idea/thought...
I have definite patches of Eczema on my body, scaley, weeping, little sores.
BUT, this rash over my body, perhaps it is not eczema. It is red, itchy, rough, but I am not scaly and weepy all over.

I am thinking it could be a different kind of skin issue.

I am also wondering if it could be related to low thryoid. I do not have all the hypothryoid symptoms, but I do have a couple.
I plan to test my thyroid in next few weeks.
I wonder if any other thyroid people here have skin rashes?

### LaurenR — 2014-03-12T21:26:19+0200

I read someone having a big reaction following a detox. My full body rash started with a series of detox events.
First part was supps, parasite supps.
Second part following colonics and colon cleanse (clay)

The eczema was already there in spots. I really think there are 2 skin issues going on here.

When I tested my DHEA-S it was high- above range (that was a year ago).
In the past I have experienced extreme reactions to bites.

I am conscious of the cycle I am in when I go to bed. Even if I do night time meditation, and I am basically asleep. I take out headphones, remove phone from bedroom, come back to bed, my body will heat up within a few mins, get itchy, scratch, wriggle, get hotter, itch more, get hotter, get itchier, fluff the doona to let cold air in, scratch, get hotter etc.
Then I run into late night cortisol spike, mind starts going, lot's of creative ideas flow (frequently at night).
More hotness and itchy and awakeness. The room is always cold- no heating in the bedroom.

While I wake up fine in the monring, I am struggling to balance the nightime circadian rhythm/cortisol because of the rash, itchy, hotness.

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-13T15:21:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-115175>

Yikes....you might ask Josh on the forum for some insights...he's been dealing with immune/allergy issues in some very creative ways.
The histamine is going to be an ongoing issue if you have mastocytosis because you have more cells to produce more histamine than the average person..worth checking out.
Some supplements that can help all over allergy issues (you may know of them already) are PRP (NuMedica) and NeuroProtek which has luteolin, quercitin and rutin in it. You have probably already read lots about the different histamine receptors...I found that I had to take something that targeted more than one to get any relief. I too used steroid cream for years and years to deal with rashes that arrived every month before my period....no body ever bothered to check my hormones....I used to get contact hives from hot water , stress...on and on. You know how it is. Rashes and thyroid can be connected too. Remember skin health is a reflection of brain health so the function of the brain rather than filling in holes with hormones is the ultimate thing to focus on. Sounds like you are doing well with light cycles etc. It will take awhile.

I would get your thyroid tested a.s.a.p! magnetico and 23 and me (really worth it to know about methylation) I know these are on your list...just giving you a pep talk
Interesting about PTSD....do you know about Somatic Experiencing work? Another very useful tool to have in your toolbox.

### LaurenR — 2014-03-26T21:17:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-118111>

So update, I did a parasite cleanse a few weeks ago, for two weeks. Things have been moving a little better and a lot more 4's on the bristol chart than before and a lot more regularity in timing. So this is good. Thinking about doing another 2 weeks as that is the normal protocol for the tincture.

I had 3 nights last week where I was asleep quickly before 10:30pm. I go to bed usually between 9:30-10pm now. Although if a go early, I never fall asleep until my partner comes to bed.
Oxytocin release before sleep GREATLY reduces my itchiness and the hotness I feel in bed, thus making it easier to fall asleep.

I am back to face dunks and sunlight in the am and need to get some icepacks again.
I underestimated the re-accumulation of things such as icepacks etc when relocating o.s.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-05T18:22:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-129804>

For sure, I have looked like this for 2.5 years and still no closer to an answer, except maybe now...

I will definitely give that cream a go!

Although I do not have all these

[As per Dr Theoharides](http://www.mastcellmaster.com)

Mast cell activation syndrome is mainly diagnosed on the basis of symptoms: (From a recent presentation)

(1) Dermatologic: flushing, pruritus, urticaria pigmentosa, angioedema, dermatographism (sometimes)

(2) Respiratory: wheezing, sore throat

(3) Cardiovascular: chest pain, hypotension, tachycardia

(4) Gastrointestinal: abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, bloating, malabsorption, esophagitis

(5) Naso-ocular: nasal stuffiness, pruritus

(6) Neurologic: headache, memory and concentration difficulties/brain fog, paresthesia, peripheral neuropathy

(7) Muscoskeletal: bone/muscle pain, degenerative disc disease, osteoporosis/osteopenia

(8) Systemic: anaphylaxis, fatigue

So maybe it is histamine.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-05T18:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-129807>

For histamine the question is.. why mast cells release too much histamine?
Some searching found this:

\--An elevation in histamine would generally be associated with classic IgE allergies. I have only tested IgG.

\--mast cell disorders respond to a wide range of triggers, which may include environmental chemicals, foods, temperature changes, medicine, smells, stress, viruses and fungi.

\--Mast Cell Disorders are not the same as Multiple Chemical Sensitivity. But some people with MCS may be incorrectly diagnosed and actually have a mast cell disorder.
I have have 5 day skin chemical tests and came up positive to numerous chemicals. This was back in 2011!

\--The long list of symptoms associated with mast cell disorders can be found at the **The Mastocytosis Society**. Some people are called “shockers” because they go into anaphylactic shock when triggered, whereas others are called “leakers” because their mast cells slowly leak their contents causing chronic symptoms. The symptoms can range from mild to severe. There are specific medical treatments that help many people reduce the symptoms of mast cell disorders.

\--genetic polymorphisms that can cause an upregulation of specific **CBS genes**, which may impact mast cell degranulation
I have the SNP.

I feel like i am the closest to solving this in 3 years.

### LaurenR — 2014-06-13T11:42:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-130731>

I have had another new discovery. I was researching MCAD and all things mast cells and talking with sufferers in various online groups, when I was pointed in the direction of Topical Steroid Addiction/ Withdrawal.

My heart sank a little yesterday. I read the ITSAN site, [http://www.itsan.org/eczema\_questions\_answered.html](http://www.itsan.org/eczema_questions_answered.html)
The symptoms. This is me. This was me in 2012 9 months into withdrawal and then I started using steroid creams almost daily...to now.

I felt a bit sick reading it all. The pain, the burn, the sleepless nights, the feeling of dying from the inside out.

I feel really sad. And scared. Healing is slow, really really slow. And I am scared to be in that amount of physical and emotional pain again...and for so much longer.

They recommend no sun during the initial flare.

I would really love to find a method of making this easier on my body. Maybe the magnetico would help. I am not sure if this would help with the nerve and blood vessel healing, I'm not sure.

They mention use of antihistamine tablets and sleeping pills- both of which I don't want to use.

I am worried about trashing my cortisol again from the itch/burn and histamine release and ruining my sleep.

Tough day.

Here is a clip from one of the leading Dr's in this field.

I took a break from reading about Topical Steroid Withdrawal and Red Skin Syndrome.

It is summer now here and it is advised NOT to withdraw during summer, as you are also advised to keep sunlight to a minimum because the skin is so damaged during flares.

I have a few trips planned around Europe, so I'm thinking I will need to wait until they are done and withdraw in October. The thought of another few months on the creams makes me unhappy.

I think that my partner and I will have to talk about when will I do this. We might have to consider moving back home and even moving in to my parents depending on how I go. I'm not sure we have the support we need here in UK with just a few friends and couples and no family.
I have been reading the ITSAN forum and crying.
Alll I know is that I went 9 months withdrawal previously and I pray that makes it easier this time.

Must fix the circadian rhythm first!**\[/quote\]

Thanks Jude. I have worked for a while at fixing circadian rhythm and I eat fish or shellfish everyday sometimes twice a day.

A side effect of withdrawal and I also experienced this in my 9-month withdrawal a couple of years ago is insomnia.
You feel so hot and itchy and like you are being burned by acid that you just can not fall asleep. My usual time to fall asleep was 3am-5am in the bad flare.
Obviously this impacts the next day greatly.
I ended up taking a natural sleeping pill and st johns wort to help. But this is where it can be difficult to get circadian rhythm in line. And I would say that, that 9months with poor sleep is one reason I headed deep into adrenal fatigue and feeling pretty down.

I definitely will plan this carefully.
My diet is dialed in except for not enough raw protein or organ meat (would like to make pate). Seafood 1-2 everyday.
Been on bottled water for 8 months now.
I have cut all my nails off to reduce damage from scratching.
Daily grounding

Will purchase:
Natural sleeping herbs
St johns wort if required
Adrenal support supplement
Licorice tea
Sago palm vit c (high dose) - I can't take quercetin due to a SNP
Curcumin
Special long sleeve clothing to prevent itching

We plan to get a magnetico, but if we are moving back to Oz not sure it's worth it getting a "northern hemisphere" one as we will need a "southern hemisphere" one there.

I really do believe that with all I have discovered on this site, that I can combine it with the info there and heal easier and quicker.

I read someone did a cosmetic acid peel on their face then used vaseline for 3 days and then did not have any more issues with their face.

I am really intrigued to comprehensively understand about the nerve ending repair and the vasodilation that causes the pain.
There has to be something in that to work with...

### LaurenR — 2014-06-28T18:39:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-132520>

I think i have just developed a duck egg IgE allergy in 5 meals and a few weeks! (i posted this in monster thread).

In order to try something new I have been eating duck eggs occasionally.
I have had them 5 times now.
1st- nothing
2nd- nothing
3rd- felt a bit off unwell
4th- 4 hours later nausea, followed be diarrhea
5th- today this happened...

warning below TMI!

Within a few minutes of eating the eggs (scrambeled with spinach) my throat starting to feel itchy.
Then my tongue feel tingly. The bigger tastebuds at the back of my tongue started to swell.
I stopped eating then took a shower as I felt a bit anxious about what was happening.

Went to lie down, started to feel nausea, 10-20minutes later I developed cramps and a very strong need of vomit.

I didn't vomit but I did have to go to the toilet, 2 x BM- normal.
Went back to bed, and feel asleep for ??15mins maybe.

Woke up more cramps, painful, nausea.
Felt Hot and had tingly hands.
2 x diarrhea

Then extremely intense vomiting! ABout 10 waves/retches all in a row.
Back to bed, more painful cramps, one more diarrhea.
Then back to sleep.

All of that within in 3 hours.

So...is it possible to develop an IgE allergy out of nowhere and with eating duck eggs 5 times?

I have no other IgE allergies (nettle tea gives me itchy throat immediately).

I found it all quite scary as I knew what was going on as soon as felt itchy and my tongue tingly. I wonder where that leaves me with hen eggs...

### Sean Waters — 2018-05-28T11:02:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/laurens-journal.9284/post-242185>

Hi Lauren,

I had eczema since 2 and Crohn's at 16 years old.

Beat Crohn's but developed rash on wrists and hands. After moving to London it spread to arms and legs and nearly everywhere. I also developed fatigue and depression, an inability to go Ketogenic aswell ( insulin resistance / metabolic issue). Hereditary anemia too.

I went to Yucatan Mexico for 2 months to heal but nothing changed. Histamine was identified as an issue.

I took 3-5 TSP of Sea Salt a day and I stopped itching. Came to UK and still fine. The rash on body cleared total. Arms still feint outline of area but unnoticeable. Hands still red and raised but very rare do I scratch.... First time in a month was today! Which is why I'm searching for info....

Plot thickens... My hand rash disappear when I take Fish Oil at extremely high dose (12g).... Jack said early on, skin issues usually tied to an Omega 6 to 3 ratio being out of whack.

Taking 12g o3 is not good idea, I get twitches and feel a bit weird. It has to be done slowly.... Jack said 2-4 years, sane time for adrenal fatigue too, another illness requiring DHA. HEAT and CT and Sun and Saunas and London EMF will all remove water from your cells. You need even more Salt to mitigate post Heat/EMF itching... Its about "salinity", sodium holds water in cells like sea water holds water even in presence of extreme sun the ocean remains. You are losing water and not replacing. Get some sodium. Remove all 06. Including Duck eggs!! It's not IGE it's 06. Pure seafood diet plus la guarande salt. Wild mackerel is cheap. £2 for 2 at tesco now lol

### LaurenR — 2014-01-15T20:12:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-post-finasteride-syndorme-experience-info-in-the-forum.9290/post-105779>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=105155)
>
> Describe how do you know that you have Post-Finasteride Syndrome.
>
> Have you been eating finasteride or applying it to skin or hair?
>
> Write essay about your self.
>
>
> It is my partner, male now aged 28.
> His story:
>
> Age 23, Weight 178lbs, very strong muscular male, started to notice hair falling out on pillow.
> Age 23 started taking finasteride pills- no test results for pre-medication.
>
> Took daily age 23 to week before 27th bday, around 4 years.
> Symptoms while on medication: unusual "ache" in one testicle (scanned showed nothing), itchy and occasional oozing from one nipple.
> Libido, thoughts about sex, erections all remained the same as before.
> TEST RESULTS while TAKING finasteride: BLOOD ONLY
> Free = 764 (225-725)
> Total = 28.3 (8.3-30.2)
> DHA-S= 5.0 (3-10.5)
> SHBG = 24 (13-71)
> FSH: 2 (1-10)
> LH: 4 (1-10)
> Oestradiol: 117 (50-150)
>
>
> Stopped Finasteride due to increased education, understanding.
> Symptoms since stopping:
> Reduced libido, reduced thoughts about sex, reduced "noticing" women, reduced number of morning erections, but still get's erections.
> Pain in testicle has gone as has nipple sensation.
> TEST RESULTS after medication: ALL BLOOD TESTS only:
> Free: 203 (225-725) (was 764!!!)
> Total: 9.8 (8.3-30.2) (was 28.3)
> DHEAS: 5.2 (3-10.5)
> SHBG:30 (13-71)
>
> FSH: 1 (1-10)
> LH: 1 (1-10)
> PROG: 0.6 (1.2-4.8)
> Oestradiol: 51 (50-150)
> low neutrophils, low platelets, low wbc
>
> Now weighs 167lbs (lost 11lbs), noticable decrease in muscle mass (not strength), DEXA scan 6% BF.
> Sleeps fine, asleep in 5-10mins. Hair not falling out.
>
> Thank you so much for you help and assistance. Really appreciate it.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-15T21:06:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-post-finasteride-syndorme-experience-info-in-the-forum.9290/post-105784>

looks like another fried hypothalamus from those meds

### LaurenR — 2014-01-16T16:51:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/any-post-finasteride-syndorme-experience-info-in-the-forum.9290/post-105910>

### nicld — 2014-01-09T16:30:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105161>

Ok, ANY are awesome. Start with you tube and see which ones you might like. Yes, there are many versions of some of the most popular songs but most of them are live recordings.

One of my favorites is Delicate Sound of Thunder, a double live album and then Pulse, another live double album. Pulse has the whole album The Dark Side of the Moon played live. The Wall is pretty good. I am a big fan of the David Gilmour stuff and the the real early stuff it a bit trippy for me.

Maybe I can try to send you a song or two since I have pretty much ALL of them. Just let me know what you might want.

One of my fantasies is to be a background singer for Pink Floyd. They don't need to turn the mic on, just let me be on stage with them

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-10T16:24:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105280>

I love their entire anthology.

Dark side of the moon is epic, as is Wish you were here.

For me personally - Momentary Lapse of Reason was a profound album I bought the tape at a local record store on the release date. I wore that damn thing out. IT was a pivotal album in my teen years.

As I was beginning my career in the Air Force they Released Division Bell. That Album has stayed with me. the lyrics on that album resonated as I was blooming as an adult.

And of course if you get Dark Side of the Moon - you can sync it up and play it while you watch the Wizard of Oz muted. Cool synchronicity totally worth watching just to watch it. I was shocked that DH never did that...

I have done a lot of dumb stuff in my life and have learned from everything so I try to say that I have no regrets but NOT buying tickets to see Pink Floyd on their Pulse tour when they were in Madison WI and Camp Randall is my one regret in life. My hubby lived one block from the stadium and we partook in some adult beverages before the concert since a bunch of his friends had tickets. We started to walk around the stadium seeing if there were tickets available and we found some but I would have had to walk a ways to get some cash and was getting tired from drinking all day so we passed (STUPID, STUPID, STUPID ).

Now, my only hope to seeing them is to either see Roger Waters (which I heard is really good) or David Gilmour if they ever tour again.

### JanSz — 2014-01-11T18:34:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105371>

> [Martin said:](/goto/post?id=105364)
>
> "i wanna be a rock chick! I need to buy a pair of thigh high boots and a mini skirt - stat…"
>
> You don't need all that hardware to be a rocking' chick.
>
>
> But just in case you happen to have a picture…
>
>
>
>
> Big Giant Leering Head

Poland 1973
I was already in USA, picking off gold nuggets lying freely on the streets.
At the time my DW was my DGF and still in Poland.
She was professional classically train ballerina. #3 in the troop, right after white and black swans.

On my request she visited my parents (for the first time).
My parents lived on a small farm, not much different from Afghanistan caves, minus head gear.
She went there in thigh high, red leather boots with fat high heels.
Between miniskirt and top of boots you could see fish net pantyhose.
She was able to charm not only my parents but also our guard dog.

......
Today, the air is 66 Â°F and fog is dense on beach of Myrtle Beach, sea temperature is 69 Â°F.
Wet sand and sea water fills out my body with electrons.
[http://weather.yahoo.com/united-states/south-carolina/myrtle-beach-12770270/](http://weather.yahoo.com/united-states/south-carolina/myrtle-beach-12770270/)

### Josh — 2014-01-12T04:23:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105419>

Consider most rock kills plants, I still like it but it makes me tired after a while. Classical through Stravinsky has transcendent beauty, after him things have gone a bit odd with the exception of Copeland and Gershwin who was more of a jazz classicist. I have been partial to the Grateful Dead, Country and Western and classic jazz. I play all of it poorly. The music that made the plants grow best that is palatable to most humans is Indian Classical...The Sonic Bloom stuff that is now used commercially is not really for humans, but the plants seem to love it. Sacred music of all types works when one is available to the sacred and I have found the sacred in rock and roll in the right state. I will confess that I listened to a Bossa Nova version of Guns and Roses greatest hits daily for a year while doing coffee enemas, do with it what you may. I wore purple overalls to the Met and sat in the 6th row center for 2 years with my grandfather's subscription after he died, I do not think I ever got why I was there. Years later, I saw the Ring Cycle from way up high and loved it. The funny thing is that as I have continued to meditate, all music sounds new, no matter how many times I have heard it. One of those little blessings and paradoxes....

### prAna303 — 2014-01-12T19:27:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105464>

Since we are into the subject of music, resonance is as i said in others posts very important for me. These waves makes me work but then i also wonder and feel like this.

How do you listen to music?

What kind of transporter and recreator of the music do you use?

Most new stuff is made with modern technology, digital ways of sampling and creating new tunes. This would in my way of thinking be very wrong. When you sample something you cut off the small details that would take space in digital era. Everything odd and analog is simply removed and made simple.

For me, this makes me feel ill, almost angry. Not just in my way of thinking but also physical, hate to listen to music the modern way. The headphone world, no good for this boy.

The only way to me then is, live with living humans and with good HiFi and vinyl.

When i dance i can tolerate more of the digital stuff because i use it as a tool, a way to connect motion into sound but at home, with a glass of bordeaux wine, candles, vinyl is the way.

I mentioned this somewhere, digital sampling and digital presentation is like a virus for me, a computer virus that makes me feel odd.

### Inger — 2014-01-12T19:40:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105467>

I like music like this

\[video=youtube\_share;tlJfjIxSjmI\]http://youtu.be/tlJfjIxSjmI\[/video\]

I do love classical music or jazz concerts tho, those smaller ones.. I often sat in a very nice hotel bar in Hamburg drinking a glass of wine and listening to their live Jazz band or piano player. I loved that. Here are no such places where I live so I have not been out for long time. I met some interesting people too in those bars, nice to talk too, as it is not too noisy or loud.

### caroline — 2014-01-12T22:45:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105501>

I am trying to process all this...... I think I am worried. It seems I like more mellow stuff - does that mean I have less energy? I don't think I do ..... I feel like I have all this energy bubbling under the surface.

When I go to the Opera - it feels to me like I am greatly enriched and energised in every pore of my being .... I can't contain myself .... like I want to jump out of my skin.

Like being in New York City ......I love every single second of just walking the streets and absorbing it all.

I have to think more...

> I have to think more...

Audrey, for me....when I am tired, I cannot handle fast music much, or even any (I am talking super tired). I do wonder about a correlation - it's like my brain does not want it then. My acupuncturist says, silence is very healing for the kidneys. I also think about the opposite - some people listen to super fast music to be able to do things, have more energy. When I used to run, fast songs would inspire me to keep going. One of my favorite MDs, Bernie Siegel, who has helped a lot of cancer patients, uses a lot of classical music in his practice to help people calm down or something. I can't remember exactly what he said. My DH on the other hand, who is actually a pro musician (or could be, he has his masters in performance from an excellent school though he does it only for fun now), LOVES his fusion jazz and anything fast. When dating, I was OK listening to that music....now that we have been married and I got sicker over the years (especially since we moved to DC in an equally moldy home), I could not hear his music mostly...it is getting better now. Only the classical stuff which thankfully he loves too bc he grew up on it.

That you love being in NYC....is great. I wouldn't. Been there many times, but now I would always rather choose nature. I must be old. LOL.

### freesia — 2014-01-13T02:01:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105509>

Has anyone noticed they can find the right note and pitch much more effortlessly (with their voice) since starting CT?
I have, and at least one other person I know doing CT has mentioned finding the same thing.

My CT embracing youngest child (just 9) made stunning progress playing a classical stringed instrument last year. He's been playing 8 months, and he's playing the same as kids who've ben playing that many years. Also, he taught himself simultaneous translation between clefs (give him music for one instrument and he'll play another) and understanding of how to work harmonies. I think he's likely extra tuned in and I'm thinking CT could be playing a helpful role here...maybe also his natural instinct for what heals (he has some neuro-physical challenges as part of his 'package'). We have never asked him to practise, he's just drawn to it - the way he was to cold.
Maybe I have it backwards yet - the 'seeking what heals' bit - but he's been fascinating for us, and his teachers, to watch.

### nicld — 2014-01-13T02:17:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105510>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105501)
>
> I am trying to process all this...... I think I am worried. It seems I like more mellow stuff - does that mean I have less energy? I don't think I do ..... I feel like I have all this energy bubbling under the surface.
>
> When I go to the Opera - it feels to me like I am greatly enriched and energised in every pore of my being .... I can't contain myself .... like I want to jump out of my skin.
>
> Like being in New York City ......I love every single second of just walking the streets and absorbing it all.
>
> I have to think more...

Ok, stop over thinking this and just enjoy music for how it makes you feel, whether that be happy, sad, mellow, calm, excited, pumped up or angry. It's all good

### cantweight — 2014-01-13T04:15:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105516>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=105513)
>
> I did notice an improvement in my singing voice, but I attributed it to the seafood. But yes, I was doing CT as well.

I would love for someone to elaborate on what is occurring in the body when this happens. I was an opera major in college and have sung most of my life....whatever that shift is that changes the singing ability is so profound that when it happens to me I cry. My voice comes back strong, pitch is effortless like freesia said, sound quality is great, range is improved......and I dont have apnea when these open throat episode occur!

I have had them last as long as several weeks and as short as a few hours. There is something very significant happening. I have had it happen with CT and without. I have had it happen while using hcg...every round, it even talks about it in an hcg book "you may find your singing voice improves".

Whatever "it" is feels like my holy grail....I experience a massive upswing in that state. Mental clarity and focus are improved as well. Generally better energy and well being.

What is "it"?

### caroline — 2014-01-13T10:56:26+0200

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=105446)
>
> It indicates our bodies reception of sound waves (energy) and how our brain both interprets it and leverages it as free energy. Certain music will resonate more than other music because of your body's state of energy.

I absolutely understand this on a personal level .... but what I was initially wondering is - can I make assumptions about you by what music you like?

After some reflection - I think not .... but JK seems to be saying that what music we like tells someone a lot about us.

Of course we would get a snapshot of the moment - and someone's energy in that moment....

Ohhhhh - that's what he meant?

But I can't just make a wholesale judgement about you if, for instance you only liked heavy metal .....

If you were trying to seduce someone - and didn't know them terribly well ..... what music would you choose???

and - even if you did know them very well - you would need to gauge their energy for maximum affect?

It takes a symphony .....

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105541>

Interesting stuff…

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QDRaCTVO67w&feature=youtu.be](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QDRaCTVO67w&feature=youtu.be)

Here is the link to the Ayahuasca Shamanism in the Amazon where the healing woman is singing the 432Hz tones.

http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=M7T02k…"
_

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-13T16:48:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105545>

You might like to go to RikBarron.com....his last three cds are in 432..one is an instrumental and the other is trad/folk (plus his last children's folk cd). No whales or chants but great music all the same...just remember he's my husband, so if you don't like it....keep it to yourself  Actually, I wouldn't mind at all...music is all about what makes you sing inside and we are all different.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T17:09:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105547>

Thanks, SeaHorse! So "Speechless" and "Over in the Meadow" are in 432? I am interested in the instrumental. I see he has a hymn in there. I liked what I heard of the samples.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-13T17:33:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105550>

"Speechless" "Never so Far" and "Over in the Meadow" are all in 432...he won't record in anything else now, but when he plays with other people he still has to tune up. We joke that that 432 is the secret key to the universe and he's out to take over the world one note at a time...but there is no doubt that it changes the experience of sound for both the listener and the player...subtle energies that have a lot of depth. The difference between listening with your head and listening with your heart.

### Ricardo — 2014-01-13T19:06:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-105560>

I remember going to seaside wooded areas in Nova Scotia:

Listening to the sounds of the carbon based lifeforms existing harmoniously in symbiosis is very grounding and satisfying.

The sound of leaves rustling gently in the wind, forest creatures climbing and scampering about, the incoming waves splashing on the rocks....I think we are hard-wired to feel good in this kind of sound-scape.

I like a lot of musics, but nature has an attraction that is undeniable.

### Da-mo — 2014-01-19T02:13:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106170>

A Momentary Lapse of Reason is my favourite Floyd album.

I've seen lots of big acts live but the one I really regret missing happened as a 1 time only concert only an hour from me. The Auckland Philharmonic asked Serj Tankian, composer and System of a Down front man, to do a concert with them. Check these clips out from that live performance and notice that the crowd is not who you would normally see at a symphony.
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wig6u3ExQM8](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wig6u3ExQM8) The Charade
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mMBCBn66nwo](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mMBCBn66nwo) Baby
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QfCiad6Tm\_E](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QfCiad6Tm_E) Gate 21
[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GEkQR9Wzock](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GEkQR9Wzock) Empty Walls

BTW, the rock versions are awesome too if you like it better that way. e.g. [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bobdhszyDzk](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bobdhszyDzk) The Charade Rock Version

"I do believe
You are strong in forming violence
We are strong performing silence
We are deceived
By all the toys of our convenience
Opening our hearts to their brilliance"

. . . . aint that the truth!!

### caroline — 2014-01-20T00:32:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106231>

I love all of this.... Thanks Dr. K. and Sandy and Nicole. All of this enriches your soul - takes you to another level - a deeper level.

I was thinking that I missed way too much because I spent too much time working - but in reality, I spent my days talking/listening to hundreds of people - they enriched my soul massively. I used to choose the CDs for the restaurant and it was always a topic of conversation - what is playing? can I get it at the music shop next door?
So - I had to choose music that most people would love and enjoy and we could talk about.

Do you guys know Gordon Lightfoot?

I love B.B. King... one of the very favorite restaurant CDs - it was hugely important to have an engaging backdrop.....

The soundscape to my life is so different from you guys...... we spent a lot of time in the Bahamas with a bunch of guys \[a band\] who owned a supper club .....we spent as much time as possible at the club listening to them and dancing.

Sun, sand and sea and the wonderful Bahamian culture and music. So - my life has been so different ... as all of ours have been. Fascinating ......or am I just easily amused???

### caroline — 2014-01-20T00:35:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106232>

Dr. K. - your second music selection .... That woman is so stunning .... the perfect "golden triangle"?

### caroline — 2014-01-20T00:44:03+0200

So - what about guys then?

### Da-mo — 2014-01-20T01:04:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106241>

Not sure about body shape/musical preference links

I have read that we're kinda hard wired for certain harmonics and sound progressions that are fibonacci ratios related - in the same way that we are visually wired to find certain proportions more appealing. Even the timing of music, the spaces between the notes show relationships to these numbers. Even down th the ratio of one turn of DNA versus the length it takes to make the turn. All through the natural universe it seems.

### caroline — 2014-01-20T01:15:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106244>

I think, maybe, it is all tied together......

But - our energy levels would be huge too ......we wouldn't be attracted to some one with low energy....

The quantum harmony of our life must include all of this....

### caroline — 2014-01-20T01:22:26+0200

### Da-mo — 2014-01-21T09:55:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106438>

Just been reading 'Energy and Epigenetics 4: Light, Water and Magnetism' and I was reminded of another couple of songs by Serj Tankian from his 'Imperfect Harmonies' album.

"Peace Be Revenged" references to rain, mitochondria and disease and disregard for nature [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DRfYgZl3Z9U](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DRfYgZl3Z9U)
Lyrics for Peace Be Revenged [http://songmeanings.com/songs/view/3530822107858844955/](http://songmeanings.com/songs/view/3530822107858844955/)

_Once we used to hear,
The light footsteps of dear,
Now we have no fear
And in turn we'll disappear,
We have built our lives,
Without nature in mind,
Slowly we've become the disease
But we won't go_

"Electron" references to electrons, protons, rain, sunlight and lightning [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TnwZOBswzQw](http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TnwZOBswzQw)
Lyrics for Electron [http://songmeanings.com/songs/view/3530822107858844951/](http://songmeanings.com/songs/view/3530822107858844951/)

_We follow the ever-falling rain,
Electrons protons fight again,
So, we run,
We follow the ever-rising sun,
With feelings of all becoming one,
Then, we fly, . . . . ._

_"When people ask me what religion I am, I always say, see that tree outside, go ask the trees the same thing, you know? I believe very firmly that indigenous populations had a really good, intuitive understanding of why we're here. And we're trying to gain that same understanding through psychology and intellect in modern civilization."_ \- Serj Tankian

I just find his music so inspirational, just like JK's work. Anyway, 'EE4: Light Water and Magnetism' reminded me of these songs . . . . .

### Gagnrad — 2014-01-24T11:58:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-106896>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106890)
>
> nice Mike - thanks .....Have you seen them live?

Yes, I have. It was a pretty good show.

There's a video of Sting from The Police singing "Can She Excuse My Wrongs" by the Elizabethan lutenist John Dowland. It's quite powerful done in updated style with multiple over-dubbed vocals and the lute player who accompanies him, Karamazov, doing something like a guitar-solo on his lute. Maybe Dowland would have approved? It is treating his music as part of contemporary culture rather than pickling it in aspic in an (anyway conjectural) authentic performance. And people have said that Dowland was like the rockstar of his day!

\[video=youtube;nntri9OfaRY\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nntri9OfaRY\[/video\]

> Can she excuse my wrongs with Virtue's cloak?
> Shall I call her good when she proves unkind?
> Are those clear fires which vanish into smoke?
> Must I praise the leaves where no fruit I find?
>
> No no: where shadows do for bodies stand,
> Thou may'st be abus'd if thy sight be dim.
> Cold love is like to words written on sand,
> Or to bubbles which on the water swim.
>
> Wilt thou be thus abused still,
> Seeing that she will right thee never?
> If thou canst not o'ercome her will
> The love will be thus fruitless ever.
>
> Was I so base, that I might not aspire
> Unto those high joys which she holds from me?
> As they are high , so high is my desire:
> If she this deny, what can granted be?
>
> If she will yeld to that which reason is,
> It is Reason's will that Love should be just.
> Dear make me happy still be granting this,
> Or cut off delays if that die I must.
>
> Better a thousand times to die,
> Than for to live thus still tormented:
> Dear, but remember it was I
> Who for thy sake did die contended

### Nick — 2014-01-29T10:01:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-107267>

Hmmm, Pink Floyd...... First heard PF The Wall when I was introduced to it by my very cool, dope smoking, hippy, buddhist uncle in the mid eighties. I had not heard anything like it and it quickly accelerated my love of music in a new direction. I grew up listening to music, my parents wouldn't let us have a TV till I was about 10 so instead we would always listen to the radio or play records. This has carried on to the current day where at home we watch very little TV but play music most of the time. My 3yo and 7yo react spontaneously and instantly whenever a track comes on that they like. They can't help but break into movement dance and motion, it is awesome to watch and a lot of fun.

Over the last 20 years my ever growing hobby has been to build a better stereo to listen to music on. I have been upgrading and improving steadily where I am now at the point where it is an amazing experience. I am not an audiophile and I get annoyed with the sort of people that will analyse a sound to death, all I ever wanted to do was play the music that I like up loud and to be able top bask in a sea of music, clean and undistorted. I wish I had played Jude some music when she was over but that connection hadn't been made at the time.

After reading JK's insights into sounds and frequencies and health I am certainly looking at my hobby in a new light. Couldn't tell you what that is but I am watching this space with a lot of interest as Jack continues with the journey. In the meantime I never ever imagined that this forum would be so into Pink Floyd, that is crazy and can't be a coincidence????

### prAna303 — 2014-02-05T23:16:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-108566>

Yes, always.

Love is nothing we loose, it is a form of resonance that can not be silenced. When music is used as a therapy, natural part of memory is restored.

I think we have a wrong way of seeing time, mostly because we do not have enough energy to expand our view. Loosing someone is just letting someone go.

### caroline — 2014-02-05T23:24:24+0200

> Now let me tell you one very basic yoga structure of your being.
> Just as physicists think that the whole consists of nothing but electrons, electric energy, yoga thinks that the whole consists of nothing but sound electrons. The basic element of existence for yoga is sound because life is nothing but a vibration. Life is nothing but an expression of silence. Out of silence we come and into silence we dissolve again. Silence, space, nothingness, nonbeing, is your innermost core, the hub of the wheel. Unless you come to that silence, to that space where nothing else remains except your pure being, liberation is not attained. This is the yoga framework.

Now VAN....he's quite the combo of cerebral fireworks and a crazy good voice. He is my all time favourite for long car trips. Driving back and forth between Newfoundland and Vermont my girls knew that we had to listen to Van Morrison at least once every hour (we were in the car for a couple of days). I love listening to all the changes and experiments he's made with his music over the years.. Willie and Ray....perfect! All of them are amazing collaborators with other musicians....something that happens when you have a creative, generous heart.

OK I think we all need to have a meet up just so we can listen to music together!

### SeaHorse — 2014-02-22T04:05:13+0200

\[

> quote="JoeBranca, post: 111605, member: 784"\]Nice! Coincidentally I just found out BT (I linked a couple of his pieces above) put out an album in 432 Hz in 2012. It is electronic and beatless but with classically informed piano... Also just the first few minutes embraces in a warm, embryonic way

I liked that too..
Joe...do you know Steve Roach's music? I studied in Arizona with his wife Linda Kohanov and we got to hear a lot of his music in his studio. So, I looked up what this type of music is called and this is what I came up with.....tribal ambient, space, new age electronic..what a mouthful!

### JoeBranca — 2014-02-22T06:22:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-111665>

That's great! I'm a little bit aware of Steve Roach's music, I know he's worked with Fripp and I think Steve Reich, with whom I'm a little more familiar. I fell into new territories like ambient, electronic, avant garde, minimalist, & free jazz back in college once I realized all kinds of musical idioms were out there, hand crafted from so many other exotic places other than what we grow up with.

What struck me is not so much the alternative spiritual underpinnings in a lot of these forms, but the overall modernist approach to a deeper attention and sense of space within sound, the actual notes and space between the notes becoming a basic architecture defining some intersting, implied environments. Music that's sort of \*with\* you rather than playing \*at\* you. There's a lot of pretenders in these genres too, but I really like to camp out sometimes in the works of the true artists.

### SeaHorse — 2014-02-22T15:05:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-111682>

Yes...I make a big distinction between the kind of mechanical, endless rainforest musak that plays in the naturapaths office and people who actually listen to water and do something amazing with the sounds. It's a good way to choose yoga teachers too....do they play top-40 new age or are you listening to real artists.

On the other hand, a couple of years ago I was listening to a lot of recordings with binaural beats and/or particular brain wave patterns. One of my favorites was simply the sound of rain...from Immrama institute...no flutes or synth. needed. It was really effective at changing my thinking because of the range of embedded frequencies. It wasn't creative like Steve Roach's music though. I like his CD Serpent's Lair the best. Dr Weil (not my favourite MD in many respects) put out a sound therapy CD set that had a lecture and then a selection of tunes...that was great music too.

Still, nothing's better than being outside and opening your ears. I heard a totally new sound last week. We've had a lot of snow and the sound of it moving off the roof is familiar it kind of rumbles and whooshes like a giant zipper...this time I was out in a fairly steep sloping field and the snow was very, very sandy, light about two feet deep and hadn't been touched. The vibration of my walking through the snow set off a fissure opening in the top layer further down the slope... the sound was like a snake hissing through dry grass but deeper....my dog completely lost his mind and couldn't place where it happened or what happened. I didn't see the crack opening until the second time it happened and I thought is was so cool....a new sound from snow that I'd never heard before.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-03T10:45:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-113149>

Here's another folksong. It begins with a lark, as all good folksongs should. It has a girl binding her golden hair. There's an outlaw, who's being hunted, including by the girl's family. She escapes to the wilds to go to him, but on the way gets caught in a storm and drowned:

> Well morn has come, she rose and fled
> From her cruel kin and home;
> And bright the wood and rosy red
> And the tumbling torrent's foam.
> But the mist came down and the tempest roared,
> And all around did destroy;
> And a pale, drowned bride met Renardine,
> On the mountains of Pomeroy.

Well ... it wouldn't be a folksong if the girl didn't get drowned.

### caroline — 2014-03-05T07:13:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-113515>

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-05T19:52:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-113624>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=113515)
>
> crap ..... the girl with the beautiful hair drowns.....

Here's another folksong played by the Hot Rats. This one is called "Step it out Mary".

It's about a girl with long and golden hair. Her father tries to marry her to a rich man, but she's in love with a soldier … so she drowns herself.

This is getting to be a familiar theme …

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-05T20:02:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-113627>

Here's a jollier folksong. I think the words were collected by Cecil Sharp in Norfolk but the tune in Somerset.

Here the girl says to the soldier:

> But if you come round to my mummy's house, when the moon shines bright and clearly,
> I will come down and let you in, and my mummy shall not hear me.

And no-one drowns.

Here's the same tune used in a piece by Ralph Vaughan Williams. Quite different in feel.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-05T20:24:12+0200

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-14T09:09:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115330>

OK, maybe time for another video. Here's a recitation for a change. Alec Guinness reads the first part of T. S. Eliot's _The Wasteland_ … a "heap of broken images" …

> —Yet when we came back, late, from the Hyacinth garden,
> Your arms full, and your hair wet, I could not
> Speak, and my eyes failed, I was neither
> Living nor dead, and I knew nothing,
> Looking into the heart of light, the silence.
> _Öd’ und leer das Meer._

… and we also have "Madame Sosostris, famous clairvoyante" which should please Audrey.

### NeilBB — 2014-03-14T19:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115417>

For you Pink Floyd fans. One of Roger Waters best solo songs, very appropriate for our modern dilemmas...

We watched the tragedy unfold
We did as we were told
We bought and sold
It was the greatest show on earth
But then it was over
We ohhed and aahed
We drove our racing cars
We ate our last few jars of caviar
And somewhere out there in the stars
A keen-eyed look-out
Spied a flickering light
Our last hurrah
And when they found our shadows
Grouped around the TV sets
They ran down every lead
They repeated every test
They checked out all the data on their lists
And then the alien anthropologists
Admitted they were still perplexed
But on eliminating every other reason
For our sad demise
They logged the only explanation left
This species has amused itself to death
No tears to cry no feelings left
This species has amused itself to death

### Josh — 2014-03-14T20:11:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115430>

Eliot and Gurdjieff were contemporaries....

> "Dear Grandfather! Your thorough explanations of various episodes that occurred during the process of existence of the three-brained beings on the planet Earth have given me a clear idea and satisfactory understanding of the surprising strangeness of their psyche; nevertheless, the question still arises in me about one particularity of theirs which I cannot yet understand at all and which, even taking into account the strangeness of their psyche, appears to me illogical. My thoughts keep returning to this perplexing question and were even concentrated on it during the holy sacrament in the djamdjampal.
> "From all your explanations about the process of existence of these three-brained beings, I have seen very clearly that although most of them, during their responsible existence, particularly since the third transapalnian perturbation, have a purely automatic Reason, yet even with this automatic Reason they can deliberate so well that they are able to determine, more or less exactly, many of the laws of Nature on their planet, in accordance with which they can even invent certain things on their own.
> "At the same time, parallel with this, reminders of that particularity proper to them alone—that is, the periodic need
> to destroy one another's existence—run like a crimson thread through all your tales.
> "And so, dear Grandfather, I cannot understand at all how it happens that in spite of having existed for such a long time, they have never become aware and even now remain unaware of the horror of this property of theirs.
> "Don't they really ever see that of all the horrors that could possibly exist in the whole of the Universe, these processes are the most terrible? And don't they ever ponder on this matter so that they might become aware of this horror and find some means of eradicating it?
> "Please, Grandfather, tell me why this is so, and which aspects among all
> those making up the strangeness of their psyche are the causes of this
> particularity of theirs?"

> " 'Plague and cholera are in any event nobler than human honesty, for people with a conscience can at least live at peace with them. '

> "Certainly there is something not quite right here.
> "But if nothing could be done by that Being who has now the Reason of
> the sacred 'Podkoolad' and is one of the first assistants of our Endlessness in
> the government of the world, namely, the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash—if
> even he could do nothing for the beings of that planet, what can we say, we
> whose Reason is scarcely higher than that of ordinary beings?
> "Do you remember how the Very Saintly Ashiata Shiemash, in his
> deliberations under the title of 'The Terror of the Situation,' said.
> " 'If it is still possible to save the beings of the Earth, Time alone can do it.'
> "As for us, we can only say the same thing in regard to this terrible
> property of theirs we have just been speaking of, that is, their periodic need to
> destroy each other's existence.
> "For the moment, we can only say that if this property of the terrestrial
> beings is to disappear from that unfortunate planet, it will be through Time
> alone, either under the guidance of a Being with very high Reason or thanks
> to certain exceptional cosmic events.

George Gurdjieff, _Beelzebub's Tales to his Grandson , chapter 43

> "The sole means now of saving the beings of the planet Earth would be to
> implant in their presence a new organ, an organ like kundabuffer, but this
> time having such properties that every one of these unfortunates, during the process of his existence, should constantly sense and be aware of the inevitability of his own death, as well as of the death of everyone upon whom his eyes, or attention, rest.
> "Only such a sensation and such an awareness could destroy the egoism now so completely crystallized in them that it has swallowed up the whole of their essence, and at the same time uproot that tendency to hate others which flows from it—the tendency that engenders those mutual relationships which are the chief cause of all their abnormalities, unbecoming to three-brained beings and maleficent for them and for the whole of the Universe. "

Chpater 47_

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-15T09:03:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115546>

There's a version of _The Love Song of Alfred J. Prufrock_ at the free site Librivox.

The reader maybe goes a bit towards over-emphasis and over-characterisation, but it is a free volunteer reading after all:

[https://librivox.org/short-poetry-collection-118/](https://librivox.org/short-poetry-collection-118/)

There's actually a recording of Eliot himself reading Prufrock around. Eliot reads his own poetry in a very flat and rather chanting way, though:

### Josh — 2014-03-15T17:34:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115614>

> [NeilBB said:](/goto/post?id=115604)
>
> Headpiece stuffed with straw ... sounds like a dietitian.

> LOL Sure does. And speaking of that, anybody in US ever happen to catch Joy Bauer on NBC in the AM? Saw her today. She looks more like an emaciated skeleton all the time, and this is what passes for a "national nutritional expert" nowadays. She was counting some calories again for us this AM...

> To quote Eliot, quoting Joseph Conrad, "The Horror. The Horror..."

Perhaps we have not quite reached the Kurtzian stage or maybe we have passed it, I see us as the mate in Conrad's "Youth" sailing away from the smoldering, soon to explode ship in his "first command" a dinghy with a jury-rig. He waxes rhapsodic about being "in command" for the first time against a backdrop of the sinking ship. He sees it as an adventure, yet it is colored by both the loss of the ship and cargo and the strangeness of the colonial world he sails into. He notes that "Youth" colors all of these things favorably.....Perhaps we are all sailing our lifeboats and dreaming that we are in our first "command" while the mothership smolders and then explodes in the background......Unfortunately, there does not seem to be any warm port for us to sail into with new adventures awaiting us........In some ways, this "Youth" may actually come after the Kurtzian stage, perhaps WWI was really the Kurtzian moment in modern history.....

### SeaHorse — 2014-03-15T20:21:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115653>

Last year I started a lengthy thread about what it felt like to be on the metaphorical Titanic.....the thread kind of went off the rails (mixed metaphors).

I ended up feeling that we really need to build our own lifeboats ...a terrifying, lonely prospect, yet potentially able to sustain a new kind of life...while the crowd on the mother ship faced certain drowning.

There is a possibility of these single lifeboats forming a flotilla......which is how I see the forum and the blogs. ............I hope there is some sense within this imagery!!

### NeilBB — 2014-03-15T20:59:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115659>

> [SeaHorse said:](/goto/post?id=115653)
>
> Last year I started a lengthy thread about what it felt like to be on the metaphorical Titanic.....the thread kind of went off the rails (mixed metaphors).
>
> I ended up feeling that we really need to build our own lifeboats ...a terrifying, lonely prospect, yet potentially able to sustain a new kind of life...while the crowd on the mother ship faced certain drowning.
>
> There is a possibility of these single lifeboats forming a flotilla......which is how I see the forum and the blogs. ............I hope there is some sense within this imagery!!

I like that very much. I have often myself envisioned modern civilization as a huge ocean liner not necessarily crashed yet, but venturing aggressively and brazenly into enemy territory. (Mankind's current war on nature). The dilemma is that the large ship can still protect us today from the harsher elements of nature (advantages of civilization) in ways that little lifeboats cant. The bad news is that the ocean liners course is foolishly dangerous now and it can only be a matter of time before it sinks.

The current answer seems to maybe involve not little independent lifeboats, but maybe a nimble band of speedboats that stays in the wake of the liner, checking in with it at times, trying to influence the liner's captain, if possible. And always keeping an eye out for inhabitable islands to steer towards when the liner finally does start to really take on water.

### Gagnrad — 2014-03-16T10:29:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-115741>

Here's a YouTube video of Richard Burton reading _The Rime of the Ancient Mariner_:

> It is an ancient Mariner,
> And he stoppeth one of three.

I expect the Aussies here will be saying, "Sounds like an England wicket keeper, mate!"

I think Coleridge had been reading writings of the mystics, maybe Jakob Böhme, or someone like that, and had a sense of everything being connected. The point seems to be that the mariner needlessly kills the albatross and somehow because of that everything goes wrong.

He finishes the poem coming back to that connectedness ... and love:

> He tries to finish
> He prayeth best, who loveth best
> All things both great and small;
> For the dear God who loveth us,
> He made and loveth all.

[http://www.poetryfoundation.org/poem/173253](http://www.poetryfoundation.org/poem/173253)

### caroline — 2014-03-24T09:24:50+0200

### prAna303 — 2014-03-24T10:10:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-117554>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=117551)
>
> Peter and Josh .... are you good dancers? do you light up your space?

In my own eyes, just someone who is scratching the surface in dance.

In all of my years of clubbing, dance has been my identity, this is where i can expand myself. From early years of breakdance to house music and hip hop, i have done it all. Not to brag but if i enter a dancefloor i stand out, yes. I take space, i resonate with the music, i connect with the ones that want to. The things with dance in modern days, say at nightclubs is that men get a little annoyed, girls take that side or the one where they gather up with me. Today you should just be part of the herd when dancing, but if you are dance in your soul you must stand out.

For many years, dj´s have been calling me to go out on that special night, they want me there because i am a reason for them to make art...

Not everyone will understand the feeling i get when i get a chance to let go... The harder, the darker side of techno is my deepest passion ever.

Live, not so good sound but one of the females today that delivers something that resonates with me.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-29T11:28:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-118645>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=118552)
>
> [http://www.washingtonpost.com/blogs...want-on-the-dance-floor-according-to-science/](http://www.washingtonpost.com/blogs/wonkblog/wp/2014/03/24/what-women-want-on-the-dance-floor-according-to-science/)

That was an painful article for me... Talk about missing so many keypoints in the game between man and woman. For me, those two videos show nothing of dance, more of weird science. I showed it to three girls lats night, just to test and they all said, what the hell is that... Dance is like a way to connect and let go, to share and gather as a tribe, no matter the level you are at.

Later on we watched a classic movie about a culture in USA mainly, crumping and clowning. Guess what, they all said that it resonated so much with them and if they would see it live they would pick those guys any day over the ones that do not dance.

Like that dude Einstein said: Dancers are the athletes of god...

I am givin' you all a two month heads up.........after the May webinar is over...........I got a funny feeling this will be popular: The first four minutes of this video.........are going to parallel this webinar.........and then at 4:00.............BOOM. Next level entered.

### prAna303 — 2014-03-30T09:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-118776>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=118757)
>
> I am givin' you all a two month heads up.........after the May webinar is over...........I got a funny feeling this will be popular: The first four minutes of this video.........are going to parallel this webinar.........and then at 4:00.............BOOM. Next level entered.
>
>
> That is all I am gonna say...................

Nice, a little game of teasing...

For me i get a lot of reactions when listening to this, same with many pieces of Vangelis...

The first part is slowly building up, opening my mind, if played LOUD on a good system. When i do this, vinyl, really loud, the sound picture is expanding, my room becomes extra large. This is like a new life starting, slowly building up.

The second part, where the drums comes alive, is becoming alive, because the usage of tones, vibrations and connecting all senses. The hand in sync with every instrument, like how your body works in sync if you are alive. Drums have been with us from the beginning i think, a way to not only communicate but also activate...

This is building up, good, i need some brain food for my summernights...

### prAna303 — 2014-03-30T10:22:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-118781>

And how it feels to make music by yourself... Have you ever tried it? Holding a guitar, just touching the strings, slowly start play, feel the things happening inside you. When a chord is played, it resonates directly with you and you are able to activate your ability to create energy... I think this is why we tend to use music for memories... The vibrations that penetrate skin, ears, eyes and also the direct electrical actions that is produced in us.

We are build of water, how does sound and touch travel thru water?

When i do my freediving a get some really strong feelings of life. Sounds get more vibrant, light gets more rich tone to it. When i touch stuff in the water it feels more, it melts into my skin.

Like i always thought, piano is the quantum computer that all computer geeks tries to manufacture today... I think, so strongly, we must go analog style again.

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-01T10:47:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-119184>

> [prAna303 said:](/goto/post?id=118849)
>
> Once in a small piece of art we can see how pictures, music and emotions sync...

Talking of different forms working together brings Richard Wagner to mind. He was all about the total experience, wasn't he? He had massive reservations about the opera of his day, and I don't think even liked or used the term "opera". He saw himself as trying to devise a new art-form. The music and the words and the visuals (he actually had maniacally detailed written instructions for stage-sets) all had to work together and draw the emotional picture he wanted. Thus in Tristan and Isolde you get this protracted refusal to resolve discordance -- you can _hear_ the emotional intensity.

If I were more musical and my German was better maybe I could sit through a whole Ring Cycle ...

### Gagnrad — 2014-04-25T17:22:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-123444>

Surely time for some more music ...

Mediaeval songs of love and loss, as sung by the troubadours. This is sung in Occitan; they've tried to reconstruct how the music might have sounded. It's quite haunting.

> That mountain before me
> Is ablaze and burning;
> There I lost my love.
> I sit down and weep. ...

### Albert83BCN — 2014-05-16T10:46:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-127196>

"The visions dancing in my mind
The early dawn, the shades of time
Twilight crawling through my windowpane

Am I awake or do I dream,
The strangest pictures I have seen
Night is day and twilight's gone away

With your head held high and your scarlet lies
You came down to me from the open skies
It's either real or it's a dream
There's nothing that is in between

Twilight, I only meant to stay awhile
Twilight, I gave you time to steal my mind
Away from me.

Across the night I saw your face
You disappeared without a trace
You brought me here, but can you take me back

Inside the image of your light
That now is day and once was night
You lead me here and then you go away.

Twilight, I only meant to stay awhile
Twilight, I gave you time to steal my mind
Away from me.

You brought me here, but can you take me back again?

With your head held high and your scarlet lies
You came down to me from the open skies
It's either real or it's a dream
There's nothing that is in between

Twilight, I only meant to stay awhile
Twilight, I gave you time to steal my mind
Away from me.

Twilight, I only meant to stay awhile
Twilight, I only meant to stay awhile
Twilight, Twilight, Twilight, Twilight."

### prAna303 — 2014-08-09T22:23:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-137402>

The subject of music...

For many years i put away my obsession... I did it almost like in secret, just small bits here and there. Often in private even if i was public, you know the kick without showing it...

I let my own history be silent, lurking, slowly building up... Time? What is that, is it forced with statics, rules and measure points only?

But then again, after four years of escape... Digging deep in my collection, there it was... Wooden, mechanical, oil, craftmanship and pure passion signed by heavy weight. Who wants this today?

The capsule is restored, music is flowing from a gone by era but what great loss has mankind done, throwing away diamonds and pearls for ones and zeroes from an ugly spotify account.

What, what is he talking about?

Think he is saying that the art of audio and analog ways of presenting it is what many is missing today.

So, to give my life and my spirit some extra vivid energy i accept that bad sound, bad presentation is not going to make it. I need the high end world no matter what...

### prAna303 — 2014-08-09T22:57:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-137405>

> [nicld said:](/goto/post?id=144070)
>
> Sounds alot like the Psychedelic Furs, very 80's

Do you know what "Joy Division" refers to? They had their own dark perspective....

> **Joy Division** may refer to:
>
>
> -   The Joy Division, a group of Jewish women forced to work as sex slaves in Nazi concentration camps, as depicted in the 1955 novel _[House of Dolls](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/House_of_Dolls)_
> -   [German camp brothels in World War II](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/German_camp_brothels_in_World_War_II)
> -   [Kippumjo](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kippumjo), translated variously as "Pleasure Brigade" or "Joy Division," a collective of females maintained by the head of state of [North Korea](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/North_Korea) for the purpose of providing pleasure and entertainment to officials, formed in 1978

### Da-mo — 2014-11-13T20:45:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-147303>

I'm a big Serj Tankian fan - anything from "Elect the Dead" for the rock versions or "Elect the Dead - Symphony" for the classical versions. Such profound lyrics too.

**Electron**

We follow the ever-falling rain
Electrons Protons fight again
So, we run
We follow the ever-rising sun
With feelings of all becoming one
Then, we fly

We fly in our dreams ever so bold
Swimming the seas covered with gold
Behold, proceed
Proceed with the blessing of all the gods
Amidst the time of men at odds
See, believe

Tell me, what it is that we believe
The storm shines bright
Tell me, what it is that we believe
The storm shines bright

Grapevines of thoughts lining the skies
Overwhelmingly of demise
So we sing
We sing to the spirit world so close
Providing our vital life force
Then we breathe

We breathe with the breath of I-Ching
With the spirit that moves through all things
Now, we see
Seeing of all that is alive
Will be the way to survive
Life, released

### JoeBranca — 2015-03-30T02:01:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-159798>

this is one of those days where I revisit an old, familiar song with fresh ears, and the lyrics speak fresh relevance within my current headspace. This one has hit me as I think about the cards being stacked against us in so many ways with the wrong turns the institutions of science, medicine and technology have taken over decades; _nevertheless_ individual, executable insight is always in reach as long as we are incurably curious. No need to spend a lifetime chasing around gurus for derivative enlightenments.

You can fool yourself
You can cheat until you're blind
You can cut your heart
It can happen

You can mend the wires
You can feed the soul apart
You reach
It can happen to you
It can happen to me
It can happen to everyone eventually

It's a constant fight
A constant fight
You're pushing the needle to the red
Black and white
Who knows who's right
No substitute you're born you're dead
Fly by night
Created out of fantasy
Our destinations call

Look up
Look down
Look out
Look around
Look up
Look down
There's a crazy world outside
We're not about to lose our pride

It can happen to you
It can happen to me
It can happen to everyone eventually

As you happen to say
It can happen today
As it happens
It happens in every way

This world I like
We architects of life
A song a sigh
Developing words that linger
Through fields of green through open eyes
This for us to see

Look up
Look down
Look out
Look around
So look up
Look down
There's a crazy world outside
We're not about to lose our pride

It can happen to you
It can happen to me
It can happen to everyone eventually

As you happen to say
It can happen today
As it happens
It happens in every way

As you happen to see
It will happen to be
Nothing happens to nowhere and nowhere

Solo

Look up
Look down
There's a crazy world outside
We're not about to lose our pride

It can happen to you
It can happen to me
It can happen to everyone eventually

As you happen to see
It will happen to be
Nothing happens to nowhere and nowhere

You can fool yourself
You can cheat until you're blind
You can cut your heart

You can fool yourself It can happen to you
You can cheat until you're blind It can happen to me

You can cut your heart It can happen to eveyone eventually
As you happen to say
It can happen today
As it happens
It happens in every way

You can mend the wires
You can feed the soul apart
You can touch your life
You can bring your soul alive
It can happen to you
It can happen to me
It can happen to everyone eventually

As you happen to say
It can happen today
As it happens
It happens in every way
