### JoeBranca — 2015-04-19T02:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-161689>

I'm on a Herzog tip...

about the 15 year old actress in the film Aguirre, a film that entangles you in the jungle and the river

> "She, like the local Indians, is played by a non-actress, in fact a girl plucked out of a Peruvian high school nearly at random.
>
> **The less she does, the more transcendent her presence. We don't see her die, but as she knows she is dying she looks at her father as if to transfer all her energy to the space immediately surrounding him.** That space does become filled with fantastic life on the raft and all that surrounds it, creating the sequence that ends the film. That scene perfectly compliments the first, where the people are the river bringing their seed to the promised land. In the last, the seed is released and the river itself carries the offspring of the imagination to the treetops."

[http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0068182/reviews-44](http://www.imdb.com/title/tt0068182/reviews-44)

### JoeBranca — 2015-05-29T07:24:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-165763>

For those who can accept the premised elements of electronic dance music - rhythmic repetition, slow growth of themes, dadaist intersplicings, discrete melodic phrases and colorful atmospheric sonics emphasized over melodic structure and lyrics, etc etc -- this record I have believed to be the Dark Side of the Moon of 90's DJ mixes. It really deserves an uninterrupted vinyl spin in darkness, but a youtube play gives the gist. Holds up against time, which is rare for trance from that era.

### Jack Kruse — 2015-07-06T00:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-171015>

A teaching lesson about numbers: Numbers explain physics better via colors. Colors is all about frequency. Colors for me and hopefully for this group is not about what we see it is about what they are.........packets of vibrational energy. **Consider red light: has a frequency of 436,803,079,680,000 oscillations per second. _That equates to the same energy in a high C note in music_.** That note has an oscillation frequency of 4,096 osc. per sec. Xrays, microwaves, cosmic and gamma rays all have these numbers and colors tied to them.........but when you read them they make no sense to you........but color has a qualia you do get. Your mitochondria in your retina are where your personal magnetic sense is and why this result is present in your test, so the picture you posted has meaning.........to you.

You know what it means to me? Your magnetic sense is no bueno. Do I have a blog on that sense? Yep. Tensegrity 5. Magnetism has the HIGHEST oscillatory rate we know of in nature. Few people know this. It is 18,446,744,073,709,551,616 oscillations per second.........

It is hard for the mind to grasp that many things vibrating that many times in a second........But guess what last week on the cruise.....everyone else did there own thing in Playa Del Carmen on the cruise........and What did I make sure to do? I went into tthe crater where those Oscillations have existed for 65 million yrs.........Patrick Griffin asked me how I expended all the energy I did every day and night on the cruise and not get tired or discharge my mammalian battery. What did I do to prepare? I remained ketotic and at once and day and drank a ton of water and ate only DHA at night........why? Because that water assimilated all those oscillations from the crater to renew me.........I knew I could give a lot.......because I was going to get a lot more in my time in the crater.

For those of you bad at math: That number above is 18 quintillion, 446 quadrillion, 744 trillion, 73 billion, 709 million, 551 thousand, 6 hundred, and 16 oscillations per second of vibrational energy. That my dear is physics 101. Get some for those eye clocks............

### Gagnrad — 2015-07-25T18:56:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-172984>

How about something very upbeat and cheerful?

December, 1963:

According to Wikipedia, this was originally called 1933 and was about the repeal of Prohibition, but "the lyrics were changed at the urgings of Frankie Valli and lyricist Parker to reposition the song as a nostalgic remembrance of a young man's first affair with a woman, and, more specifically, Gaudio's courtship with his wife, Judy Parker"

The next two are are a more evolved musician, who has looked with rage and turned to the joyful rhythms of reggae

Michael Franti & Spearhead - Everyone Deserves Music 2003

Michael Franti & Spearhead - The Sound of Sunshine 2010

It was too late

It's too late
She's gone too far
She's lost the sun

Came the time to realize
And it was too late

She's come undun
She didn't know what she was headed for
And when I found what she was headed for
Mama, it was too late

It's too late
She's gone too far
She's lost the sun
She's come undun

She's lost the sun

She's come undun

It's too late
She's gone too far
She's lost the sun

And when I found what she was headed for

Life is a waterfall

We lose ourselves

### Jake Tassell — 2017-09-18T13:20:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-223893>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=105134)
>
> With all this talk about Pink Floyd .... I would like to buy some .... but which ones? which album?
>
> This must sound really lame....
>
> I gather - after a visit to iTunes ..... that a song can be on different albums and have different treatments???
>
> Don't laugh ...... I have had my head buried in Julia Child et all for the last century ...... I have never owned an ipod - preferring live music.
>
> Where should I start? ..... and if you laugh at me - I will know .....

I don't think anyone needs help with their 'musical education'. I really think that's mistake number one.

_You_ know what you like. _You_ know what resonates well with you. If that turns out to be Jim Reeves, Liberace, or The Goombay Dance Band, and everyone laughs at you because of it, so be it, say I.

Why force someone else's square peg idea of what they consider to be 'good music' on yourself.

I personally hate Pink Floyd, but I defend anyone else's right to like Pink Floyd, and I ain't gonna be anyone's enemy _because_ they like Pink Floyd.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-18T20:43:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-223918>

Children of the light.......behold the gods of sonic waves.

Lyrics
Oh, let the sun beat down upon my face
And stars fill my dream
I'm a traveler of both time and space
To be where I have been
To sit with elders of the gentle race
This world has seldom seen
They talk of days for which they sit and wait
All will be revealed

Talk in song from tongues of lilting grace
Sounds caress my ear
And not a word I heard could I relate
The story was quite clear

Oh, baby, I been blind
Oh, yeah, mama, there ain't no denyin'
Oh, ooh yes, I been blind
Mama, mama, ain't no denyin', no denyin'

All I see turns to brown
As the sun burns the ground
And my eyes fill with sand
As I scan this wasted land
Try to find, try to find the way I feel

Oh, pilot of the storm who leaves no trace
Like sorts inside a dream
Leave the path that led me to that place
Yellow desert stream
Like Shangri-la beneath the summer moon
I will return again
As the dust that floats finds you
We're moving through Kashmir

Oh, father of the four winds fill my sails
Cross the sea of years
With no provision but an open face
Along the straits of fear
Oh, when I want, when I'm on my way, yeah
And my feet wear my fickle way to stay

Ooh, yeah yeah, oh, yeah yeah,
But I'm down oh, yeah yeah, oh, yeah
Yeah, but I'm down, so down
Ooh, my baby, oh, my baby
Let me take you there
Come on, oh let me take you there
Let me take you there

### Jack Kruse — 2017-09-19T01:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-223951>

> [Billybats said:](/goto/post?id=223936)
>
> Reeeeaaallly.. I didn't listen to the whole song, didn't understand it. Okay will listen to the whole thing. Thank yooooooou.

The song is about never giving up........giving life No Quarter. Giving your all even when things look grim.

No Quarter lyrics:
Close the door, put out the light.
No, they won't be home tonight.
The snow falls hard and don't you know?
The winds of Thor are blowing cold.
They're wearing steel that's bright and true
They carry news that must get through, oooh
They choose the path where no-one goes.
They hold no quarter, They hold no quarter.
Walking side by side with death
The devil mocks their every step
The snow drives back the foot that's slow
The dogs of doom are howling more
They carry news that must get through
To build a dream for me and you
They choose the path where no-one goes.
They hold no quarter, They ask no quarter.
The pain, the pain without quarter.
They ask no quarter.
Yeah! Without quarter, quarter, yeah!
The dogs of doom are howling more!
I hear the dogs of doom are howling more!

### Da-mo — 2018-01-11T16:37:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-232244>

So . . . . what was the great song of 2017?

My 2nd daughter reckons it was Despacito

but being older and wiser I would place Despacito alongside feel=good songs like the Macarena. these songs and the videos that go with them capture the joy of life and the feeling of being alive. All my children are in their twenties looking forward to the best years of their lives . . . . . . oh to be able to put an old head on young shoulders . . . . . if only to dispel the invincibility of youth
Most of us here (not all) are looking to perpetuate, recover or project that feeling. We are older, we have issues, we have heath problems to solve and we have an urge to fill what time we have left with a quality of life. We want to heal and thrive like we once did without squandering the energy we have like we did in our youth.

For me the song of the last year just has to be RagnBone Man's Human. I challenge you to find anyone who does not know every word of this song and who will not remember it in 10 years time.

Give yourself a beak. You're only human after all . . . . .

### Da-mo — 2018-01-11T17:11:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-232251>

BTW,Happy New Year everyone!!! I wish for you all that the year brings you what you need - which is not necessarily what you want. So on a musical note . . . .

I''m not a Stones fan but . . . . if you try sometimes . . . . you just might find . . . . . you get what you need . . . . .

### Da-mo — 2018-01-11T17:40:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-232257>

About once each year I convince my son to sit in the sun with me and play chess with a few beers and if he doesn't take over the sounds I put this on for background music . . .

I have a special chess set for this with a board made of sandblasted etched glass and glass chess pieces. I will bequeath it to him one day when I pass from this world. I think I will place a copy of this album with it.

### Da-mo — 2018-01-11T18:08:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-232264>

Next month I am going to see Queen Live with Adam Lambert. I cannot think of anyone else who could stand in for Freddie. Dat boy got some serious pipes!!!

### Da-mo — 2018-01-11T18:56:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-232271>

On my 49th birthday in April 2017 my son picked up a guitar and started playing with it. Some sort of structure formed and he quietly started singing the first verse of a song. At the chorus he momentarily stopped playing the guitar and gestured for people to join in - his sisters (my daughters) joined in the vocal. Totally spontaneous and it was the best birthday present I ever got by FAAAAAARRRR! It wasn't a song they planned to sing for me and I dont think they even sang it to me - but seeing the bond between them in that moment made my whole life worthwhile. This is the song they sang together as brother and sisters ..... it still brings a tear to my eye just recalling that special moment. . ,

Mate, it would not be fair for me to compare. I carry a heavy negative bias with respect to John Farnham. I acknowledge the virtuosity of his voice, but I can't bring myself to like it.

My ex-Mother-in-law is a devoted fan of both he, and Celine Dion - as in, "all day every day on repeat" devoted. It may be some catastrophic pairing of the two of them, but they both now send all of the wrong kinds of chill up my spine.

Coming down

And wishing you were here tonight is like holding on
But I still get to see your face, right?
And that's like nothing they can take, right?
So there won't be no feeling in the firelight
Hoping this will be right
Time to show your worth, child
I remember when her heart broke over stubborn shit
That's no way to be living, kid
The angel of death is ruthless
And I'm always thinking
Summertime with the bikes out
Pushing our luck getting wiped out
Days with nothing, but laughing loud
Underneath my coat, when you tap my shoulder, hold my hand
Nights with nothing, but dark in there
You could be my armour then
Island smiles and cardigans
The nights that we've been drinking in
We're here to help you kill all of this hurt that you've been harboring
Confessions should be better planned
Alone that night, I'm surely damned
Run away and I'll understand it
What's important is this evening I will not forget
Purple, blue, orange, red
These colors of feeling
Give me love, I'll put my heart in it
And I think about it all the time
Lights went out, you were fine
You kinda struggled not to shine
I still love you though
I still love you though
I still love you, always
So hold me when I'm home
Keep the evenings long
Crack and break and part ways
I still love you though
I still love you though
I still love you always
So hold me when I'm home
Keep the evenings long
Let's not crack and break and part ways
And I wonder if I can let her down
Somewhere between psychotic and iconic
Somewhere between I want it and I got it
Somewhere between I'm sober and I'm lifted
Somewhere between a mistress and commitment
But I stay down
Girl, I always stay down
Get down, never lay down
Promise her break everybody off
Before I break down
Everyone just wait now
So much on my plate now
People I believed in
They don't even show their face now
What they got to say now?
Nothing they can say now
Nothing really changed
But still they look at me away now
What more can I say now?
What more can I say now?
You might feel like nothing was the same
And I still been drinking on the low
Mobbing on the low
Fucking on the low
Smoking on the low
And I still been plotting on the low
Scheming on the low
Furthest thing from perfect
Like everyone I know, yeah
And I hate that you don't think I belong to you
Just too busy running this to run home to you
You know the paper my passion
Bittersweet celebrations
I know I can't change what happened
I can't help it, I can't help it
I was young and I was selfish
I made every woman feel like she were mine and no one else's
Now you hate me, stop pretending
Stop fronting, I can't take it
Girl, don't treat me like a stranger
Girl, you know I've seen you naked
Girl, you know that I remember
Don't be a pretender
Getting high at the condo
That's when it all comes together
You know I stay reminiscing
The makeup sex is tradition
But you've been missing girl
You might feel like nothing was the same
And I've still been drinking on the low
Mobbing on the low
Fucking on the low
Smoking on the low
And I've still been plotting on the low
Scheming on the low
Furthest thing from perfect
And I wonder if I can let her down

\----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
**Greek words for love**

-   _[Agápe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agape)_ (ἀγάπη _agápē_) means "love: esp. charity; the love of God for man and of man for a good God.

-   _[Éros](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ancient_Greek_eros)_ (ἔρως _érōs_) means "love, mostly of the sexual passion.

-   _[Philia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philia)_ (φιλία _philía_) means "affectionate regard, friendship", **usually "between equals".**
-   _[Storge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Storge)_ (στοργή _storgē_) means "love, affection" and "especially of parents and children".

\---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
**Restoration of Peter**
Two words used for "love"

.

### 5G Canary — 2019-07-30T22:35:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-275211>

My husband has been listening to “Human” by Rag’n’Bone Man over and over lately. While I love that song too- I have been consistently drawn to his other song appropriately named “Skin.” He wrote it about a deep love that could have been if they hadn’t been from to different worlds.

It has so many double meanings I don’t think the artist even realizes what he wrote. The video and song are beautifully done. To me the song is about what a love affair with Mother Nature might have been like if we hadn’t been forced to live in an artificial world.... causing us to live in these two different worlds.

As he sings...”cause it was almost love....it was almost love.” It definitely reminds me that “it’s not almost”- I still have a choice now. I CHOOSE the LOVE that should have been all along.... I still have “Skin” in the game NOW.

We have a choice and we don’t have to choose these lyrics....

“We bleed ourselves in vain
How tragic is this game?
Turn around, I'm holding on to someone
But the love is gone
Carrying the load, with wings that feel like stone
Knowing that we nearly fell so far now
It's hard to tell
Yeah we came so close, it was almost love
It was almost love, it was almost love
When I heard that sound
When the walls came down
I was thinking about you
About you
When my skin grows old
When my breath runs cold
I'll be thinking about you
About you”

### JoeBranca — 2020-01-08T20:20:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-284344>

Sometimes with music, the story of the creative process is just as beneficial as the actual listening experience. In this case, the artist released an experimental electronic-orchestral album, and the name is Everything You're Searching For is On the Other Side of Fear. Fitting theme to launch me into 2020 as I anticipate the changes and upheaval that comes, not really knowing what the other side will look like. Missteps, challenges, opportunities, growth.

With this particular piece in the youtube clip, I'm impressed with how he did it. Essentially the outcome is as much a discovery for the artist as any listener.

"This was composed in a very unique way - I wrote 4 bars at a time; never listening to the previous 4. By doing this in this fashion (and coming back to it every now and again) it has this unique feeling of contributing to blossom into something new and unexpected (even by me and I wrote it!).
.
"I do these kinds of compositional “thought experiments” as apart of my daily routine and rarely do they amount to something that feels this magical."​

### 5G Canary — 2020-06-03T17:42:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-289996>

[This song has always resonated with me but in a completely different way recently. Most of us can “talk without speaking and hear without listening” ... we all actually have this ability but very few of us use it. We have many senses and it’s about time we start using and trusting in them. We are all connecting whether we acknowledge it or not. Our connection is stronger in our current environment.... zip code matters not just in the environmental way but also in the energy, frequency, vibration and gravity of those in that zip code. This song sung differently resonates the cries of innocence lost in the dark shadows... a sick world that silently has been calling out to us. Silently awakening us to their songs. But most of us are distracted or have kept it silent and like in the lyrics “silence like a cancer grows”.... it’s definitely _Time_ _to_ _Collectively_ make choices to _Change_ our thinking. To choose our thoughts wisely... for the greater good of all. What if it was that easy.... to collectively be silent no more! To Collectively Choose _Love_....](https://youtu.be/u9Dg-g7t2l4)

### 5G Canary — 2020-10-09T19:04:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-292774>

[@John Schumacher](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/23768/)
Thanks for sharing your favorites too! Kashmir.... I’ve always loved this song too!

**Kashmir** is the northwestern region of the Indian subcontinent. It is also called and known as the Heaven on Earth because of its natural widespread beauty. Until the mid-19th century, the term **Kashmir** geographically denoted only the valley between the Great Himalayas and the Pir Panjal mountain range.

**_Oh, let the sun beat down upon my face

And stars fill my dream
I’m a traveler of both time and space
To be where I have been
To sit with elders of the gentle race
This world has seldom seen
They talk of days for which they sit and wait
All will be revealed_**

Robert Plant once said, “I wish we were remembered for Kashmir [more than Stairway To Heaven](https://auralcrave.com/en/2018/07/15/stairway-to-heaven-why-led-zeppelins-masterpiece-is-actually-a-personal-growth-lesson/)”. I wonder why? I always felt like Robert Plant was literally “**_Planting_**”... planting seeds through his music and collectively we are water those seeds.

According to folk etymology, the **name** "**Kashmir**" means "desiccated land" (from the Sanskrit: Ka = water and shimeera = desiccate). In the Rajatarangini, a history of **Kashmir** written by Kalhana in the mid-12th century, it is stated that the valley of **Kashmir** was formerly a lake.“ Coincidence “**_Plant_**” and “**_Water_**?” The song was brilliantly orchestrated and continues to grow....

Yes’s song.....Sounds like “Yes” you served in the military... thank you for your service! Love the line in the song....” Listen, should we fight forever Knowing as we do know fear destroys?” Fear is the real virus and Love is the cure. When they get us to live in fear we live our lives backwards. Live mirrored is Evil.... fear is the enemy in which we must fight against.

> [View attachment 16738](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/16738/)

[@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/) -> _Thank you for your thoughtful response !_
I was fortunate, my name was not pulled during the draft. I was spared. War, hatred, revenge are not in my practice. I've sought after: collaboration (rather than competition), love (rather than dissension), forgiveness (rather than retaliation). I see the divine has weaved a functional path for us to find. I'm currently writing a book. Here's a clip:

Water instructs us best in understanding why _Nature_ designed molecular collaboration. It shows us that water is preoccupied with extensive self-association. Water clathrates pull its positive electrons in and stratifies out its negative electrons. Nature reveals both a push toward surface tension and a cohesive pull, creating a beautiful spherical form. This gives water properties for hydration and solubility, yet it can repel “almost hold at bay” molecules like lipid hydrocarbons, _fatty acids,_ without losing its innate characteristics.

Question: Can we humans learn from water our need for collaborative interdependancy?

Looking Forward...
John

### Dan2 — 2020-12-05T09:21:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-293670>

> [Chantelle said:](/goto/post?id=293668)
>
> This made my day. I love finding new music...

I was really excited finding that band too! I like finding music from countries other than the USA/UK/Canada. And when it's from the '60s onward a lot of the bands were influenced by popular pop and rock bands from the US and England, but they also have the influences of the music from their countries, so it's an interesting combination.

And sometimes funny

This one's hilarious

\_\_\_

There are record labels that put together albums that are compilations of songs from various bands (and full albums by one band too). It's a good way to find new songs and bands.

Soundway
[https://soundwayrecords.bandcamp.com/](https://soundwayrecords.bandcamp.com/)
[https://soundwayrecords.com/](https://soundwayrecords.com/)

Strut

The least obscure recent funk band

### Dan2 — 2020-12-06T15:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-293696>

I was listening to his albums and got to that song you posted and thought yeah this is one of the best ones. The chord progression and arrangement sounds like lots of songs from that era but it's good when the little details come together and sound just right, you know. And I like the lyrics being in another language because it adds wonder while I feel like I still understand it some.

I skipped through all of his '70s albums.
(I took the "[https://www](https://www)." out of the links below so it doesn't slow the page down auto-loading too many videos.)

02 Ma E' Un Canto Brasileiro
youtube.com/watch?v=Nj2R2CXJr9k&list=PLbIN9DHtOfK-OC9LDl0ofjVwHMupJm3Rc&index=2
1:35 sounds Os Mutantes-y. And then the contrast with the electric David Bowie-sounding part after 3:00 and it builds like it's going into a heavy chorus but then instead back into the softness. I like how that complements.

05\. Confusione
youtube.com/watch?v=Y3AfI061swo&list=PLbIN9DHtOfK85uoGgjiPGTr3mh7zLt65N&index=5
At 3:05 sounds like a synth cat. They only do that one more time at 4:17. I love finding those moments, like the whistles in Cumbia Que Te Vas de Ronda ([https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...h-my-music-education.9297/page-45#post-292732](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/page-45#post-292732)).

04\. Umanamente Uomo IL Sogno
youtube.com/watch?v=IQQa6OZ8EWs&list=PLbIN9DHtOfK9k9fFdjcC0FSkC8uCHNAjt&index=4
I like the whistling in this too. And the whistling and humming being a little out of tune sometimes. The whistling melody at 2:34 is beautiful. That part's perfect.
I've been relistening to this one lots of times. This one's my favorite. Something about the whistling melodies. This is a good find. Up there with the Os Mutantes 1968 version of Baby.
I like the soft sound of other songs on that album, too -- "Umanamente uomo: il sogno".

A short (0:52) fun one
Gli uomini celesti (Ripresa)
youtube.com/watch?v=ClLwbSdp0nQ

7 e 40
youtube.com/watch?v=biyV3A3YwnU&list=PLcj4QD8DvJio416GvWsZoFfe5Yu2IxOVu&index=2
1:11 - "Presto presto yeeeaaahhh"; put that on loop for 30 seconds straight over a scene of people triumphantly making dinner fast and you got a golden ticket commercial for a pre-made pasta sauce company, like art should be for.

This one's silly -- Maledetto Gatto
youtube.com/watch?v=B8tGUVVScng&list=PLbIN9DHtOfK9iqJzHcGYaUAqZZm5yDSUu&index=7

\--->> I found the song you posted on an album in English. 5:06

I did some more Bandcamp digging.

This is an interesting album. Very weirdly fun. Traditional Central and South American musical styles mixed with psychedelics and electronic effects makes some interesting genre, but I don't know much about it, what its called and the nuances of subgenres. So I don't know what this would be called more specifically than Colombian Psychedelic? Mayans on LSD?

Meridian Brothers - Desesperanza
[https://meridianbrothers.bandcamp.com/album/desesperanza](https://meridianbrothers.bandcamp.com/album/desesperanza)

Something about those simple traditional styles mixed with weird playful electronic effects complement each other so well. It's like alien kids songs.

### Johan Lindøm — 2021-09-24T15:33:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-304169>

\[QUOTE = "Sean Waters, post: 300999, member: 19464"\] We need to go back to the traditional basics man ...... Literature, Live Theater, Dancing, Sports, Farming .... got to get the fuck off these devices. \[/ QUOTE\]
True.
I played a little guitar once, I should play a little again. Music is not like in the old days, as it once was. Spotify, MP3 is not like an LP player, etc. The technology has taken some of the nuances out of music. Made it flatter in a way. Music was often played at 432 Hz, but that is no longer the case, most of the frequencies have been changed and adapted to a "better" technical standard ... frequencies that are easier for the technology to use.
[https://open.spotify.com/playlist/5AOaEQcJpV6ACLD7Ou5uCC](https://open.spotify.com/playlist/5AOaEQcJpV6ACLD7Ou5uCC)
Well, I'm not the expert on this, but possibly an area we can increase quality of life?

### Dan2 — 2021-09-28T02:09:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-304321>

> [Johan Lindstrøm said:](/goto/post?id=304312)
>
> Anyone have any info??
> { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

The notes opera singers sing are affected by the reference pitch and tuning intonation system. Some pitches are more stressful to the singer's body when using opera technique and some pitches are easier to sing with good technique. The reasoning explaining it gets into the microcosm macrocosm ideas about the human body and the solar system. So maybe when the solar system changes then opera singers can sing better at new pitches, but apparently opera singers have been preferring some pitches more than others since the Renaissance, so I guess the solar system changes slowly. And the pitches being stressful or easier has a lot to do with the intensity of the technique control and volume of opera singers; someone singing jazz or blues or something with more casual technique could sing at A440 for a hundred years and it's not gonna damage their voice, but an opera singer might notice damage to the clarity and control of their voice quickly enough to end their career early. But if the resonance of some pitches and harmonics can harm the physiology of the opera singer because of the intensity of the resonance, maybe a rock singer's voice being amplified and standing in front of the PA would affect their voice quickly (still doesn't matter much though, unless they're singing in a hair metal band where they want to sound like opera technique, because they can just sing with a new voice -- like Bob Dylan's voice. An opera singer's technique and voice is like a representative of an ideal body (especially when it's a fat Italian girl), and so the differences affect their role as a singer and whether they're considered a technically good opera singer of that voice type. If Dylan's ideal is how he's heard, I guess okay, sure, Bob, chain smoke and get in a motorcycle accident and play and sing at any reference pitch you want to and it still sounds good. Or like Mick Jagger on Monkey Man -- be careful about the pitches around your register shifts while making the monkey sounds, Mick; you don't want to sound unprofessional.)

Look at these maybe in this order, or to get your attention the video first.

[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/rev\_tuning\_hist.html](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/rev_tuning_hist.html)

[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/fid\_91-96/fid\_911\_jbt\_tune.html](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/fid_91-96/fid_911_jbt_tune.html)

So when middle C is 256 hz, the A can be a different frequency depending what the temperament, intonation system is -- equal temperament, just intonation, Pythagorean etc. With equal temperament, when C's 256, the A's about 430.5 if I remember right.

First she sings at A440 (I think, or some high pitch around there). C256 singing starts at 7:08. The way she breathes in at 9:04 is so good. And 9:49 and 11:19. The way her face and eyes open up at 11:22 onward is beautiful. She's like a cute baby being overwhelmed with herself, with beauty. Such a cutie. From 11:19 on, God, it's so beautiful. I'm in awe of her.

More about reference pitch and opera singers' voices:

[https://larouchepub.com/eiw/public/1988/eirv15n06-19880205/eirv15n06-19880205\_025-renata\_tebaldi.pdf](https://larouchepub.com/eiw/public/1988/eirv15n06-19880205/eirv15n06-19880205_025-renata_tebaldi.pdf)

[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/rev\_chart.html](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/rev_chart.html)

[https://larouchepub.com/eiw/public/...0812\_024-revive\_verdis\_tuning\_to\_bring\_ba.pdf](https://larouchepub.com/eiw/public/1988/eirv15n32-19880812/eirv15n32-19880812_024-revive_verdis_tuning_to_bring_ba.pdf)

More videos:
[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/2007/historic\_videos.html#960529](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/2007/historic_videos.html#960529)

A bunch of other links:
[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/articles.html](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/articles.html)
[https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/revolution.html](https://r.schillerinstitute.org/music/revolution.html)

Jack has talked about this somewhere here....

I have read that autistic children will stop and listen when music is played at it's natural resonance.

There was a woman on the forum \[Sarah\] whose husband is a musician and played pubs etc. He found when he played at natural resonance people would stop chatting and listen.

Natural resonance is much more calming. I definitely know this to be true for me.

### JoeBranca — 2023-05-09T18:01:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-321139>

Since the Tetragrammaton podcast I was reminded how much Rick Rubin is an inspiring human being to me. He facilitates and gets the pieces and players in place to produce something new or fresh. The podcast episode, his initiative to bring together the melanin/POMC world to the neuroplasticity world, is the latest example. The earliest example of him doing this had an impact on me. Actually bigger than I realized, as I'm thinking about it fresh decades later.

When I was 10 or 11, a song on the radio was the only one that provoked me enough to go down to the record store and buy an album for the first time in my life. It was that Aerosmith/Run DMC collab, back around 85 or 86. That was Rick's doing. He proactively brought together two worlds that pop culture at the time said don't go together. My impression is that Rick dug into the 70s archives for a forgotten hard rock hit, and meanwhile he saw the brilliance of the emerging hip hop sounds, and knew they should come together, almost like they could complete each other in some way.

The specific genius in my opinion was Rick either consciously or instinctively knew that the hip hop overhaul of the Aerosmith song would bring to the forefront the essential underlying groove of that sort of classic rock. It was something people felt but didn't really notice underneath all the distorted guitar and screaming, and it got lost in the post disco 80s era. But RR came along and said, No its not a bygone era this is what makes this music so cool, and you can find it everywhere.

All that to say, it wasn't just a song experiment, it was a statement a breakthrough in the "silo" music industry. For the attentive, moments like that really can shape your own ability to perceive differently, be receptive to new ideas no matter the source.

The music video as goofy as it is, really highlights this notion -- the compartmentalized worlds with a wall between them. Only when both sides are provoked enough by each other is there a breakthrough and meeting of minds.

### caroline — 2023-05-09T18:18:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-321140>

G
Great perception
I feel incredibly lucky to be experiencing all this

### Jack Kruse — 2023-05-12T23:38:18+0300

### NikkiYork — 2023-07-27T20:59:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-need-help-with-my-music-education.9297/post-324370>

Watch the movie "the wall" It's great. I mean, it's probably more catering to an angsty kid in puberty, but it should still be good. I need to rewatch it. I was a scared to paralysis adolescent, and for me it was great.

I saw Pink Floyd live when i was 16 , and i didn't like it as much, as I love it in the right setting. It can be very overwhelming, in a good way, and i would say the track depends really on the situation.
I have had one of the best talks and encounters at the 11:11 clock in the middle of the desert at 11;11h, 2010. and they were playing "time". it was at Burning Man, and we traveled out there on our bikes into the unknown, to find a group of people, huddling around that clock, waiting for it to be 11;11. But we didn't know that.

The stars were so bright, and a bunch of random people discussed their view about the meaning of life and where humanity will go. The amount of wisdom shared was beyond what i can describe... and there sure is a reason, why people who went to Burning Man once, keep going every year.

The best show I saw in my live was David Bowie in a very small venue In Germany. The stars literally aligned for me to be there, and he played for 3.5hours to honor the time he lived in Germany. I was standing pretty much face to face with him. What a genius, and he certainly burned the candle from both ends... and should he have met Dr. Jack to reset his POMC pathways.

Last year i decided to let go of all the old musicians who I loved when I was younger. just because i liked them then, doesn't mean they are making my live fuller now.

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-09T13:18:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-105145>

Iâ€™ve been biohacking for years (I just didnâ€™t call it that). In the UK we canâ€™t get the tests done like you can in the US and elsewhere, and (apart from an acute emergency room visit), Iâ€™ve not consulted a doctor for 16 years. Two of my three children are unvaccinated (my first went down with ear infections after the first 2 vaccinations). Went under the conventional wisdom radar after my third child was born and we refused to vaccinate yet again. I think he was born with adrenal fatigue, (he developed problems which I later found out was triggered by fluoride), and Iâ€™m convinced that vaccination at that time would have been a complete disaster.

Iâ€™m a strong believer that if you opt out of one system, you canâ€™t just do nothing â€¦.. so Iâ€™ve trained in a lot of complementary therapies, some very fringe â€¦â€¦ (Radionics, iridology â€¦..) plus complex homeopathy, nutrition, colour therapy. Iâ€™ve also attended seminars on the autistic spectrum, hyperactivity etc. My son is in his teens, and fine, and that we have reached this point with him is truly a moment of grace. (Iâ€™d better say here that my kids have had access to the medical profession if necessary, but on those fortunately rare occasions my DH would usually be the one to take them until they gave up raising the vaccination issue). I now practice alternative therapies, very part time, using what Iâ€™ve learned.

I had followed CW for years before having my son, and Iâ€™ve been connecting dots, mainly on my own, since then. Iâ€™m interested in the chart of homotoxicology as a symptom tracker, biometrics, epigenetics, bioresonance, the power of the mind to influence physiology (mental CT anyoneâ€¦..?) The JK blogs have spotlighted more dots, and Iâ€™m making more connections.

Before reading the blogs and bringing in some of the ideas, I was already healthier at 56 than I was at 40, but I knew I was missing a couple of important dots â€¦. The BAB, no eating after 7, wifi off at night, a few cold packs and cold showers, lots of iodine rich foods and â€¦â€¦ lanula appearing on my fingernails, particularly the right hand, bit of body composition change, and this morning I noticed in the shower that Iâ€™m growing hair again on my lower legs and under arms, again particularly on the right side.

So Iâ€™ve taken the plunge and started a journal to keep me on track.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-09T16:36:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-105163>

Hi Sue. I haven't done mental CT yet, but sometimes when I have difficulty laying down in the icewater, I imagine the bright sunlight, so strong I have to squint my eyes. Then I seem to just relax, and then I can get my upper back wet. In order to relax in other situations, perhaps I should try imagining strong sunlight instead of the typical ocean shore.

I know when I was new to CT, as I was preparing my bath, I would start to heat up. Perhaps I have done mental CT after all.

### Josh — 2014-01-09T18:12:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-105178>

Beware the DIY approach with no labs. It is worth figuring out a few labs to track some basics. For example even knowing the size of your red blood cells will tell you if you have B12 issues that probably point to methylation, etc.. I have studied and practiced all of the above, and without labs, one can miss important stuff. THat being said there is a lot of value in using them together and seeing what can be confirmed with labs. While the NHS may not play along, there are other resources in the UK if you can pay out of pocket. One could always take a quick trip to the East for some labs. They are cheap and very good in India for example.

### caroline — 2014-01-09T22:36:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-105219>

Welcome Sue ..... I look forward to following along with your journal.

I keep saying over and over\[ad nauseum!\] that you never know when someone else will say something that rings a bell for you .... well - you just did that for me!

I was sitting at the beach the other day and happen to look down at my legs - I was horrified to see hair I hadn't noticed! I haven't needed to shave my legs for years! Mind you it is very lite and soft - so not offensve!

I don't know how long it took me to notice that the outer third of my eyebrows had grown back!

When new people ask about a time frame - it is so hard to explain .....it isn't really relevant ....we just keep growing and learning ... and doing better and better

One thing Dr Barry says about thyroid tests is that it shows the blood levels, not what is going on in the tissues. For example euthyroid hypometabolism is where there are normal thyroid function tests, but the tissues are unable to respond properly. I know how you feel about being in the queue ....Iâ€™m looking at dementia avoidance (grandmother, and now my mother heading that direction), plus my father died of liver cancer following the removal of his stomach due to cancer. I wasnâ€™t breastfed (Mum had an op on her breasts (cysts) before I was born), I have biometric indicators of being prone to obesity and diabetes. Iodine appears to be my next key â€¦â€¦ Low stomach acid can cause absorption problems, and iodine is needed to make stomach acid â€¦. (and gastric acid is needed to help release B12 bound in protein foods). My lanula starting to show once I upped the iodine rich food intakeâ€¦..

Mumâ€™s cysts, she started going deaf in her 50â€™s â€¦. Dadâ€™s health history (his previous digestive problems point to low stomach acid). I was prescribed iron when I was pregnant, when I think iodine would have been better. Third child very sensitive to fluoride â€¦. In utero the foetus can provide mother with thyroid hormones and adrenal hormones â€¦â€¦ Connecting dots â€¦â€¦..

### Grmoline — 2014-01-24T16:38:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-106912>

You may have solved this since your earlier posts...... I've had the same issue with finding places to get labs done in the UK without having to go through a doctor. Check out bloodtests.com. You have to pay, but I've been able to get some of the biohacking tests through them. York tests often have a sale for things like homocysteine (cost me Â£79 the last time). Other saliva and urine tests (e.g.., cortisol, DHEA, melatonin, steroid hormones, etc.) can be done at US labs through a naturopath or FDN practitioner over here. It's pretty pricey to test that often, but it will at least provide a baseline and then you know what you are working with.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-02T12:00:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-107879>

The decision is made, the wheels are in motion - we are moving! Should be out by the end of March, hopefully earlier, leaving the house to be sold after we move into temporary accommodation - 2 mobile homes put in a T shape configuration, joined by a decking walkway. Kitchen/dining area and a relaxing area for son to study in one, plus his bedroom and bathroom area. Will use the kitchen area in the other to put in a washing machine, it has a master bedroom plus a spare bedroom and a nice cosy living area. The back area behind is completely secluded, so no problem sunbathing ... Only drawback is no bath, only showers so plan is to put an old bathtub outside and use a hosepipe ... (listened to the CT webinar yesterday so well motivated!) There is also a well ..... Good location in terms of EMF.

We are getting rid of about 90% of our possessions, as we don't want the hassle of trying to store things we might never need when we do move to somewhere permanent. Will be doing this experiment for a minimum of 6 months (because not paying commercial house rent or mortgage for 6 months covers the outlay of the move). After that we will see - if we love it (or the health benefits) we can stay there longer, if it doesn't work out we can take our time finding somewhere to live without the hassle of also trying to sell a house...

### caroline — 2014-02-02T12:15:57+0200

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-03T13:21:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-108081>

Decided yesterday that as I currently have a bath, it would be stupid not to try and get a bit of immersion CT in before the move (been doing showers)... If I'm not prepared to do it now, DH has enough to do without me adding an outside bathroom to the specifications  So ran the bath with cold, thinking would do it without ice first time, stepped in ....... had to get out before I even sat down, the water was so cold my feet really hurt ... a real get out quick hurt .... Note to self: last session was in late summer/early autumn, don't expect the same experience in February ..... Found the bath thermometer, not working, so winged it and put a bit of hot in, then tried again ... It was cold but not painful .... Did about 30 minutes, was cold for a couple of hours afterwards, and right hand ring finger numb, but otherwise OK.

Think I will find the outside bath a better option, as I'll be able to look at the stars etc ... (boredom a big factor now, although I did put a comedy on my ipad in the bathroom yesterday, I was watching it to keep me in the bath for 30 minutes, not because I particularly wanted to watch it). Gonna stick with it though, as I do want an outside bath when we move ..... It will probably go near to a nearby barn wall, so I'll tell DH the bad news is I still want an outside bath, the good news is that we don't need the barn wall tiled

### sooperb — 2014-02-03T14:33:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-108085>

I am so envious that your OH seems committed too, mine is still poo poohing EMF, now saying he can't eat a full English every day (not that we do) because he isn't losing weight either and he's never going to accept that we should get rid of wifi, start opening the bedroom window at night, turning off anything electrical and CT? He'd never do it. He has given up carbs mostly, happy to eat lots of meat/fish and green veg. (although he moans if we have broccoli too often). I can do a lot of things on my own but he'd never relocate as he likes the "village", the facilities, everything on hand, plus he's doing a job that requires him to live locally, at least in the county. So great that yours is happy to help you along. Sounds idyllic.

I was wondering if it would be possible to meditate in a cold bath? I bought an infrared thermometer from Amazon for about a tenner, it seems to work quite well even though it might be a few degrees out up or down.

An outside bath would be awesome Sue .... especially one built for two

Did you see the podcast where Dr. K. talks about his meditation room??? very, very cool - especially if you have a partner in crime!

I love, love, love, what you are doing .... my idea of how to live life!

Live large! and take no prisoners.....

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-04T20:28:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-108312>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=108085)
>
> I am so envious that your OH seems committed too, mine is still poo poohing EMF, now saying he can't eat a full English every day (not that we do) because he isn't losing weight either and he's never going to accept that we should get rid of wifi, start opening the bedroom window at night, turning off anything electrical and CT? He'd never do it. He has given up carbs mostly, happy to eat lots of meat/fish and green veg. (although he moans if we have broccoli too often). I can do a lot of things on my own but he'd never relocate as he likes the "village", the facilities, everything on hand, plus he's doing a job that requires him to live locally, at least in the county. So great that yours is happy to help you along. Sounds idyllic.
>
> I was wondering if it would be possible to meditate in a cold bath? I bought an infrared thermometer from Amazon for about a tenner, it seems to work quite well even though it might be a few degrees out up or down.

Him being on board with what I’m doing, fortunately he’s seen benefits such as my sleeping better means that I’m not permanently knackered or moaning that he snores …Turning the wifi off at night is a small price to pay for the drop in earache he gets from me! No contest!!

He’s not on board for CT at all yet – mainly I think because when we met I had Raynauds (amongst other problems) – and had numb fingers and toes , and uncontrollable shivering sent me to bed …. Having worked hard to overcome that, he is concerned that I’m inducing the same symptoms .

He’s also easily lost some weight without trying, and by not eating late has reduced snoring a lot and no more sleep apnoea (on my now occasional sleepless night I can really hear a difference)…. and his energy levels are better. At my worst sleep wise I would sometimes go to the other end of the house and I could still hear him snoring ….. Other times I would lie there awake and there would be huge snores and then …… silence ………….. and then after a gap a huge snort as he drew breath again ……

Meals wise, we eat the same main meal but otherwise tend to fix our own, I don’t eat lunch often now, and we rarely want the same breakfast … He prefers pork chops to the traditional English breakfast I go for, and I often eat seafood which he never wants at breakfast time. He also usually eats breakfast before me (a throwback from when I was so tired I would snatch some extra sleep when he got up). For the main meal we’ve sort of got a “one pot extra” rule…. If he’s cooking a curry, he will put on an extra pan of veg so that I avoid the rice….. If I’m cooking I’ll do rice or potatoes for him if he particularly wants it, but it’s mainly sweet potato or root veg. But the basic meal is the same. A throw back from when we first married, I was vegetarian, he was a meat eating beef farmer … we just agreed then not to force dietary guilt on each other and so the one extra pot rule started then, and is just used slightly differently now that we both eat meat….

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-04T20:35:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-108315>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=108214)
>
> An outside bath would be awesome Sue .... especially one built for two
>
> Did you see the podcast where Dr. K. talks about his meditation room??? very, very cool - especially if you have a partner in crime!

I occasionally teach meditation, including once teaching someone who was starting a meditation group in a prison …. I’ve meditated during childbirth, but had to stop because the labour stopped …. …. (I mentally put myself on another planet to take a break from the pain, without realising the labour had stopped, told the midwife where I was when she asked what was happening – she said can you come back to earth please you are supposed to be giving birth, so I brought my mind back and the labour set off again ……).

I find it much easier to meditate outside, so maybe when I have my outside bath, I can put my mind elsewhere whilst the cold does its job

### sooperb — 2014-02-05T18:43:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-108493>

Sounds good  I find I'm doing a lot more of the cooking lol, he gets what he gets but as we both pretty much enjoy the same foods it isn't too hard. I can sympathise about the snoring and I recognise the sleep apnoea. Many's the time I have lain there worried at the silence in case he'd died lol. He's nowhere near as bad as he was, it's some nights rather than most nights. Funny, I eat a handful of prawns most mornings, I don't bother to offer them as I know he wouldn't be interested. Fortunately he's been happy enough to give up carbs apart from the occasional lapse so it isn't an issue. He's not keen on kale though so we tend not to have it. I buy it and add it to stuff he's not having but I can rarely get through it all before it goes over.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-08T17:47:45+0200

Just got delivery of 3 litres of the well water from where we are moving to, it was pumped straight into glass jugs. Ice cold and more - ish.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-11T20:16:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-109665>

The water is a revelation.  Its spring water, and I drank the 3 litres on Saturday and had a gallon on Sunday, Monday and heading that way today too. I'm craving it, and even drinking it later in the evening I don't need to get up in the night .... I usually dislike drinking water, and finish up making excuses to disguise it in tea  but I even rinsed out a couple of empty wine bottles and filled them with the water and took them to work, and rushed home for more because it wasn't enough. Amazed at the difference its making.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-23T12:09:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-111781>

Well it’s been a weird couple of weeks , I’m sad … but not depressed  Letting go of most of my possessions, furniture and pictures up until now, next it’s my books ….  Probably some detox going on as well.

I had to stop CT – couldn’t cope with it after working in a cold basement and drinking all the cold spring water, I was getting my old Raynauds symptoms back without getting into the tub … But that has now passed, and I’ve bought my outside bath for when we move next month…

Wish I’d done the water intake thing first …. That has been 100 per cent worth it, because it makes everything else so much easier. I thought I was hydrated when I obviously haven’t been, and actually having the mental focus at the same time as the physical energy is something I’d forgotten and not experienced for a long time. So it’s a score for hydropower,  and now I’ve got hydration better, it’s time to head for the FIR sauna and then on to moonlight and stars CT.

One of the interesting things for me is that it has changed my thinking about food ….. Not only about what I eat now, but how I shop. It isn’t the end of the world if the fridge looks empty, I have started buying bits as and when we need them, instead of filling it up “just in case”. I’ve got four cupboards for food here, and there will be a lot less storage when I move, so after buying less generally recently, I decided to sort the cupboards out and properly run down the stock. I had no idea how much food I had, when I was constantly thinking about food I bought far too much, and was unconsciously squirrelling it away ….. I even found 11 bars of dark chocolate (85%).  Sorting the freezers now too …..

I’ve now experienced that the right hydration can change your thoughts and behaviour, and even if my body composition hasn’t caught up yet, I am now shopping and storing food like a woman without a weight problem.

### caroline — 2014-02-23T12:38:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-111785>

I think it is great how you are making it all work ......it isn't an all or nothing deal ...you are so good at figuring out what works for you .....

### sooperb — 2014-02-24T17:02:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-111934>

I must admit to a smidgen of envy, I wish my OH was open to moving even to a more sympathetic house or location but he's dead set against it. It is good how you're thinking it all through and making the adjustments as you go along.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-27T12:28:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-112427>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=111934)
>
> I must admit to a smidgen of envy, I wish my OH was open to moving even to a more sympathetic house or location but he's dead set against it. It is good how you're thinking it all through and making the adjustments as you go along.

My OH has been open to the idea for over 20 years. We married 13 weeks after our first date, and it was agreed then. He built our house, on land originally owned by his grandparents, the house was being finished when we met, and from the word go I knew that I wouldn't want to live here into retirement. He agreed to be open to a change when the time was right, so we sold my house. I've lived over 20 years in a house I wouldn't have picked, (and next to my FIL and BIL which has had its problems) and it’s too big for us now. OH sees the logic – we worked out how many hours a month less he would need to work if we had a smaller house. He also knows that if we stay here he will have to spend a lot of his eventual retirement maintaining a property that is already too big. If he does that, then we won’t have the time or the money to pursue other interests, like travel. He also doesn't want any of our children feeling they "ought" to live here, something he went through, having a mentally disabled brother. Although my BIL has since died his father is refusing to move to a more age appropriate home, and is living in a house that is too much for him, with land he can’t cope with. OH is taking on more responsibility there now.

We've also called time on this house because I think he is in danger of working himself into the ground if he tries to do it all, and it was what we agreed all those years ago. It’s also about what happens when one of us go upstairs, if I go first he is adamant that history won’t repeat itself with our kids having to support him to live here. So fortunately although it is for different reasons, we are both on the same page

### sooperb — 2014-02-27T12:39:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-112430>

Sounds just wonderful to have such concord between you. We moved into our present home on a "needs must" basis and I've disliked it ever since. We tried to move in the first six months but property was through the floor so we stayed put. Then when hubby retired the first time, he decided to invest his lump sum in an extension. It's made the house bigger but the layout is all wrong. He likes it  He especially likes the location as he says we have everything within reach, doctors, supermarket, library, pretty much all we need and within walking distance for when we are old!!!!! I can't change his mind at all. The house is ok, next to farmland and has a brook running alongside but it's not the style or age I would prefer. He says there's nothing wrong with it and in one sense he's right, but I still would move given the chance.

I'm not sure what it is about some old folk wanting to stick in a house that is too big, too cold, to difficult/expensive to maintain. I used to work in Social Work and saw many who refused to budge, I don't know if it's a security thing or a fear of change but on the odd occasion when we have moved someone from a draughty, cold, dilapidated property into a warm, comfortable, well maintained flat where everything is on the same level, they have loved it once they've settled. They make new friends because they are only along the corridor and they can socialise or not as they choose. You can take your memories with you, you don't have to stay in one place to reminisce about good times etc. Once you've moved, maybe FIL will have a change of heart if he's left on his own.

### Sue-UK — 2014-02-27T21:24:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-112524>

Soo, I don’t think I will ever want to live long term in a house I own again, I think I want the flexibility of renting, maybe investing our equity in this house in property to rent to other people. Still invest in property, but without the emotional attachment that could hold us back later in life!

We’ll still be close to FIL, and that in itself is a conscious decision. We think he shouldn’t be driving now, and because we are close enough to support him at the moment he doesn’t. But that is a recent development – he needs to get used to not driving, and then hopefully let the car go when it comes up for MOT/tax etc. If we are not close enough I reckon he would start again, and we would be left with trying to convince him to stop, or else involve his doctor against his wishes.

Other update – the second mobile home was delivered yesterday, Ground needs to dry out a bit before it can be put in place alongside the other one, but another step closer.

I’ve become a bit overwhelmed with information ….. studying the Fourth Phase of Water, and Physics in Mind….. Trying to keep up with the recordings within the 24 hour window …. (greatly appreciate the opportunity) And working my way through the redox blog over and over again, then getting sidetracked into other blogs …… This morning found me with the window wide open, leaning out trying to get some natural daylight first thing, whilst listening to the final 20 minutes of the Evan Brand interview …

I’ve put the main points of the Redox RX blog into an order that I can try and work through, and its highlighting where I am slipping ….. hopefully it will help me get back on track ….

1\. Use reverse osmosis water, [spring water](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B0069FTP0G/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B0069FTP0G&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20), or [mineral water](http://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B005HG9ERW/ref=as_li_qf_sp_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&camp=1789&creative=9325&creativeASIN=B005HG9ERW&linkCode=as2&tag=jackru-20) for your drinking water consistently. **Spring water** i**n place. My ceramic water dispenser is no longer being used as a door stop**

2.A constant source of high quality marine/animals protein is needed to maintain your redox potential. **In place, but maybe need a few more swaps for more seafood. That’s drifted a bit.
**
3\. Lowering stress is a great way to improve your redox potential. **Working on – move will help! Working on a specific meditation plan.
**
4\. If your redox level is low you should avoid all alcohol no matter what. You should also avoid black and red tea and salt because both are loaded with fluoride salts. **Next big one to work on!! Alcohol and salt no problem, tea is the biggie, I’ve cut down but need to cut it out ……**
5\. Meyer cocktails, HBO therapy, and infrared therapy will improve your redox potential. As **I’m improving hydration, FIR sauna use is booked in for March.
**
6\. Be barefoot as much as possible when walking upon the earth or concrete. **Easier when I move or when the ground dries out, whichever comes first! But when I move the first job of the day will be to walk barefoot over the field to the well to collect the water…..
**
7\. Avoid all unnecessary non native EMFs. **Ongoing work in progress, still too much computer use.
**
8\. Pay attention to sunsets and dim light at night. **Doing a bit more of, amber glasses and putting red light in through wrist before sleep (using a Bioptron hand held device that I’ve already got). When moved I plan to go back to the well at sunset to collect more water……..
**
9\. The Fournier effect using warm and cold water – **move will mean I can have hot spring water in an outside bath. (Just put in a change of spec that the bath be set up so that we can light a fire under it, got one of those looks …. )
**
10**.** Physiologic ketosis improves your redox potential as well. **Need to think about**, **not in ketosis.
**
11.Cold Thermogenesis improves your redox potential because it improves the flow of electrons on the surface of water and proteins without any energy costs. **Move will make easier, Fournier effect appeals more but when I am CTing under the stars it will hopefully get better!
**
12\. [Consider a Magnetico sleep pad](http://jackkruse.com/magnetico/) if you can afford it. – **least do-able, so put on the back burner for now.
**
So it’s now on record – I’ll see at the end of March what’s changed

### Sue-UK — 2014-03-06T14:10:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-113757>

I keep dropping by and trying to read as many posts as I can, but I'm struggling to keep up with everything Not as well as I have been, and I'm spending longer online than I should be selling our stuff.... Things seem to be dragging about the move, but if I think about it, we're still on schedule, but the weather means its not being done in the order we originally thought.

The first mobile home was already there, so its just taking out what we don't want and reflooring it now, rather than later as we thought. The second mobile home is now on the land but not put in place, its a week after its arrival and the ground is still too wet to move it, and the lorry delivering the crushed stone for underneath it couldn't get close enough without its wheels spinning in the mud, so although its near, its an extra job to get it where it is supposed to be ...

On the plus side  the delay has meant I listened to the EMF webinar recording this morning, and have discussed the electrical spec to this second mobile home  (I was actually able to explain about the risks of it being much smaller rooms, dirty electricity etc ) I now know that we'll be able to separately switch off sockets, lights and the heaters in this second home, and as that's where me and DH will be sleeping, they can all go off at night, and we can use a battery operated alarm clock, and a torch. If it mega freezes the heaters might need to be on low, but I reckon that's not likely to be much of an issue until next winter, and by then the log burning stove should be in ......

### sooperb — 2014-03-06T18:35:22+0200

### Sue-UK — 2014-03-13T10:27:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-115147>

Wasn’t fully recovering from being ill (had food poisoning from a really stupid food decision), and then worked out I was using my normal work computer a lot more than usual to sell our possessions ….. And the feelings of anxiety, gut in knots, and generally feeling worse seemed to follow the extended sessions…. I’m not anxious about moving, so didn’t get it …… Was tempted to throw supplements down my gullet but as an experiment I’ve gone back to the 3 legged stool…… Water, check ….. Light not perfect but better ….. so EMF maybe? Found my EMF sensor in the drawer at work (used it years ago to demonstrate the fields emitted by microwave ovens and tvs etc….) and tested around the computer I’ve been using for work … Way too high at the seat, with printers, microphone and speakers on. It was the microphone and 2 speakers (particularly the speaker with the off /on switch and volume, even when it was switched off) that was way up. They’ve gone now (will use my I pad at a 4 ft distance minimum to listen to webinars and speak to my brother on sky pe). The wifi is too close to the work computer, but the device isn’t good at RS detection (checked online and someone said don’t use it for that as a lot of RS devices are outside its range). So I’m assuming that the wifi is a major issue too when it’s on during the day, but although I can’t get a decimal point figure of how close to zero the device now shows for other EMFs, with the printers off, it’s down at the low end at least.

Working in that EMF with a wifi topping hasn’t been doing me any good, now I’ve got a lower EMF figure with a wifi topping, still not good but getting there ….. And of course it’s a change of spec for the move …. No problem from himself though – although it’s probably not ideal as a piece of test equipment, a gadget with flashing lights and beeping is worth 10,000 words ….  Got him to test before and after removing the speakers and microphone ….

I’d typed the previous bit in draft …. And then before I could post it events developed dramatically yesterday …. My FIL collapsed, ambulance….. would have been a helicopter to hospital but too foggy …. Fortunately he managed to press his alarm switch which automatically dials our number, if he hadn’t pressed it .... The ambulance crew were amazing, and the hospital too, emergency treatment at its best.  So yesterday was a lot of waiting, lots of EMFs and stress, but when we had to come home to collect some personal items for him, I just went out into the garden and did 10 minutes barefoot on the grass …… Won’t know FIL’s prognosis until later today, but when we left last night he was feeling brighter and he’s a fighter

### Sue-UK — 2014-04-01T16:51:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-119230>

Long story short, FIL had a couple of heart attacks in the ambulance, body filled with fluid (DH had taken him to the GP twice in the previous couple of weeks because of severe breathlessness, had a blood test for anaemia, prescribed iron, but no other physical checks). He spent 10 days in hospital being stabilised, his cantankerous gene expressing itself more and more as the days went by. Because he is stabilised he appears to be much better, but his heart is now weaker than before.

After agreeing to a social services assessment, at the last minute he flatly refused any help, telling the hospital he can manage on his own (he can’t) and that anyway we would be living close by…… I got very close to abandoning our project.... . But boundaries are in place (I did suggest a moat and a drawbridge as a change of spec).

After an interesting conversation with a senior OT in the hospital, she’s made me realise that if he has no experience of struggling, he does not need to get outside help in, and is less likely to be open to moving to somewhere more suitable. She implied we have been enabling him, and I agree we’ve sleepwalked into that over the last 5 years, since his last major health scare.

As his breathing is better and whilst he is coping better at the moment, we have gone back to giving him the same support as he had before, but no more. DH has agreed to take me away for a long weekend on our own sometime in May, and FIL has agreed in principle to outside support if we are ever away, and if he changes his mind then that is his choice, we are going anyway. If that happens we will move as soon as the house is sold, and use the mobile homes for the odd weekend.

We got two weeks behind with the move plans, but we’ve done it at last, although a few of the important bits (like the landline so that we don’t have to keep mobile phones on at night because we are both emergency contacts) will be two weeks late, and there’s still things to move and things to get rid of… Until the new phone line goes in we are going to spend a couple of hours a day here doing the internet stuff and getting the place ready to sell.

Meals and my water intake weren’t ideal with the hospital visits and the stress, so I’ve been depressed and still feel yuk at the moment. I haven’t posted on the forum for a while but have been looking in and reading what I can, thanks all for the gems. I bought the optimal reset special and downloaded some of the bonus webinars to MP3, which have been a real boost.

The basics should all be moved in by tomorrow, so I intend to start afresh and make the most of opportunity I have, and when I wake up I will walk barefoot across the field to collect my spring water and then have a BAB……. And I’ve signed up for gold membership. Going to do 6 months as a gold member as a present to myself ….. Onwards and upwards ……

### nonchalant — 2014-04-02T05:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-119355>

Nice, Sue. Glad you are getting back to where you want to be.

### caroline — 2014-04-03T09:06:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-119575>

Hi Sue.....sounds like everything is coming together. Walking barefoot across a field to get your water sounds awesome!

### Sue-UK — 2014-05-01T14:11:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-124255>

Well almost another month has gone by …… FIL went downhill after a couple of weeks at home and a routine blood test showed excess potassium in the bloodstream and dehydration, so after 5 hours in A and E on a Friday night he had another 12 days in hospital being stabilized … He had periods of confusion, thought he was going home when he wasn’t, that his bed had moved when it hadn’t, and thought things were his that weren’t … (we had to post back the false teeth …….). The hospital medically advised him to go into a nursing home.

In the hospital they were drawing bloods and tweaking his meds daily, and he’s now back under the care of his GP, and won’t get the same level of monitoring. Having been out of the hospital a week he’s coming to the end of the "honeymoon period" but is insisting he will not go into a nursing home ….. In hospital he did agree to carers coming in twice a day for 6 weeks, and then tried to get out of that as soon as we brought him home …… I told him he was ignoring medical advice and that if he wouldn’t accept interim help from carers for himself then he would have to suck it up and do it for us, as a family we were going under, we were stressed, physically and emotionally exhausted and had lost income with going backwards and forwards to hospital etc instead of earning our living and getting our house on the market. We need time to gather our strength and avoid going broke. It was tough love, he didn’t like it but didn’t argue, I think he just intended to turn the carers away …. But on day 1 DH made sure he was there to let the carer in and got all the forms completed to get him into the system, FIL was frosty and wouldn’t let them do anything for him, which carried on for the first week until they turned up yesterday and wanted to withdraw the whole service. He’s gone down to once a day now, but after another conversation with him yesterday he did let the carer wash and cream his feet and legs last night ….

So the house isn’t yet ready for sale, the mobile homes aren’t set up properly …. We’ve moved in anyway, just doing the best we can ….. We have 5 more weeks grace while the carers are booked to get as much done as we can, first priority being getting the house on the market.

Still doing the well water, and have upped the barefoot walking, even when the ground is sodden from the torrential rain. It’s colder at night, moonlight and starlight and no artificial light whilst I sleep …. What should have been a giant leap has become stops then baby steps …… but we're finally making a bit of headway with FIL's care and at least I’m not going backwards.

Bought some lamb heart …. Trying a bit raw will be well out of my comfort zone, but that’s the next challenge.

### sooperb — 2014-05-01T19:12:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-124365>

Our elders can be soooo difficult, seen it many times. I used to work in a hospital social work team so I've met many older folk who are stubborn and refuse help, it's mostly because becoming frail frightens them and reminds them of their own mortality. Sometimes they agree a trial and realise that the carers are helping them to stay independent, sometimes they refuse until the message hits home that they really can't manage. Then there's the question of payment after the first six weeks is up, having got the service free they don't see why they should pay. It's a difficult time for all concerned, with luck he will understand that they can help him to stay at home if he cooperates.

Does he have a personal alarm to push if he needs assistance outside of the care visits? There is such a lot of help that can be accessed but if he's resistant it may take time for him to agree. Men seem to be worse than women at accepting help, maybe they see it as a sign of weakness or maybe they are just very private and find personal care very intrusive. I wish you luck with him, it's obviously taking its toll on you both.

### Sue-UK — 2014-05-02T18:27:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-124626>

> [sooperb said:](/goto/post?id=124365)
>
