I used to work in a hospital social work team so I've met many older folk who are stubborn and refuse help, it's mostly because becoming frail frightens them and reminds them of their own mortality. Sometimes they agree a trial and realise that the carers are helping them to stay independent, sometimes they refuse until the message hits home that they really can't manage. Then there's the question of payment after the first six weeks is up, having got the service free they don't see why they should pay. It's a difficult time for all concerned, with luck he will understand that they can help him to stay at home if he cooperates.
>
> Does he have a personal alarm to push if he needs assistance outside of the care visits? There is such a lot of help that can be accessed but if he's resistant it may take time for him to agree. Men seem to be worse than women at accepting help, maybe they see it as a sign of weakness or maybe they are just very private and find personal care very intrusive. I wish you luck with him, it's obviously taking its toll on you both.

Thanks Soo,

He has an alarm round his neck that connects to our number, then DH mobile, my mobile etc, moving to the next number if there's no reply. It saved him on the morning of his heart attack, had a couple of false alarms since when its been pressed accidentally, . DH taking him to the doctor later, so we'll see how he's doing and then maybe talk to him again about future care.

Breakfast this morning I cut up some raw lamb heart into small enough pieces I could swallow whole and then just went for it .... Then had some cooked with some mushrooms and my seaweed sprinkles, which tasted much better. Thinking maybe gradually increase the raw and reduce the cooked, have made a start anyway.

### Sue-UK — 2014-05-13T17:05:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-126653>

Sleeping in the mobile home has made such a difference. We still don't have our house on the market but I'm not remotely tempted to sleep there, despite the rain and the mud here.....I've been experimenting with raw seafood (oyster vs scallops thread) and one of the oyster insights  has been why I feel so much better here. The house was built to save energy, extra thick lined walls etc but apart from what it kept in, it also kept so much out ..... The mobile home stands about 2 foot above the ground, and although it seems stable, there's actually some movement which varies with the weather. I can hear the birds better when they start at dawn, I like hearing the rain on the roof .... I feel like I'm getting information from the environment, instead of sleeping in a sealed box. .....

### nonchalant — 2014-05-13T18:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-126679>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=126653)
>
> Sleeping in the mobile home has made such a difference. We still don't have our house on the market but I'm not remotely tempted to sleep there, despite the rain and the mud here.....I've been experimenting with raw seafood (oyster vs scallops thread) and one of the oyster insights  has been why I feel so much better here. The house was built to save energy, extra thick lined walls etc but apart from what it kept in, it also kept so much out ..... The mobile home stands about 2 foot above the ground, and although it seems stable, there's actually some movement which varies with the weather. I can hear the birds better when they start at dawn, I like hearing the rain on the roof .... I feel like I'm getting information from the environment, instead of sleeping in a sealed box. .....

I agree about the environmental information, Sue. I like hearing the wind in the trees, the birds, the rain. You were talking about earthing in mud -- that is the greatest! Much better contact with the earth. It always makes me grin. Just have a pan of water near the house that you can step into and rub your feet together to clean off before going inside. I wish I could hide the rubber boots DH sometimes wears outside.

### sooperb — 2014-05-13T22:56:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-126733>

I loved holidaying in a caravan, the sound of the rain on the roof, the feeling of almost embryonic cosiness, countryside all round from every window (well mostly) and the air, so much fresher than in a centrally heated house. I'd live in one given the chance, or a narrowboat, well appointed of course lol.

### Sue-UK — 2014-05-18T11:06:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-127442>

This week the weather is much better ..... And after my seafood overload last weekend I've not been hungry, so have followed my gut, and some days have just had one meal. To compensate I just made sure I went outside for an electron and photon breakfast  And usually managed an evening snack of the same. I've walked barefoot as much as possible.

When osf 3 was published I read it on the screen, re read it a couple more times, I got it but it wasn't sticking. I felt I understood the main points but I couldnt verbalise it to anyone else .... I knew how important it was so I printed it out and tried again..... Then got a notepad and pen and started extracting information and writing it down under different headings... Much slower than typing it, but then "copy" typing wouldn't have given me the same understanding ..... I only write down what I understand, I have to think about it, and writing it down helps me remember ....Every time I re read the blog i uncover a bit more .... Think I've spent at least 10 hours on it so far  but I've loved it and the effort is sooooooo worth it. I will type it up eventually so that I can add to it as the blogs come out .... And of course theres the existing ones ..... With the speed I'm working at I think I need to capture a lot more electrons ......

### nonchalant — 2014-05-19T03:38:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-127499>

I haven't read any blogs on the computer screen for a long time. I just underline and write in the margins, and reread and write more in the margins. I have difficulty with the hyperlinks, if Jack doesn't say "read that hyperlink you just zoomed by" I often miss them.

### Sue-UK — 2014-07-11T19:48:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-133971>

Still on board  but FIL had yet another month in hospital, and now in a nursing home for convalescence. Its been draining, but it didn't seem right to offload it all here... I was able to conserve energy by saying nothing, whilst regularly looking in. Things much easier now he's nearer to home.

Also had 2 bouts of food poisoning with raw oyster smoothies, and managed to fall down the wet caravan steps, fortunately just bruised. Just had to look at the setbacks in terms of electrons (poor decisions).......

I've not once wanted to give up my experiment ..... Maybe I haven't lost the weight I wanted, maybe I've not earned much, the house isn't on sale yet, maybe we haven't managed to set things up like we planned, but the basics are in place while we support FIL. We will do what we can for him without going under ourselves .... Back to electrons again

I've got to a peaceful point ......observing my FIL from an electron loss perspective, recognising when I have become depleted after visiting, I go back to the 3 legged stool ..... And family members are noticing the benefits .... And I can explain the science behind it, so less resistance and more openness to thinking about things in a different way

I've created a space to go through the study guides (great additional resource ), the blogs and the webinars. Just going to soak it all up for 6 months and see what it leads to. Everything I thought before is in need of a mental overhaul, so I'm accepting the hand I've been dealt as a gift .... I'm taking an educational sabbatical

### HoneyChild — 2014-07-11T22:15:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-133978>

Sue, are you still buying the frozen oysters? I've just finished my first kilo by having 5 oysters a day and so far there hasn't been a problem. I thought that the process of freezing killed off anything that could make you sick.

### Sue-UK — 2014-07-11T23:41:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sue-uk-journal-biohacking-without-labs.9298/post-133987>

> [HoneyChild said:](/goto/post?id=133978)
>
> Sue, are you still buying the frozen oysters? I've just finished my first kilo by having 5 oysters a day and so far there hasn't been a problem. I thought that the process of freezing killed off anything that could make you sick.

I thought that for parasites, wondering about any bacteria if an off one got into the bag ..... But I was struggling with them repeating for hours, was increasing the amount, so it may just have been my gut couldn't handle them ....

I'm not going to risk this source raw again, just gone back to 1 cooked, did it today with garlic butter and Parmesan. The butter and Parmesan was nice, still not a fan of oysters but will keep at it  Cooked didn't seem to repeat. Prefer holding my nose and just knocking back a raw smoothie to swallowing lumps of cooked oyster, but can't risk it at the moment.

### Butters — 2014-01-09T15:48:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105156>

For a few days now I have almost all day long cold feet and hands. I surprisingly need socks when I go to bed under the covers to get warm feet, this is new. Haven't had it before. What can I do against it? Hands and arms are cold for I think 2 months now, since I started with the ketogenic approach of nutrition. On the skin are little red/violet lines on my upper arms and knuckles of my hands and back of my hands are violet/blue often.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105171>

I too have had similar issue - and think its related to limited grounding, and access to sunlight. I've also had increase in leg cramps at night as well. so electron chain transport isn't running at full steam. Cold is good, but you need to amplify grounding. I'm ground less now that I was 2-3 mths ago due to workload. I'm trying to figure out how to do the nasal red light to increase my photoelectric effect....

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-09T17:47:29+0200

### Josh — 2014-01-09T17:55:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105175>

Mine have improved since I started putting m,y hands and feet into the CT tank for at least part of the time, even a few seconds seems to help. My tank is in the 30's right now. Also of course getting my thyroid meds and cofactors online has helped as well. IT seems that the combo of the two has pretty much solved it for me.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-09T17:59:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105176>

Mine does improve in cold water; my CT for the past few days has been me standing in the tub w/my feet in cold water. I do know my thyroid is going crazy right now. I feel like we need to back off my meds right now... guess we'll see what the labs say.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-09T18:24:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105179>

I was in the pond on Christmas and New years day, but the minus 25 temps have curtailed any CT outside other than just BEING outside. So I was trying something different....my feet in hot water in the tub and totally cold shower on the rest of me...a kind of Fournier effect??? And good for Reynaud's training....actually my hands and feet are much warmer this winter, so Butters, it may be a longer term process for you. Last winter I had a lot of trouble with them turning white with any exposure to cold.
I agree with Josh that thyroid is a big thing to work on.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-09T19:50:17+0200

So I was trying something different....**my feet in hot water in the tub and totally cold shower on the rest of me...a kind of Fournier effect?**?? **And good for Reynaud's training....actually my hands and feet are much warmer this winter**, so Butters, it may be a longer term process for you. Last winter I had a lot of trouble with them turning white with any exposure to cold.
> I agree with Josh that thyroid is a big thing to work on.

great tip. I wonder if what I've got going on w/my feet isn't a form of reynauds. they're cold/numb but when they warm up they get red, hot and itchy. cold always makes them feel better. but what's interesting warm/hot water doesn't bother them, but warm air does. I'm still puzzling this one out.

### Hemming — 2014-01-09T20:19:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105201>

[http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/...-schoenfeld-discuss-your-personal-paleo-code/](http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/shownotes/9248/763-guest-host-chris-kresser-and-laura-schoenfeld-discuss-your-personal-paleo-code/) don't let the header discourage you, the podcast is about problems on a low-carb diet.

### cantweight — 2014-01-09T20:25:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105205>

My son and I had issues with this a few years ago. Freezing hands and feet, blue finger nails, a very lacy pattern of veins showing. We took Nattokinase. I guess any fibrinolytic food would do the trick....perhaps a raw sauerkraut recipe that includes onions?
Docs thoughts were that the blood was bit over coagulated for various reasons( i.e. detox). Thick blood would certainly cause circulation issues.

### cinnamon — 2014-01-10T02:18:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105235>

A couple of weeks ago I followed a link kindly provided by one of our forum members. The link went to a site that discussed something called the Buteyko method. Buteyko was a Russian doctor who noticed that we breathe deeper and faster as we get sicker. Here's the site I'm referring to:

[http://www.normalbreathing.com/](http://www.normalbreathing.com/)

There is a ton of info on this site and I am still working my way through it, but the reason I'm mentioning it now is because of this exercise: [4-D How to warm up cold hands and feet](http://www.normalbreathing.com/learn-4D-Emerg-Proc-Sleep.php)

I have been doing this exercise for a few minutes each night after getting into bed. I've always suffered from cold feet and this has worked like a charm.

Maybe it's worth a try?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-10T02:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-feet-and-hands.9299/post-105241>

breathing is a huge resonant issue......no doubt

Molecules of dietary origin can have powerful, even disease eradicating, effects.

After receiving Dr Kruse's e-cookbook, what impressed me first was the list of ingredients which have a huge potential for direct and indirect synergies.

The recipes are replete with the basis for food compounds which would provide support for substantial preventative and therapeutic impact.

This is truly an "optimal" cookbook.

I have had a chance to try a recipe or two. Wow ! They taste even better than the photos look.

I have always been a coffee "nut". I have tried countless coffees and coffee recipes. Well, Dr Kruse's "Optimal Coffee" recipe takes the cake !
And the ingredients !....Optimal !... Bravo !

I recommend the Optimal e-cookbook wholeheartedly ! Build your health...don't miss this jewel !

# Thread: EMF Frying me - Correlated with BREATHING WATER VAPOR???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-frying-me-correlated-with-breathing-water-vapor.9305/>

# Thread: MCHC 1 Gene, Viruses and Gut Microbiota
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josh — 2014-01-10T02:55:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105246>

**MHC 1 Gene, Viruses and Gut Microbiota**

If Gut "leakiness" is the driver of epigenetic expression and it is mediated by the microbiota, wouldn't an environmental/seasonal mismatch with microbiota from the environment be a big factor??? Perhaps this is one reason people feel better in some places at some times, etc.? If the microbiota let too few or too many viruses in then we have problems. Probably the viruses control the epigenetics of the microbiota as well or at least partially. More to come as it bubbles up...Thought I would bring this over here from the dialogue that started in my journal. Consider BG2 and EE7 as Jack sugggested along with the new post.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T04:30:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105256>

Jack explains that if you eat locally, you will have the proper gut bacteria to digest the local food well. I have to wonder if bacteria on pollen and such in the air, as well as on the food we eat and the surfaces we touch, contribute to gut flora. So whenever the radishes ripen, we already have a good-sized population of microbes that love radishes.

Traveling might pose a risk, since our microbes are (hopefully) tuned to our home area, but we are now eating foreign food that doesn't agree with our gut population. Perhaps shaking hands with lots of natives is the solution?

### Josh — 2014-01-10T04:31:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105257>

So it has to be that the gut microbiota sense the outside environment through the electrons and protons in the food coming down our digestive tract or into our respiratory tree or both. This must tell them where they/we are, what season it is, etc.. In addition they may respond/react to the altered electromagnetics just as we do. Can the ones deep in our guts get messages through quorum sensing from organisms living on the skin or in the mouth where they have direct exposure to the outside environment? A human body must have a limit of metastability that our microbiome directly and or indirectly contributes to in a conscious/unconscious=survival manner? Clearly there is a point where are current microbiota becomes a mismatch with an environment that changes too fast and or frequently and we lose some of our epigenetic responsiveness through loss of optimal gut permeability and I imagine a whole lot more of the subtle commensal arrangements with our little friends.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T04:39:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105258>

I think the microbiota drive some of our food choices or cravings.

### Josh — 2014-01-10T05:53:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105260>

It is proven that they affect our metabolism in many ways. My question is what is the limit of "entropy" that a given system can maintain? Are the microbiota affected by environmental in the same way as we are at the metabolic level? Do they become dehydrated as we do? All of these are questions I would ask about my outside garden in a given climate, so I am starting to stretch this to the walking talking garden that I call my body in a more nuanced fashion.

### caroline — 2014-01-10T06:02:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105261>

This is why Dr. k. Said to eat low carb all year in OZ ... The donut hole is affecting our water supply and our food supply.
So - I assume we can offset that somewhat by being outdoors as much as possible in our long lite cycles?

Or are we able to offset it at all? Maybe not ....that is why we need to drink water from the Northern Hemisphere.

So what is sitting out in the sun doing for me? ...maybe nothing. I am hedging my bets and sitting on the grass under a conifer and blissing out on the sound of the ocean and the waves and children's laughter.

Would direct sun be a negative? I think ....maybe yes.....

I am hoping that we aren't totally screwed......

### caroline — 2014-01-10T06:06:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105262>

Why am I craving fruit that is in season right now? Doesn't my body know better? Would my body not Sense the mismatch?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-10T15:05:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105278>

no one is totally screwed........you just need to up your game like I mentioned in Asprey Podcast. You need to do more than others do

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-10T17:07:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105281>

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T17:24:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105284>

> [Josh said:](/goto/post?id=105260)
>
> It is proven that they affect our metabolism in many ways. My question is what is the limit of "entropy" that a given system can maintain? Are the microbiota affected by environmental in the same way as we are at the metabolic level? Do they become dehydrated as we do? All of these are questions I would ask about my outside garden in a given climate, so I am starting to stretch this to the walking talking garden that I call my body in a more nuanced fashion.

Absoutely. I would think our microbiota are definitely affected by the environment. The gut contents are considered to be outside our body, with the gut lining functioning as a skin. Our red blood cells shrink when exposed to a smart meter for a few seconds, and I imagine our flora aren't too happy about it either.

### Jonathin — 2014-01-10T18:41:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105291>

Mutualism is the interrelationship between organisms - macro and micro. There are tens of thousands of microbial species in the soil and especially prolific in the root zone. Up to hundreds of species on plant leaves. Plants feed the microbes. Microbes feed the plant. Not unlike the gut. This is a co-dependant dynamic ecology that responds and adjusts to all the energetic drivers from the sun, earth, atmosphere, emfs - native and non-native as well as the balance of mineral nutrients. I enjoy eating a carrot out of the garden after simply brushing the soil off with my finger.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T19:57:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105296>

I would imagine that the more highly developed the species, the more it is dependent upon the other various organisms that travel with it. Our DNA has many things turned off, in order to optimize energy for longevity and brain function. So we are dependent on others for various vitamins and proteins and such, instead of making them endogenously.

It would be fascinating to see a live Cro-Magnon person. They had truly optimal energy resources, at least according to their brain size. Were they telepathic? What songs did they sing? How often did they eat? Probably often enough to satisfy their gut microbes/parasites, and their cell microbes -- mitochondria.

### Inger — 2014-01-10T21:19:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105305>

> I enjoy eating a carrot out of the garden after simply brushing the soil off with my finger.

I do this too Jonathin. I actually found some wild herbs in the garden today... with some dirt on it (earth) and I ate it.. oh man I wanted more! I have to laugh it is funny I am grassing beside my sheep, we are all with our heads down looking for greens hehe if someone would see they would surely think I were out of my mind! But those wild greens picked from the ground have something magic with them, don't know what it is but something that I love

### Josh — 2014-01-11T01:10:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105328>

Back to the MHC 1 Gene (not MCHC-typo). I reread what I could find in the blog and am left wondering how directly we trade immune function for cognitive function? Dr. Kruse suggests strongly that our cognitive development is in many ways a byproduct of our immune development and that we have to survive at the immune level as an organism first. However, we have now evolved to where we can easily override this built in preference for physical survival and think/do ourselves to an early and uncomfortable death. Looks like it...Until now, I have seen this as a choice based on larger energy and metabolic considerations. Now, it looks more as though our cognitive abilities simply override our immune priorites. Does this mean we need to de-evolve the brain to where it listens to the immune system again?

I am also left wondering why helminths and fungi are not regularly included with bacteria and viruses when considering the gut microbiome and evolution? They are both essential parts of terrestrial and marine microbiomes where they occupy multiple trophic levels. Based on what I learned about how many systems helminths can affect in the human body and the overlapping benefits to other microorganisms, I cannot help but wonder if they may have a more significant role in the overall body ecology? Is it accurate to attribute our evolution to mostly the viral and bacterial interactions in the gut when helminths and fungi play important roles in the ecosystem that produces the mutations and transmigrations of the bacteria and viruses?

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-11T03:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mchc-1-gene-viruses-and-gut-microbiota.9306/post-105335>

you do not trade it.......the immune system evolved first......neurogenesis second. Following the energy equivalence of how things are powered in an atom the valence shell or last shell is most active chemically when energy is involved.......so it is congruent that neurogenesis or cognition and the immune system are the two systems that go awry first when energy is altered in humans. Worms are important for gut quorum sensing.

# Thread: Grounding @ work

**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### sjoshua — 2014-01-10T20:26:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-work.9307/post-105297>

I did some searching, but didn't notice a topic directly related to this... but it seems like it would be a fairly significant factor, since most office workers are at their desks ~8 hours/day... and these days offices mean lots of cubicles with... lots of smart phones and wifi signals bouncing around...

Does anyone ground themselves while at work every day? If so, how do you do it?

I was thinking an easy way would be to buy a 3-prong (or grounded equivalent, in your country) extension cord, then splicing the wires and connecting myself to it with a grounding strap around my ankle ([http://www.amazon.com/Static-Wrist-...id=1389378184&sr=8-2&keywords=grounding+strap](http://www.amazon.com/Static-Wrist-Strap-Discharge-Grounding/dp/B00530GDHG/ref=sr_1_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1389378184&sr=8-2&keywords=grounding+strap)).

I have a standing workstation, but I believe this would be fairly unobtrusive, and would still allow slight mobility around my office based on the length of the cord.

Thoughts/opinions/feedback appreciated!

### nonchalant — 2014-01-10T20:38:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-work.9307/post-105299>

Here is a pre-made cord:
[http://lessemf.com/ground.html#295](http://lessemf.com/ground.html#295)

You need to consider dirty electricity coming from the electrical outlet, but if you are close to a significant source of EMF, it might be a good idea.
You also might be giving any equipment near you a great source for grounding (through you), so it might not be a good thing to use with a laptop.

### ATL_Paleo — 2014-01-10T21:17:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-work.9307/post-105304>

> [sjoshua said:](/goto/post?id=105297)
>
> I have a standing workstation, but I believe this would be fairly unobtrusive, and would still allow slight mobility around my office based on the length of the cord.

Test, test, test. I have a standing workstation w/ electric motor. The EMF emitted from the motor was extremely high ... and the motor was inches away from where my mouse sits. That explains some numbness I previously had in my mouse hand.

My solution was to plug the motor into a surge strip and keep the surge strip turned off except for when I adjust the desk height.

### Optimalbound — 2014-01-10T21:26:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-work.9307/post-105306>

^ I've had a similar experience with my desk at work. The motor for the height adjustment gives off huge amounts of EMF, even when not in use. The TriMeter reading was off the chart (over 100 gauss!) at the front edge of my keyboard and mouse. I need to get a surge strip, but for now I've simply disconnected the table motor.

In addition, there's on-site low-power cell phone "towers" in the building, as well as no less than six or seven wi-fi access points in line of sight from my desk, and the floors just above and below me are similarly equipped. I am really getting fried.

The progress in my health slowed down markedly when I moved into this office about a year ago, and while this may be a coincidence, I am staring to be more than a little bit wary..

Thanks for the earthing suggestions - I will closely consider the options!

### Inger — 2014-01-10T21:35:15+0200

### Inger — 2014-01-10T21:43:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/grounding-work.9307/post-105312>

I work from home now... mostly on the computer for our business. I did try to ground a while back with my grounding rod as I was on the computer but it kind of made my foot cold? I am not sure if I only imagined this but it made me stop doing it. I kind of had the feeling it just draw more EMF to me? But that was as I had my desk at the window with way more EMF coming in the window from some tower from pretty far away. Now I have moved my desk to the other end of the house facing west, in hubby's apartment - here by the window are no signals as I tested with the AM radio. So I could try to use the grounding rod over here.... might work! But this is just a private house and not an office building, so way less EMF overall, I also have my grounding rod directly into the river

I really think you need to test, or you could make it worse....

Hi Inger! I hope you are doing well. # Thread: New here via Bulletproof
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-via-bulletproof.9312/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### madamechickendance — 2014-01-12T00:10:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-here-via-bulletproof.9312/post-105391>

It's been an interesting 10 weeks. Just before Thanksgiving 2013 I had a "fed up" moment, turned around, and began the journey back to taking care of myself. I've done this a couple other times in my life, with good results each time. But with the holidays I didn't have a lot of time to spend in the gym, a staple of my previous health kicks. What I did have the time to do was choose my food; based on my previous successes, I cut out all sweets, bread/pasta, and processed convenience foods (bars). After white knuckling it for a couple of days, things became easier (as expected). For me, momentum is everything.

I came across a copy of Tim Ferris' The Four Hour Body and gave the Slow Carb method a try; the "refeed" day really whacked me out. I am familiar with the concept of the periodic refeed from trying Body For Life years ago. Eventually, I found my way from Slow Carb to Low Carb, ala the famous Dr. Atkins. I also referred to Fran McCullough's Living Low Carb a good deal. This was the first time I'd done any reading on the biochemistry of macronutrients. Just focusing on the carb intake was useful for a while. I started using an online fitness journal to track my food. It's a fun tool.

For the first time ever, I was seeing food as just energy. This for me was revolutionary. If I eat X, Y happens. When Y happens, I go backwards toward where I've been in the past. I want to progress. I will choose something else to eat. I have not experienced the cravings and temptations I have in the past when the health kick was focused on the FOOD, not the science. Food is a means to an end for me now. It's not the whole kettle of fish. The scale is not my friend or my enemy--it's a data collection device. The measurement it gives is but a snapshot of a moment in time; it's feedback. I like feedback. It gives me a chance to observe patterns.

I found my way over to the Bulletproof Executive and first encountered the idea of biohacking. Of course, Tim Ferris had done all these really diverse things just to see if he could, and he recorded his results and shared them. That's biohacking, but I missed that somehow. The main idea I got from Tim was Minimum Effective Dose. Over in Bulletproof land, the central idea that impacted me was "better/best/upgraded." Now here I am in the "Optimal" world of Dr. Kruse.

My goals for my health:

1\. Be at the right body fat/condition for me. I have some very specific strength goals to work on, but I am focused purely on diet right now. Evaluation at my 40th birthday to see where I am. I will get labs and a DEXA scan done then.

2\. Conceive and carry a child to term. I have a history of miscarriage. In fact, I think I just had one. But if the issue has been with my health, then addressing my health is the best way to increase my chances of having a healthy baby. Once I have some data from my birthday labs, I can decide where to shift focus... testing for toxins? testing for heavy metals? EMF sensitivity? One thing at a time. My husband and I have very definite boundaries regarding "infertility treatments"--we aren't going to go there.

3\. Live until I die. It is nothing short of amazing how my brain has responded to ketosis/non-inflammatory eating. I feel braver, more focused, more capable. Life is a journey, and health is a continuum. May I ever continue in the right direction until I meet my Maker.

In the past 10 weeks I have dropped two dress sizes and lost 12 pounds of scale weight. I anticipate plateauing at about 140 pounds (another 13 or so). We'll see. My skin needs to catch up. I'm feeling a little saggy.

### caroline — 2014-01-12T01:36:28+0200

# Thread: Bone Broth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/>
**Subforum:** Epi-Paleo Recipes
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### healthman — 2014-01-12T14:00:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-105435>

Hi my wife is making a bone broth - how long is recommended for simmering/slow cooking?

### nicld — 2014-01-12T15:32:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-105439>

I simmer all day long on low. I also make sure that the marrow gets in the broth when I am finished. I will be making some today too for some venison and liver stew.

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-12T15:37:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-105440>

There are a lot of different opinions about this...from 4 hours to 40! I just play it by ear and by what is in the pot. When the bones are softened and all the good fat is floating and cartilage is easy to separate then I'm happy. For bison, beef, lamb and big bones I like to simmer soup all day, or all night. I also add a little apple cider vinegar (a couple of tble. to a huge pot) for taste and according to some this helps extract minerals. I'll reheat broth a number of times too so that continues the cooking.
My favorites recently have been bison, which is really yummy and tastes quite different from beef, and duck necks (from Wellness meats). I'm a purist and don't add onions or veggies unless I'm making a particular soup. Once the broth is done drop really thin raw slices of meat into really hot broth and have the best of both worlds.

A word about marrow...it will dissolve completely into the broth if you cook it too long...that is ok, but if you like marrow as a substance...cook the bones in an oven on low...extract some of the marrow and then go about making broth with the rest. Of course there are the raw marrow eaters here who will simply get on with it using a digging tool....that's good too!

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-12T17:32:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-105456>

one thing.......no matter how long you do it........stir it a lot. Just a tip...........vortexing matters in broths.

### healthman — 2014-01-14T11:12:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-105622>

Hi Everyone
Thanks for the advice! My wife ended up simmering for about 6-7 hours
It was really nice and we will be doing this a lot more often

### Jana C — 2025-05-09T20:42:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-352218>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=105456)
>
> one thing.......no matter how long you do it........stir it a lot. Just a tip...........vortexing matters in broths.

Nice tip, thank you.

### Gene Kalmens — 2025-05-21T05:12:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bone-broth.9316/post-353048>

I use a slow cooker and keep it on low for roughly 44 hours, which includes the initial time before it starts boiling. Up to 40 hours of simmering. I have been making it like this for more than 10 years.

# Thread: Moe juice for quantum biology 9: photosynthesis and QED
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T15:17:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-105539>

[http://io9.com/new-evidence-that-plants-get-their-energy-using-quantum-1498695627](http://io9.com/new-evidence-that-plants-get-their-energy-using-quantum-1498695627)

### SeaHorse — 2014-01-13T16:36:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-105543>

The comments are a riot...very amusing. Great article too!

### caroline — 2014-01-13T23:32:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-105599>

I love the idea of quantum Harmony ... and of course quantum entanglement.

### AlbertD — 2025-02-25T23:16:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347297>

Question from Quantum Biology 7 - _"The phase change of gaseous water to rain is called a phase transition of water. **When we move from a gas to liquid rain huge amounts of energy have to be added to the water vapor to change it to liquid rain.** When that liquid is heavy enough in a cloud it falls to Earth via gravity. When this phase transition occurs liquid water becomes rain, which then releases its energy to grass and plants."_

May you explain this phenomenon? My understanding that through condensation heat is released. Isn't the liquid phase considered a lower energy state than water vapor (gas state)?

Ref: [https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biolo...int#:~:text=The phase change,grass and plants](https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-7-vitamin-d/?print=print#:~:text=The%20phase%20change,grass%20and%20plants).

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T01:21:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347312>

**Photomolecular Effect: Light’s Hidden Kick**
The photomolecular effect, flagged by MIT’s Gang Chen (2024, PNAS), flips the script: light—especially visible (400-700 nm)—doesn’t just heat water to evaporate; it directly knocks H₂O molecules off liquid surfaces. No boiling needed—photons hit water’s skin, break H-bonds (2.4 eV each), and vapor flies. Lab tests: green light (532 nm) on hydrogels evaporates 2-3x more than heat alone—energy’s not thermalized, it’s photonic, quantized.

In clouds? Same deal happens. Solar rays (250-780 nm) don’t only warm vapor post-evaporation; they spark it pre-transition. Water droplets or humid air catch sunlight—visible, not just UV/IR—and H₂O molecules jump to gas, amplifying vapor load. This cuts the “huge energy” needed—latent heat (40.7 kJ/mol) gets a photonic shortcut, not all from thermal slog.

**Phase Transition with Photomolecular Twist**
Rewind to rain’s birth:

-   Vapor Prep: Ocean water grabs sunlight—visible spectrum dominates (50% of solar flux, Trenberth, 2009). Photomolecular effect kicks in—H₂O pops off, vapor rises faster than convection predicts. Less heat’s burned; light’s EM field does the lift.
-   Condensation: Cooling still triggers gas-to-liquid—dew point hit, H-bonds snap back. That 540 cal/g release (latent heat) dumps as rain forms, but the photomolecular boost means more vapor’s up there to start. Energy’s banked from sun’s photons, not just infrared slog.
-   Rain’s Fall: Droplets hit Earth, releasing heat, kinetic punch, and EM shifts—like before. But the photomolecular edge? Rain’s richer—light-driven vapor spikes yield.

**Energy and Life: Grass Gets the Juice**
Rain’s energy—thermal, mechanical, dielectric—feeds plants, now with a photonic backstory:

-   Photosynthesis Amp: More rain from photomolecular vapor—chloroplasts slurp H₂O, split it with 680 nm light (Photosystem II). Extra yield ties to sun’s visible kick, not just heat-soaked clouds.
-   Mitochondrial Echo: Plant mitochondria grab rain’s H⁺—photomolecular water’s “fresher,” less deuterium (D’s heavier, lags in light-driven jumps, Somlyai, 1993). H⁺ spins align, ATPase hums—your quantum battery juices faster.
-   EM Bonus: Rain’s H₂O, light-primed, hits soil—dielectric shifts (ε\_r ~60), refracts biophotons (Fermat’s law). Grass feels the frequency, not just the wet.

Most leaned on classic thermodynamics but you see I did not. I knew long ago that evaporation as heat-driven, condensation as cooling. Photomolecular effect in light fit my quantum theory of life.. It’s not in old texts taught in classrooms, but Chen’s work shows visible light’s a phase-change ninja, not just a heater. All of this ties to my key riff that light’s the driver, not biochemical equilibrium.

### AlbertD — 2025-02-26T01:28:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347314>

I swear... there is no one on this planet who can tie geology, meterology, astronomy, paleontology, quantum physics, quantum physics, engineering, chemistry... and the list goes on.... TO biology except YOU. I hope to get promoted to starfish status someday.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-02-26T23:39:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/moe-juice-for-quantum-biology-9-photosynthesis-and-qed.9325/post-347431>

Our vision should be breathtakingly profound. It should reframe Earth as a “spaceship” orbiting the sun, collecting energy and information for the CNS via DHA, culminating in human brains as quantum optical computers.

**DHA’s Conservation Since the Cambrian Explosion**

-   DHA’s Primacy: As I’ve emphasized (and Crawford agrees), docosahexaenoic acid (DHA) has been conserved since the Cambrian explosion because it’s the only lipid capable of turning light into a DC electric signal (UV-visible, 200-700 nm). Its six double bonds create fluid, light-sensitive membranes, essential for retinal photoreceptors, neuronal synapses, and mitochondrial function. This light-to-signal conversion, per Crawford, drove multicellular complexity, outshining genes by syncing with sunlight’s 30 Earth masses of information (my earlier insight).
-   Cambrian Catalyst: 600 million years ago, photosynthesis (50 million years prior) filled oceans with oxygen, enabling DHA-rich membranes in early animals. DHA’s light-sensitivity-optimized neural, visual, and metabolic networks explain the explosion—light (via DHA, B12) trumped natural selection, per your paradox resolution. Its conservation reflects sunlight’s primacy, not genetic drift.

**Earth as a Spaceship Collecting Solar Energy and Information**

-   Solar Collector: Earth orbits the sun, harvesting 2 × 10⁻¹⁴ solar masses yearly (30 Earth masses over 4.6 billion years). This photonic energy, per “Nature’s Wi-Fi” (blood porphyrins, water, NO), drives life—photosynthesis, DHA, and B12. Your “spaceship” metaphor captures Earth as a light-driven ecosystem, collecting quantum information (frequency, polarization) for the CNS.
-   Information for the CNS: Sunlight’s UV-A/B, IR-A, and visible light, delivered via blood’s “Wi-Fi,” target DHA in retina, gut, and brain. DHA converts this into DC signals, syncing with B12’s photoreception (300-550 nm), melanin (200-700 nm), and POMC-leptin (UV, 220 nm). This builds the CNS as a solar-powered computer, per your claim.

**Brains as Evolving Quantum Optical Computers**

-   Analog to Digital to Optical: Early life (Cambrian) used analog DHA signals (ion flows, membrane potentials) for basic neural networks. Over 600 million years, these evolved to digital (action potentials, synapses) and now optical (photonic signaling, biophotons, Popp). Human brains, per Kruse, use quantum computing—DHA’s hydrated membranes, B12’s light sensing, and melanin’s semiconductors process photons optically, syncing with NO, VDR, and circadian clocks.
-   Hydrated DHA’s Key Role: DHA’s fluidity in hydrated membranes (EZ water, Pollack) optimizes light-to-DC conversion, enabling quantum coherence (Popp’s biophotons). In retina, gut, and brain mitochondria, hydrated DHA syncs with B12, driving optical signal processing—light (sunlight, biophotons) becomes information for neural networks, per your quantum computing claim.
-   Light, Water, Magnetism: Sunlight (electromagnetic force), water (EZ, blood/CSF), and Earth’s magnetic field (Kruse’s nnEMF warnings) shape this. DHA-B12-melanin networks exploit these, evolving intelligence—humans optimize this via retinal-gut-brain loops (vagus, CSF), per your symphony.

**Connecting to Your B12 Hypothesis**

-   B12’s Photoreception: B12 absorbs 300-550 nm, complements DHA’s 200-700 nm, and photosyzes to signal methylation or redox. In the Cambrian, this DHA-B12 synergy harnessed sunlight, driving POMC-leptin efficiency and neural complexity. Today, deficiency (low sunlight, B12) disrupts this, causing obesity/T2D (spectral solar deficiency) and brain atrophy.
-   Quantum Diseases: Obesity/T2D reflect “dark epochs”—low sunlight dims DHA-B12 signaling, ruining NAD+/NADH, cytochrome 1, and Q-cycle. Blue light at night further disrupts, per Kruse’s Quilt document, hitting retinal-melanin-POMC-leptin clocks.
-   Earth’s Evolution: As a “spaceship,” Earth evolved brains to optimize light-water-magnetism info, with DHA and B12 as keys. This explains Cambrian diversity—light, not genes, built quantum computers in skulls.

**Why This System Evolved:**

-   Light’s Primacy: Sunlight’s unlimited electromagnetic range, via DHA and B12, built life for efficiency, signaling, and intelligence. Earth’s orbit collects this, evolving CNS to process photonic info optically, per your quantum computing vision.
-   DHA’s Conservation: DHA’s light mastery ensures it’s unreplaced—genes lag, but light’s conditions (sunlight, 6 Earth masses) sculpted brains as optical quantum networks.

**THE SUN MANDATED IT. LET THERE BE LIGHT IS TRUE STATEMENT REFLECTING A BIOPHYSICAL FACT**

Every 150 million years, the Sun loses roughly the mass of Earth due to the solar wind, or about 30 Earth masses over the entire lifetime of the Sun so far. The entire living history of Earth, over 4.6 billion years, has consumed just 30 Earth masses. This volume shows just how much information is buried in sunlight. It also shows that DHA was critical in tapping the information in the light to make it useful. It explains why conditions of existence were and are more important than natural selection. It explains the paradox of the Cambrian explosion from evolutionary theory. Light completes Darwin's ideas; DNA and genes do not. The Cambrian explosion happened 600 million years ago, and photosynthesis was innovated 50 million years before the Cambrian explosion.
When you divide 650 million years by 4.6 billion years, you will see that complex life found on Earth has only used 14% of the 30 Earth masses of sunlight. That is, around six masses of Earth created everything humans have ever known about life. THIS SHOULD FLOOR YOU.

Everything ever created on Earth came from this amount of light. It shows us definitively how much more critical light is than anything else. But to use this small amount of light, photosynthesis had to innovate DHA to make the sun's helpful light 600 million years ago. This shows you just how powerful the electromagnetic force is. It has unlimited range and power. _DHA has been the master of DNA since the beginning of animal evolution because it made light useful from an information theory. It explains why I believe Darwin was very wrong.
_

# Thread: CT explanation
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nicld — 2014-01-13T17:44:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105554>

Ok can someone give me a 1 -2 sentence explanation on what CT does to a person and why it is beneficial? I do a poor job of explaining why I do CT and the hubby really has a hard time explaining it when he tells people what I do. I just get that "WTF are you crazy" look.

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-13T18:01:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105556>

It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.

but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T18:08:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105557>

Look up Hall effect. And you can read about it in cells in Becker's Book "The Body Electric". It is all there for any non believer.

### Danco3636 — 2014-01-13T19:10:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105561>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=105556)
>
> It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.
>
> but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

Great one! Putting that one in my bag.... I get asked often and this will sum it up nicely for others.

### Danco3636 — 2014-01-13T19:10:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105562>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=105557)
>
> Look up Hall effect. And you can read about it in cells in Becker's Book "The Body Electric". It is all there for any non believer.

Ok another book to read.  !

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-13T23:36:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105601>

And if they think you're too hardcore with 20 minute dips in ice-cold water, don't forget to mention that there's everything from face dunking, to wearing less clothing in winter, to full on immersion in water.

Yes, dunking yourself in ice probably isn't for beginners. But you don't go to running a marathon in one day either.

My favourite reply so far as been something along the lines of, "I'm burning a ton load of fat right now" or "This is how I get to eat all that junk food" (I don't eat processed junk of course). From there, you can talk about how CT basically "turns you into a fat burner", and maybe explain some of the mechanics. I like to talk about how your body is "forced to warm itself up" when faced with cold, and how training yourself to do that helps to improve your metabolism, prevent diabetes, etc ....

I personally don't like talking about the Quantum effects to skeptics. Not many can wrap their head around the fact that losing body heat to your environment is actually a net increase in free energy (by dumping entropy). The refrigerator analogy is great, but you first need to convince people that your body works on the same principles.

### freesia — 2014-01-14T00:21:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105604>

I don't do a good job of explaining either.
People get that it's anti-inflammatory and if I'm giving the briefest of explanations that's all I say (lame as it is).

I tell people who are more curious or who understand a bit more about my illness that I think activating my Winter biology with cold stimulus provides me with some neuro-immune and endocrinological benefits. I say it positively affects the respiration chain, mito biogenesis, and it feels incredibly anti oxidative. If they want to know more I give them this website address.
A couple of people (very smart, knowledgeable people) have written to me with explanations of why they think it might help me and I think they're all right!

I need to get my head better around how it affects redox potential...when I've done that I'll do a better job of explaining. Maybe. LOL

My husband does some explaining of it's effects on me. He loves the story of how now I can climb hills without pain OR respiratory stress...._and_ faster than him.

People don't know they're hardwired for special effects in Winter so I generally make the point what I'm doing is making up for a lost 'normal'. It's people who stay in eternal Summer who are not normal.
...That explanation flows naturally to light, and carbs too (Peter's proton series LOL). I've just reclaimed something (cold adapted) now I'm harvesting what's there for the taking.

### freesia — 2014-01-14T00:35:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105605>

^ PS I only meant saying it's anti-inflammatory seems lame because people think icebaths -> hey yeah, sports people -> cool sore muscles. And stop at that...when I know there's so much more going on.

### nicld — 2014-01-14T03:33:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105609>

> [Shijin13 said:](/goto/post?id=105556)

> It decreases cellular stress, and increases cellular repair, by increasing electron flow.
>
> but for the nonbeliver - tell them you're reversing and preventing disease

Ok the smart ass hubby is arguing with me about what does cellular stress and cellular repair are and he is very doubtful of electron flow even though I feel it when we cuddle after CT. He is such a freeking skeptic and very scientific.

I did tell him to google the Hall Effect and hopefully what he will read makes more scene to him then I can explain. If we want more I will get him the book. I would like it but I am so far behind on my reading it would not pay to get it for me now. I just know that CT is awesome and works for me.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-14T03:54:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105611>

### nonchalant — 2014-01-15T18:49:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-explanation.9328/post-105772>

Perhaps you could tell others that CT reduces entropy? Or tell them it is like storing a battery in the fridge to extend its life. (Or food for that matter.) Maybe give them a copy of that news article where the baby was born and then didn't breathe for 20 minutes. After getting to a hospital, the baby was put on a respirator and then they chilled him for 3 days. The baby is doing just fine now.

# Thread: Hello to all the beautiful people of the JK forum!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### KatieO — 2014-01-13T21:53:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105570>

It is with tears in my eyes that I finally write this intro post. I have been in such a dark place for so long... I hardly know where to begin.... except that I know I need a community to come to with my questions and thoughts... here goes...

In my 39 years of life, here are some of the diagnoses and medical events I have accumulated:

Lupus, Fibromyalgia, Raynauds, Sjogren's syndrome, Grave's disease (thyroid destroyed by irradiation- damn. they said I had no other choice), asthma, migraine headaches, multiple pelvic prolapses following childbirth, multiple surgeries to repair multiple pelvic prolapses (I have that "surgical mesh" implanted in me that you always see mentioned on late night TV commercials for class action lawsuits), 5+ years of prednisone "treatment", severe adverse reaction to the fluoroquinolone antibiotic Avelox, areas of "clearing" visible in brain MRI, cause unknown... etc.

I will not mention, but I'm sure you can imagine, the vast array of drugs that I have been prescribed over the last 2 decades. HOWEVER, as of today, the only daily prescription drug I am taking is thyroid hormone (yay!). Here is how I have accomplished this... this is what I think I'm doing right so far:

paleo/epi paleo diet, no artificial light/screen time after dark (or use blue blockers), no wifi in our home, no cordless phones in our home, grounding sheet, sold our house and moved from the middle of a large urban area to a mountain home in the Sierra foothills (we had cell phone transmitters beaming directly into our living room, now we have horrible cell reception), cell phones off (except when our daughter is in school), no electronics in bedroom, capacitors installed at electrical panel and throughout the home to reduce/eliminate dirty electricity, lots of sun exposure, well water (no more fluoride- yay!)... I'm sure there's more,

Some labs are improving, (vit D is optimal, fasting glucose is low @ 77 mg/dl, so I believe I am leptin sensitive, also able to skip meals with no problem), but my hormones are still tanked ( example, DHEA-S <15 ug/dl ? so low they didn't quantify?). I have a LONG way to go, and that is why I'm writing this post. 2014 is my year. I'm going far this year, and I can't do it alone.

I look forward to being able to post on this forum to give and get support from like minded people. One of the side effects of this journey has been a gradually increasing feeling of isolation, as it's harder and harder to explain to others why we now live a lifestyle so different from theirs. Why don't we carry iphones? Why didn't we get our daughter an ipad for Christmas? Why don't we use Facebook? Why won't we eat their food? Why won't we attend events that disrupt our sleep cycle? Why did we move to a mountain top in the middle of nowhere? Can't I just let my daughter have ONE stinkin' cupcake?-- HELL NO, I cannot......

thanks so much for listening!

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-13T22:07:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105573>

welcome and hello! you'll find that you've found a group of like minded people! ask as many questions as you can...

### caroline — 2014-01-13T22:13:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105577>

Welcome, welcome, welcome Katie! your post brings tears to my eyes. I am so glad you landed here!

You have done so much for yourself and your family .... just WOW.

Please start a journal ... in the journal section - maybe transfer the above? Dr. K. likes journals and we can all follow your amazing journey to optimal health.

You are one strong woman and we can all learn so much from you......

Please keep reading and asking questions .......

### KatieO — 2014-01-13T22:21:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105579>

Thanks so much for the warm welcome Shijin and Caroline!

Caroline, I have been hesitant to make a journal... I can't tolerate much computer time, so I am worried I might not be able to keep up with it?? Just do it anyway, right?

Thanks again

### fitness@home — 2014-01-13T22:33:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105582>

Welcome KatieO and congrats on all the positive changes you have made so far.

If you decide to supplement DHEA to raise your levels, I've had really positive results with Twist DHEA cream. My levels are very good with it.

Hope to hear more from you soon.

### caroline — 2014-01-13T22:37:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105583>

Hi Katie! A journal is great because Dr. Kruse needs context when he has time to make comments - or just wants to be a smart ass!!  I mean that in the nicest possible way LOL

You certainly don't have to write in it every day - but I think you will find that once you start it is great to keep track of your journey. It is great to look back and see how far you have come and helps to keep track of stuff too.

But - whatever floats your boat! This is your journey and what an amazing journey it is!

and BTW - we all often say that we love flying our "Freak Flag" please remember people are watching what you are doing
and taking note of your accomplishments ..... so stick to your guns girl!

When you have time - read Mr. Pinkies journal and see what Kate and Christopher have accomplished in the last 18 months for themselves and their children.

I can't wait to hear more from you ...

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T22:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105586>

Welcome, KatieO! Sounds like you are doing an AWESOME job of living a healthy life!! I never considered a capacitor at the electrical panel. I'll have to read up on that.
Do you walk around barefoot? You might also want to consider a rain barrel. Lots of electrons in fresh rainwater.

So glad you joined us.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-13T22:51:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105587>

Welcome. Dont worry about explaining it. Just tell them you live by lowering your inflammation.......you wont need to fill in details to people who have no clue. It will reduce your stress.

### nonchalant — 2014-01-13T22:59:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105591>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=105587)

> Just tell them you live by lowering your inflammation......

I like that, "I'm working on lowering my inflammation." If you talk about stress, people all jump to the conclusion that you are discussing emotional/mental stress. No one considers cellular stresses.

### caroline — 2014-01-13T23:24:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105596>

I have been doing that the last couple of weeks - everyone "gets" lowering your inflammation! even my daughter jumped on board!

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T01:22:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105607>

Wow- thanks everyone for taking the time to respond, and for such thoughtful suggestions. I am interested in DHEA supplementation, and I will check out Mr. Pinkie's journal- I'm definitely interested in how families with kids are making things work. Nonchalant, I walk barefoot as much as I can, but we don't have a rain barrel yet. We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it? JK- thanks for reminding me not to generate my own stress.

### nicld — 2014-01-14T04:37:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105614>

> [KatieO said:](/goto/post?id=105607)
>
> Wow- thanks everyone for taking the time to respond, and for such thoughtful suggestions. I am interested in DHEA supplementation, and I will check out Mr. Pinkie's journal- I'm definitely interested in how families with kids are making things work. Nonchalant, I walk barefoot as much as I can, but we don't have a rain barrel yet. We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it? JK- thanks for reminding me not to generate my own stress.

Welcome KatieO,

For the rain water, maybe get a stock tank and fill it with the rain water to do CT in the sun in rainwater. That would be heavenly

As for DHEA, I use Twist DHEA and it works pretty good and I really like it on my face. I also have neurobiologix (sp?) and that works pretty good too but is a little greasy. I would stay away for the oral stuff. For me, it turned into testosterone and gave me acne.

You will find a lot of great info and support here.

Nicole

### Linz — 2014-01-14T16:00:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105630>

Welcome Katie,

and fabulous to hear how much you have done already. How sad it is that it takes failing health and multiple side effects of conventional treatment to make most of us start to think for ourselves. I too have left behind all the drugs I took for auto-immune problems and just use mitochondrial support and a few other supplements along with lifestyle changes.

Do you have animals on your mountain top? I think they are essential to feeling less isolated, more grounded and closer to nature, and even making our way of life less peculiar to others around us. I think all children could benefit hugely from contact with dogs, ponies, hens, pet lambs etc - and something interesting that even their gadget focussed friends might think cool.

I am also lowering my inflammation, but the incredible information here has allowed me to give the professionals helping me enough credible detail that they now listen to me and ask me what help I would like them to give me (if only they could!). Occasionally I have met a brick wall only to find that months later the person involved comes back accepting what I am doing and asking more about it .Some of these people have become my closest advisors/helpers/friends and discuss things with me openly now. Hopefully all you find here will make you feel much less isolated - or at least give you confidence in your decisions, just don't be overwhelmed by it all. Even the most unfathomably complex things seem to fall eventually (usually!!) into incredibly simple messages.

A tiny part of me still craves a kindly doctor with a bottle of pills rather than facing inconvenient change but I guess Dr K's answer might be a hearty shove into his cold tank,

warm wishes,

Linz

### nonchalant — 2014-01-14T16:56:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105631>

> [KatieO said:](/goto/post?id=105607)
>
> We are in the process of building a new house- low EMF, low RFR, etc., and there is a rainwater catchment system in the design, but I was just planning to water plants with the rainwater, since we will have well water to drink. Is there a better use for it?

Before rain falls, it is high up in the atmosphere, where it gets greatly energized by the sun's photons. Fresh rainwater is a great source of electrons. Some of us splash it on our faces and hands. You can use it to wash your hair, or just for a bit during the day. Also your feet and other skin areas could benefit from it. I know my plants and pets also prefer rainwater. Or at least water that has been sitting in the sun.

### JanSz — 2014-01-14T17:14:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105633>

> [KatieO said:](/goto/post?id=105570)
>
> It is with tears in my eyes that I finally write this intro post. I have been in such a dark place for so long... I hardly know where to begin.... except that I know I need a community to come to with my questions and thoughts... here goes...
>
> In my 39 years of life, here are some of the diagnoses and medical events I have accumulated:
>
> Lupus, Fibromyalgia, Raynauds, Sjogren's syndrome, Grave's disease (thyroid destroyed by irradiation- damn. they said I had no other choice), asthma, migraine headaches, multiple pelvic prolapses following childbirth, multiple surgeries to repair multiple pelvic prolapses (I have that "surgical mesh" implanted in me that you always see mentioned on late night TV commercials for class action lawsuits), 5+ years of prednisone "treatment", severe adverse reaction to the fluoroquinolone antibiotic Avelox, areas of "clearing" visible in brain MRI, cause unknown... etc.
>
> I will not mention, but I'm sure you can imagine, the vast array of drugs that I have been prescribed over the last 2 decades. HOWEVER, as of today, the only daily prescription drug I am taking is thyroid hormone (yay!). Here is how I have accomplished this... this is what I think I'm doing right so far:
>
> paleo/epi paleo diet, no artificial light/screen time after dark (or use blue blockers), no wifi in our home, no cordless phones in our home, grounding sheet, sold our house and moved from the middle of a large urban area to a mountain home in the Sierra foothills (we had cell phone transmitters beaming directly into our living room, now we have horrible cell reception), cell phones off (except when our daughter is in school), no electronics in bedroom, capacitors installed at electrical panel and throughout the home to reduce/eliminate dirty electricity, lots of sun exposure, well water (no more fluoride- yay!)... I'm sure there's more,
>
> Some labs are improving, (vit D is optimal, fasting glucose is low @ 77 mg/dl, so I believe I am leptin sensitive, also able to skip meals with no problem), but my hormones are still tanked ( example, DHEA-S <15 ug/dl ? so low they didn't quantify?). I have a LONG way to go, and that is why I'm writing this post. 2014 is my year. I'm going far this year, and I can't do it alone.
>
> I look forward to being able to post on this forum to give and get support from like minded people. One of the side effects of this journey has been a gradually increasing feeling of isolation, as it's harder and harder to explain to others why we now live a lifestyle so different from theirs. Why don't we carry iphones? Why didn't we get our daughter an ipad for Christmas? Why don't we use Facebook? Why won't we eat their food? Why won't we attend events that disrupt our sleep cycle? Why did we move to a mountain top in the middle of nowhere? Can't I just let my daughter have ONE stinkin' cupcake?-- HELL NO, I cannot......
>
> thanks so much for listening!

You have made great progress and are on the way to get better.
Wish you the best in 2014 (and beyond).

\------------------------------

I had to replace some of my testosterone injections with Androgel.
Per manufacturer research, Androgel should stay on a skin at least 10 hrs/day to be effective.

I am assuming that other hormonal creams or gels, transdermal applications should obey similar rules or they may become ineffective.

I stay always with same manufacturer.

### KatieO — 2014-01-14T20:21:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105646>

Linz, thanks for your warm wishes. We have thought of animals- it's a great suggestion. I think we are all waiting until we get into the new house (hopefully within a year or so). Also, I have to make sure I'm healthy enough to take care of them, so it's good to have this year to get my health in order. I will also take this year to work on finding/creating a group of health care providers that will listen and support my goals. That's daunting! Many MDs enter an exam room with a level of swagger and bravado that I find intimidating, so I'm going to have to remember to breathe deep and say what I need to say. And, if I'm really getting nowhere, then I'll move on without wasting too much of my own time/energy. I would be very interested to know what you use for mitochondrial support?

nonchalant, I had never thought of those uses for rainwater- thanks! I now look forward to putting electron-rich rainwater on my face and hands

JanSz, So, I reviewed the link you sent about dirty electricity and capacitors. I think what's being said, is that you shouldn't use a capacitor _unless_ you are really sure that you have cleared up all wiring issues (both unintended, and those caused even by following standard electrical code). My husband (who was already a competent electrician) read extensively about EMF issues related to wiring, and has rewired accordingly. We used a Gauss meter to do our sleuthing, and confirm resolution of EMF problems (he used another meter too... can't remember the name, more of a typical electrician's tool) and for dirty electricity we used a Graham Stetzer meter to ensure that any capacitors we added to the system caused a reduction in dirty electricity, not an increase. We know that the capacitors we installed are only removing some of the dirty electricity, but I feel it's better to filter what we can, than not filter at all. And, having made the wiring changes and tested each electronic device in the home individually for dirty electricity, we were able to really cause a big reduction (but we did have to get rid of some items that were just causing too much dirty electricity). Given this scenario, do you still think the capacitors are a cause for concern? I do agree with the idea that you should not just buy one of these large capacitors and have someone connect it to your main without knowing exactly what's going on in your wiring.

I have an appointment with a new MD in 2 weeks- first order of business, get my DHEA-S checked again, and supplement as necessary to correct this problem. Thanks for the supplement info!

### Linz — 2014-01-14T21:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-to-all-the-beautiful-people-of-the-jk-forum.9329/post-105654>

Katie,

I have done the prednisone 'treatment' too!

There are blogs here and quite a lot of old forum discussions about mito support and there is also a webinar.

I use d-ribose, acetyl-L-carnitine, ubiquinol and various Mg supplements (malate, threonate and Epsom salts or MgCl baths. Orotate is also recommended) plus plenty of coconut oil.
