# Thread: Interesting n=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danco3636 — 2014-01-14T06:44:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105616>

OK after reading Jacks response to the below questions I decided to do my own n=1 on "Protein Refeeds". For the last few years I have been epi-paleo keto diet.... I hover around 7-8% BF and am pretty well built. Been wanting to drop a few percentages to be back to my lean 5-6% or less body fat. That is where I was when I did Leangains before......

Well yesterday I did a protein refeed.... It went like this:
AM Coffee with a small amount of MCT and GF butter...
Fasted till about 2:00 or so and had a meal of protein based low to no fat seafood (shrimp and oysters) and egg whites.
Then had meal 2 (6PM), (a bit larger) of no fat seafood (cod more shrimp and egg whites with turmeric, curry powder and some sea greens and kale)
Filled me up great and killed my hunger until I went to bed around 9ish.....
Well I slept 9 hours..... bam! (got up for the bathroom twice to urinate & upon rising)
Well I woke up and felt great.... I looked and felt leaner....
When I was sleeping my body heated up and was warmer than usual.

Upon rising I dipped into my 45DF pool and did some outside movement and climbed a few trees. I followed it up with a cold shower....

Seems like this protein seafood based refeed did me good. I felt great all day, did a fasted weight session later and hit some PR's.

Today I ate normal epi keto diet..... craved some mac nuts so I had some of those as well...

So I will do another tomorrow and see how it goes. I may implement these 2-3 times a week as needed to get lean and feel great. Going to keep with testing them...

PS.... I also used a product called "KetoForce" to help keep in in Ketosis.....

Thanks for the questions
yewwei.tan

_**Protein Refeeds instead of Carb Refeeds

Listening to your interview with Dave Asprey reminded me about his recommendation to eat some starch (100-150g) every 3-7 days in this post: [http://www.bulletproofexec.com/the-b...safe-starches/](http://www.bulletproofexec.com/the-b...safe-starches/)

Dave claims to have experienced dry eyes, poor sleep, increased LDL, and even leaky gut, by not having enough carbs.

Question 1: Would protein refeeds work just as well as carb refeeds to prevent the symptoms that Dave experienced?

I ask because from what I know, refeeding with protein only would act on many of the same pathways as a carb refeed. In both cases, AMPk goes down, mTOR increases, leptin comes up a little (though more so with acute carb refeeds), and some would argue that the total insulin response that you can get from protein is comparable to carbs.

Even when it comes to thyroid health, overfeeding with protein seems to get T3 up and running again just as well as carbs: [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC371281/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC371281/)

Question 2: If protein refeeds work, what sort of quantity would illicit a significant effect?

For example, let's say you are eating 120 grams of protein a day to stay in ketosis. What would happen when for one day a week, you increase that dose up to 220 grams?

Question 3: What sort of negative effects would a protein refeed have compared to a carb refeed? Or put another way, should one consider refeeding by the +100 grams once a week if inflammation is still high?

From the IF and Leptin blog post:

AMPk pathways requires really optimal leptin sensitivity and signaling to be occurring between the brain, liver, and muscles.
Question 4: When is the best timing for a refeed?

I would think first thing in the morning, and in the 4-6pm window would be best to overfeed with protein.

Thanks!**_

### Shijin13 — 2014-01-14T17:56:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105635>

great n=1.

I know I've experienced greats amts of energy when just eating seafood, particularly raw, for the day. love the way it makes me feel...

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-15T01:22:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105694>

Haha, I asked that question because I was trying to figure if there is a way to maintain a really lean physique without compromising longevity.

I still have a bunch of questions that are yet to be answered of course, and need to think about them for awhile more.

For example, I was wondering if consuming less protein on other days is a good idea. I got this idea from Tim Ferriss's ['Geek to Freak' post](http://www.fourhourworkweek.com/blog/2007/04/29/from-geek-to-freak-how-i-gained-34-lbs-of-muscle-in-4-weeks/), and while I don't endorse almost anything in that post, he did talk about reducing calories by 50% for one day a week to prevent "protein uptake down-regulation". So his theory is that taking a low-protein day somehow re-sensitizes you to the to the effects of a protein surplus.

I was thinking that a really lean, but keto-adapted person, would have decreased amino acid requirements as a result. So the standard "eat more protein" as you get leaner may not actually have to be taken to extremes of the 3g/kg bodyweight recommendation that Martin Berkhan uses.

So what if said person eats 100-120g of protein a day for 5 days in the week, and then spikes that up to the 200-300g range for 2 days in a concentrated feeding window?

\---

Then there's the question about frequency -- how often to stuff your face with seafood? Common recommendations say that the leaner you get, the more re-feeding you need. Why is this?

\---

Anyway, I'll be experimenting as well. Just had basically a pound of grass-fed rump steak the other day that I get from a local farmer. Macadamia nuts are awesome too! Lots of these available here in Australia of course.

Thanks for sharing Dan!

### MeghanK — 2014-01-15T02:58:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105709>

what kind of Mac Nuts do you guys eat? I find the raw ones tasteless....

### yewwei.tan — 2014-01-15T03:16:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/interesting-n-1.9333/post-105713>

Only ever raw macadamias for me. I get them at this place near me for anywhere from $21.50 to $24 per KG (approx $10 - $10.90 per LBS) -- [http://www.rosssqualitynuts.com.au/](http://www.rosssqualitynuts.com.au/)

I find them incredibly sweet and tasty when raw, and actually like them better than the roasted ones. But this place I get them from is a wholesaler, and I'm pretty sure shipping them down from NSW or Queensland to VIC would only take 1-2 days, and the nuts would be pretty fresh as a result.

### Danco3636 — 2014-02-10T17:49:55+0200

Raw and organic. My Mac Nuts are tasty.

# Thread: My Earthpulse N=1
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Arcole — 2014-01-14T18:28:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-105637>

After 6 months of hemming and hawing, I finally popped and purchased the Earthpulse. A number of factors entered into my decision to go that way versus the Magnetico, not the least of which was $$. After three nights with it, I thought I needed to start a journal for my own sake and for the possible edification of the rest of the community who might also be considering.

Participants include myself, my husband, and my 8 month old 5 pound Maltese dog who sleeps in his crate on a chair next to the bed.

I received the Earthpulse very quickly on Friday 1/10/14 but had left my printouts of the documentation at work. I didn't want to sleep on it without reading up carefully first. The next morning I retrieved my quickstart info and did the 1 hour Recovery entrainment which had me drooling out of the corners of my mouth in a state of deep relaxation alternating with sleep. Even the dog fell asleep. That led me to choose Recovery as our setting of choice for our first night.

Night 1 -- 1/11/14 -- Recovery
I, husband, and dog slept like we'd been knocked out by bricks. I woke easily and felt very refreshed.
No waking to pee either, which has gotten better for me since starting Melatonin and Magnesium, but I still generally wake once. I didn't even wake up.

Night 2 -- 1/12/14 -- Recovery
Another solid night for all of us.
No wake to pee. Easy waking and great daytime energy.

Night 3 -- 1/13/14 -- Recovery
Everybody was wakeful. I woke at 1:30 and went to the bathroom and again at 3:00 but didn't get up. Even the dog was wakeful and played in his crate. But I still went back to sleep after each waking very easily and woke after 8 hours at 5:30 with no desire to snooze the alarm (a big deal for me because I generally sleep 9 hours and still want to snooze). So far, daytime energy has been great. I did use the Earthpulse during the day at work on Alert setting on 1/13/14 and am using it again 1/14/14. I also had my regular chiropractic adjustment on 1/13/14. I am not sure if any of this made a difference for me personally.

So far the husband has said he's sleeping fine. The dog doesn't say much about it.

Resting breath hold times since 7/9/13 have held steady at between 1:00 and 1:05 with one check early on as low as 45 seconds.

After the first week, I will test again under the same circumstances and see what I'm getting.

Hope this helps! Holler if you have questions!

Arcole

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-14T18:49:31+0200

### Arcole — 2014-01-15T22:32:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-105805>

Night 4 -- 1/14/14 --- Recovery

We were all a little wakeful, I'd say. However, I didn't get up to go pee and went right back to sleep after about 2-3 wakings. I did use the unit on Alert during the day as well. After 8 hours, I was alert and chirpy at 5:00. Energy levels today have been very good. My overall pain and stiffness rating is pretty good as well.

### Arcole — 2014-01-16T23:21:09+0200

Night 5 -- 1/15/14--- Recovery setting

Woke once to pee at 1:00 but went right back to sleep and did great on waking after 8 hours at 5:00. No grogginess, no desire to snooze. Aches and pains are some better I'd say. Everyone else seemed to sleep well and husband said at breakfast he felt good and had slept well. Did NOT use ALERT setting the day before. I am wondering if Alert during the day has any hangover for me on sleep during the night. If so, I am sad because I like the Alert setting. I can't tell any huge difference, but I do feel good when running it. I am doing without again today to continue the experiment.

### Arcole — 2014-01-23T18:33:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-106776>

Multiple day update from my log.

Night 6 1/16/14 -- Sleep 3 -- poor sleep but the 8 year old grandson in the bed with us was the culprit. No alert setting because my daughter took the second unit back to school with her.

Night 7 1/17/14 -- Sleep 3 -- sleep 9/10! no wake to pee.

Night 8 1/18/14 -- Sleep 3 -- no wake; sleep 10/10!! Recovery might have been just a little too high for either me or my husband but Sleep 3 seems much better.

Night 9 1/19/14 -- Sleep 3 -- no wake, sleep 10+. Great energy during the day.

Night 10 1/20/14 -- sleep 3 -- woke once to pee, but went right back to sleep. sleep 9/10. Weight down 2 pounds since beginning!

Night 11 1/21/14 -- sleep 3 -- didn't get up to pee but was a little restless, sleep 7/10; forgot to take my Melatonin and Magnesium (which is also oxide and not malate because I ran out and picked up what I could get quick). Other slightly restless nights might also have been nights when I didn't take my pills. I will do more experimentation to see exactly which element makes the most difference in sleep, the melatonin or the magnesium.

Night 12 1/22/14 -- sleep 3 -- took my pills! Didn't wake at all, 10+ sleep quality. No grogginess.

Plus, my arms haven't gone to sleep at night in several nights and this had been a regular nightly occurrence despite chiropractic care. Also my waking joint pain has gotten significantly better. I'm trying to avoid wheat and corn completely since wheat and particular is an irritant to my joints, but even with some odd consumption, my morning stiffness is much improved. Sleep 3 appears to be the right setting at this time for me and my husband, so I'm not going to mess with that for a while. My daughter also had to back down from Recovery to Sleep 3 due to wakefulness.

When I first began sleeping on the earthpulse, my Resting Breath Hold time was :58, which is about the usual for the past eight months. However, my check today put me at :51 seconds, so no improvment in that quarter yet. However, I'm not fretting about it. I'll just keep doing what I'm doing and check it again in another week. The website documentation states I should see an improvement in two weeks and if not, to contact the inventor. I've talked to him by phone once already and was very pleased with the interaction. Dr. K, he knows you as the "cold water guy" and admitted to not having kept up with your blog for a couple of years. I told him he needed to check it out because you both talk about so many of the same issues with mitochondrial function and magnetism.

Hope this helps somebody!!

### fitness@home — 2014-01-23T18:48:06+0200

### Arcole — 2014-01-30T23:12:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-107494>

Multiple day update again:

Night 13 1/23/14 -- sleep 3, wake once to pee, morning pain levels improving; 10 sleep quality,

Night 14 1/24/14 -- sleep 3, no wake at all, sleep quality 10; magnesium only, no melatonin

Night 15 1/25/14 -- sleep 3, no waking but slightly restless night 8/10 sleep, magnesium only

Night 16 1/26/14 -- sleep 3, no waking, sleep 8+/10; magnesium only

Night 17 1/27/14 -- sleep 3, one waking to pee, sleep 10/10, magnesium only

Night 18 1/28/14 -- sleep 3, no waking, sleep 10/10, magnesium only

Night 19 1/29/14 -- sleep 3, woke once to pee, sleep 9+/10 due to vivid dreams about snow (wonder why? LOL), magnesium only

Summary so far: My morning waking stiffness and pain has gradually improved until I'm down to very little stiffness and almost no pain in the morning in my feet and ankles. This has been despite the occasional dietary slip-up which I know makes things worse. With 10 being excellent, no pain, I've gone from steady 7s to 9s and 9+s. My sleep quality overall has also improved dramatically over the last few weeks. I've gone more nights in without waking to go to the bathroom than I have in years. Also, any wakings I've had have been short and I've had almost no trouble going to sleep and staying asleep. Morning wakings have been much, much better than before the Earthpulse. I don't feel groggy or want to keep snoozing. This is a major improvment. Also, I have done much better of late with the magnesium only. I'm going to stay off the melatonin and see if my sleep continues to be good without it.

My daughter has also been reporting good sleep on sleep 3, but says she sometimes feels groggy in the mornings. At least part of hers, she admits is the fact that she's a college student and gets to bed much later than she should. But she reports that her sleep is very sound.

My dog had thirteen baby teeth pulled yesterday, slept great last night, and today is playing and eating like nothing happened. I can't tell for sure that sleeping near the Earthpulse helped him recover, but I can't rule it out either. My husband also reports really sound sleep and has not been tossing and turning nearly as much as he used to. In fact, my sleep may be so much better because his is!

Resting breath hold: 1:01 -- shows nice improvement

### sooperb — 2014-01-30T23:33:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-107497>

This sounds as though it's been a really positive experience and a worthwhile investment, I am really pleased for you

### Arcole — 2014-01-30T23:33:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-107498>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=106800)
>
> I was surprised to read that you are taking melatonin?

I started taking 5 mg of melatonin several months ago after reading one of Jack's blog posts about supplements in hopes of improving my sleep. It did help, but the magnesium helped more. The Earthpulse plus the magnesium (about 455 mg as Magnesium Malate right at bedtime) has really helped my sleep dramatically. I have stopped the melatonin to see if my sleep continues to stay good without it. However, I'm hanging with the magnesium because it also helps my skin not be so dry. When I upped my dosage recently, my dry skin went away on my hands, even in this awful cold weather.

### Arcole — 2014-02-18T17:18:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111000>

This is a long overdue update. I will sum up.

Nights 20-34 all look practically identical. Sleep 3, rare wakings and no more than one, great sleep 9-10.
Nights 35-37 look less fantastic but I did it to myself. On Feb 6, I fell prey to Girl Scout Cookies and early Valentine's candy and over the next few days managed to sugar bomb and stress my immune system into the toilet. I got a very mild cold on Feb 9, which a week later turned into a fatigue and gland issue I haven't managed to shake. So my sleep is suffering, my pain is up, and my overall fatigue levels are bad. Yet I somehow managed to pull off a 1:05 breath hold time. I figured I wouldn't make 45 seconds!

That tells me a lot! Despite being sick, my overall sleep has been better even the last few days than it ever was before the Earthpulse. During crazy stressful nights of being woke up at 2:00 a.m. with a crisis, spending one night with my husband in the hospital (he's fine), I was understandably wakeful, but my overall sleep quality was so restorative I managed to keep functioning.

If I hadn't trashed my system with sugar, I'd be doing fabulous right now. So no more sugar! No matter the temptation!!

Also, I have just gotten my results back from 23andme and ran them through Sterling's app at the MTHFRsupport site. I'm +/+ for C677T and 13 other snps in the Methylation section. So tomorrow afternoon I have my first consult with Dr. Tim Jackson. Maybe this is a sign of improvement to come!!

On a side note, dealing with Earthpulse's inventor Paul Becker has been fabulous!!! He responds so quickly to emailed questions and has really stood by his product with me. It has been a pleasure to do business with him. Once I get some of my MTHFR issues in line, I will touch base with Paul about improving my breath hold times even more. However, I feel pretty confident that these stupid snps are giving my body grief right now. I just don't know how much grief.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T17:29:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111003>

That's a really positive review for your experience. Is your daughter's experience reflecting the same benefits, even though she perhaps abuses her sleep cycle by not going to sleep at a reasonable time? I'm keen to try one, well I am a gadget freak lol, but I can't bring myself to spend the extra for the custom's duty and tax.

### Arcole — 2014-02-18T17:39:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111008>

I would say my daughter does indeed abuse her sleep cycle and she knows it. She is trying to do better though since finding out she too has methylation issues. She is heterozygous for both C677T and A1298C as well as a number of others. But she does say that Sleep 3 has been a very good setting for her as well and she uses it nightly, which to me says a lot since college students aren't very good with consistency.

I'll get a real update from her soon and post. On the other front, my husband and dog are sleeping very well themselves. Now that my husband has changed some of his lifestyle habits, most especially with regard to diet and watching his blood pressure and blood sugar, I am hoping he will get even more benefit from it. It took a high blood pressure crisis to wake him up, but he's finally on the epi-paleo wagon with the rest of us. He's already lost 20 pounds too. Men. Sigh. All he had to do was give up the honey buns at last. I haven't had a stupid honey bun in seven years and can't lose 20 pounds.

### sooperb — 2014-02-18T17:57:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111019>

I sympathise.

We gave up carbs, mostly, two years ago this month. We both lost weight initially especially as we also did intermittent fasting. We slid backwards quite a lot after a few months, he'd lost over 21lbs and I lost about 14lbs, the most I'd lost in a long time. We needed to pull our socks up though but even being very low carb., no wheat, bread, potatoes, rice or pasta except once in a while, neither of us can lose and we both put most of the loss back. I started the reset but he isn't too interested but even so, 2 meals a day only, very few carbs, I managed to give up sugar completely but still no result. I made a start on CT, face dunking, abdomen icing and two baths so far and cold after warm showers. Still nothing doing. I am so surprised that cutting out sugar hasn't achieved any weight loss, I really thought it was going to be the winner but no. I haven't given up nor am I stoking myself with sugar laden food, it's do or die this time lol. It's a mystery to me although I'm sure Dr. Jack might have some sort of answer except he hasn't called into my journal yet lol.

### Arcole — 2014-02-25T00:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-111986>

Nights 38-42 --- My sleep for the past six weeks has been without a doubt deeper, longer, and more restorative than it has been in many years. Most nights I have slept like I did in my twenties. This has been a huge improvement. Lots of nights I don't wake at all and if I do, the waking is generally brief. Sometime I get up to go pee, sometimes not. Sometimes I sleep through 9 or 10 hours straight. In the mornings I am typically not groggy at all, which is also a huge improvement. The sleep 3 setting seems to knock us all out, me, husband, and dog, pretty much without fail. My husband in particular has stated that his sleep has dramatically improved. He used to have lots of sleepless nights, but no more.

My overall assessment personally of the Earthpulse is that I am not going to be sleeping without it, unless Dr. Kruse knows something awful about the way it works that I don't know. I believe that for me, it has filled in a piece of the puzzle with regard to sleep very nicely. I think it has some features to recommend it, price and portability springing to mind. I do not regret the purchase at all. I will update again for certain at 60 days and 90 days or if anything changes.

### sooperb — 2014-02-25T00:31:33+0200

### Terminator — 2014-02-25T05:45:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-earthpulse-n-1.9336/post-112052>

I love my earthpulse, I only use it when I travel and only sleep good in recover mode. I had to travel for Work and did not use earthpulse for first two nights and all except 1.5 hours of third night and my sleep was very bad except for 1.5 hour of earthpulse. Fourth night sleep was good. I cannot tolerate any sleep mode other than recover mode.

At home I don't use it. I don't have any wireless devices, shut down the power to entire house except Fridge & Furnace, I have 20 Gauss magnetico pads (bought used) on 13 inch mattress and sleep very good. I don't travel too often but next week I will travel to again and will use earthpulse every night in recover mode. I will try to report when i get back.

### Inger — 2014-01-14T20:56:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-and-the-military-from-a-former-agent.9338/post-105652>

This is all so sad.. it makes me mad. I called our electric company again today, as they seem to ignore me and my demanding them them to take the smart meter off. I sat and waited for so long at the telephone, and 3 times the person changed.. in the ends I got to tell my issue and she said she will forward it. WTH? I said I NEED TO TALK TO THEM WHO ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE METER AND WHO CAN PROMISE ME THEY COME AND TAKE IT OFF. Guess what she said to me? _There are no such person_. You just get stuck with them to the point they say they will call you back, and they do not call.

I just cannot believe this is possible at all in a country like Finland. But it is. If this was my house I would certainly kill the meter no doubt - but this is my parents house and I am scared to make a fire or kill the electricity all together in their house. After the talk with the company... I just cried. It is just so exhausting.. and it is just too sad. How can they do this? They are so weirdly polite... but they ignore you at the end. But I am not going to give up yet. I will keep bombarding them. Maybe... in the end I will take a sledgehammer. it is kind of calling my name in the garage

# Thread: Sandra's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 109
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sandra — 2014-01-16T01:07:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-105837>

Hi Everyone,

This is my first post on the forum though I have been lurking for a while and reading a lot about what Dr Kruse writes. I was interested when I saw the link to the 90 day re-set, so thought I should give it a go.

A bit about me ... I am from Australia, heading quickly towards 40 years of age, normal height and weight, have been eating a mostly paleo diet (no grain or sugar, but some dairy) for about 15 months, have had a magnetico mattress for around 8 weeks, and have installed an RO water filter in our home. I am trying to 'do all the right things' for me, as well as slowly transfer my husband and children to a more paleo life-style (all 3 are big grain eaters).

I don't have any major health issues (that I am aware of) and am hoping none arise, though from what I read, they can kind of sneak up on you!!

One thing that I do want to sort out is my tiredness. It's not chronic, doesn't stop me from doing anything, have had it for ages (I remember friends telling me I was always tired and yawning during my teenage years at school) and I am always yawning and not feeling totally energised. I thought that a paleo diet might change that, and that the magnetico might as well, but early days. I did have some tests done at the GP some time ago and I was iron deficient - not anaemic, but still low iron. I elected to try to eat more iron rich foods rather than to take supplements as the GP suggested. I didn't go back for a follow up test, but presume my iron is still low as I am still tired. Just talking to a naturopath today and she suggests that one reason for tiredness is that there might be some foods I am eating that react with my gut and do not allow the iron to be absorbed properly ... hopefully I'll know more next week after some tests. I am assuming it might be dairy as I was told 7 years ago by a kinesiologist (while he was testing my daughter for allergies) that I was intolerant to dairy. I didn't know enough about it then but the more I read about paleo and after discussions with the naturopath, it won't surprise me if that is it. So, I am hoping for an easy solution, otherwise it's onto round 2 to try to get to the bottom of it.

I have not had any other tests done that have been mentioned on the forum or by Dr Kruse ... maybe that's my next step. I've also not thought too much about CT - perhaps I should give that a go too??

That's all for now! I hope I have this in the right section, and looking forward to chatting with you.

Cheers

### Sandra — 2014-01-23T12:04:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106736>

Just home from an interesting trip to the Naturopath, who also does Kinesiology …

I'm still getting my head around the alternative medical practices, but the more I read and experience, the more I think there might be something in it.

To cut a long story short, she thinks my body reacts to gluten, corn, peanuts, soy (all at an amount of 10/10), dairy (5/10) and oysters (2/10). And she thinks I have candida, which apparently would explain my sub-optimal energy for many many years. She thinks that the candida has created a leaky gut which leads to bloating and inflammation in my body, and also the pain in my shoulders (from no apparent injury).

With any of these alternative therapies, I wonder if I divulged too much information about how I was feeling and certain ailments before the session, and was she just coming to the conclusions based on what I said … but I don't think so, as I could feel differences as she was testing the various vials.

So, I guess I have nothing to lose by giving it a go … a 1 month course to get rid of the candida, and then a 1 month course of pro-biotics to build up the gut flora again. I don't eat any grains, corn, peanuts and soy any more (for the last 12 months), and have a very small amount of dairy, so might cut that out totally for the next month and see how it goes.

I'd be interested to hear if anyone else has had experiences with kinesiology and what they thought of it. Thank you

### Sue-UK — 2014-01-23T13:47:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106740>

> [Sandra said:](/goto/post?id=106736)
>
> I'd be interested to hear if anyone else has had experiences with kinesiology and what they thought of it.

I remember over 25 years ago taking my eldest child to a kinesiologist because of hyperactivity problems, and he tested various foods and my son was put on a special diet. It worked brilliantly for a while, (physically at least, but emotionally very difficult), and then problems started again, and it was found at the second session that heâ€™d become â€œallergicâ€ to things he was OK with before. Each session cost a weekâ€™s wages we couldnâ€™t really spare to see this very respected naturopath/kinesiologist. What I've since learned is that it can tell me what, at that particular moment, is going on about certain things tested, but it doesnâ€™t give the whole picture, and most importantly - it doesn't tell me why. My sonâ€™s hyperactivity wasnâ€™t caused by a lack of the faddy diet I unfortunately put him on.  That set us back 2 years, and made things worse for a lot longer than that.

I've personally had machine bioresonance testing rather than kinesiology, but a machine still has limitations. For example I tested badly for milk, so I nipped up to the local shop and got some non-homogenised local full fat liquid milk, and the result was completely different. (Fortunately the therapist is a friend who is on my wavelength about experimentation and encourages people to bring things in they want testing.) The test vial was dried milk of unknown origin.

So although I would say that its fascinating that someone can experience the energetic difference when certain things are tested, I believe its a response to the contents of the vial, on the biologic/energetic terrain at that moment.

### Sandra — 2014-01-24T09:04:47+0200

### caroline — 2014-01-24T09:31:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106885>

Sandra - personally I would say the GP is a D\*\*k. I have had probably 6 hsCRP done thru medicare. The ones they don't cover is rT3 and prenenolone and they are about $50.00 each.

Sad to say - you need to find another Doc. Did I tell you.... I found my Doc by asking the pharmacist at a compounding pharmacy. They are perfectly fine with giving you names of Docs who are writing scripts for BHRT... at least you will know they are interested in functional medicine. After - the pharmacist asked me for feedback because someone else had asked her for names. She was moving here from out of town.

You may also need to fib a little .... like make up a back history ....maybe a Mom Or Grandmother who had TD2 or thyroid issue or whatever. You need to think outside the box to get what you need.

The one test that is harder to get here is IGF1 .... medicare comes down hard on Docs if they ask for that test too often. I have had it done twice in the last 18 months. My Doc just has to make up his little story on the requisition to cover himself.

Your Doctor should be a partner - don't settle for anything less!

How are you? Say Hi to Nick!

### Sandra — 2014-01-24T12:37:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106897>

I was just reading a few of the earlier blogs. One spoke about inheriting genes from your mother and grandmother that affect mitochondria and metabolism, so thought I'd write it down ...

maternal grandmother
\- very strong and healthy all of her life
\- grew up as a farmers wife and worked hard
\- had the biggest fruit and vegetable patch I've ever seen
\- majority of fruits, vegetable and meats were home grown/raised, and meat would have been mainly grass fed, probably lots of organ meat (very little waste of anything as there was not a lot of money to go around and 7 kids in the family)
\- probably not much fish eaten
\- everything home baked, and there was bread, cakes and biscuits
\- very lean body composition in all photos I ever saw of her in the younger days, and until the end
\- walked a lot for daily exercise
\- died aged 87 from a stroke which they think was brought on as a side effect of an eye operation

mother
\- growing up with the food mentioned above
\- quite slim until after I was born
\- started to put on weight and become quite overweight, though not obese, from when I was around 10
\- now has thyroid problems (and is on medication), probably from diet as there is a lot of dairy, grains and sugar, though she still home-cooks most things. Also a lot of beer and no exercise.

### Sandra — 2014-01-24T13:43:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106903>

... my mum likes line dancing too!! ... you can take the girl out of the bush, but you can't take the bush out of the girl!!

I don't think the grains would have been locally milled - possibly, but not sure.

All the kids were really healthy early on, but now that they range from around 70+ to 50+, they are starting to have issues.

The eldest male, 72, had been going through chemo and radiation starting about 3 years ago for prostate cancer (clear now).

The next oldest female, 70, has been healthy all her life, married a farmer, home baked and grew fruit/veggies, walked every day for 10kms, and has recently developed high cholesterol - resisted medication for as long as she could, tried natural medication, but just within the last 12 months started to take something prescribed by the DR as it wouldn't come down (I don't know any more specifics than that, and when it was happening I didn't really know any of this stuff to help her out more).

Next child, male, 67, quite healthy, still lives on the farm, don't know much more bout him.

Next child, male, 65, slightly overweight, but still active, and don't know a lot about him either!

Next is my aunt who is 63. I think she's OK, but has been concerned lately with something in her eyes ... not cataracts or glaucoma, can't remember it!

Then it's my mum, 60, overweight, thyroid issues ...

And lastly my aunt, 58, periods of overweightedness but does Weight Watchers - otherwise I think OK.

I think my mum's pregnancies were all good, with me it was fine, natural delivery. I grew up on a farm too (a dairy farm!) - until I was about 18. Again, we had a big veggie patch, had cattle, sheep, pigs, chooks on the farm, so all meat was grass fed. Mum baked a lot too, so very little processed foods, but still dairy and grain. My 2 pregnancies were all fine, fell pregnant very easily and natural births. Actually, there was one thing that I never really got to the bottom of with #2 (girl, now turning 8) ....

... when I was pregnant with my daughter (2nd child) we went out to dinner at a seafood restaurant (have always loved seafood - esp prawns!!). I was about 6-8 months pregnant and ate a bowl full of the best seafood - fish, shell fish, crustaceans ... YUM!! The next day by entire baby bump was itchy and covered in a hives rash. It went away by the afternoon. I was concerned and went to the doctor who couldn't tell me anything except that I probably had a reaction to the seafood I'd eaten the night before (eventhough I'd eaten it all my life). I didn't eat shell fish or crustaceans for the rest of my pregnancy.

FAST FORWARD 6 months and my daughter has an allergic reaction to egg (which we initially thought was a heat rash ... itchy hives on chest, but went away on its own). Another 6 months later, and she has a reaction to peanut butter - same hives as the egg reaction, but this time was on chest, tummy, back and face. Raced her to our nearest doctor, 5 doors down the hill, and he watched as the rash went away within the hour with no effect on breathing. We then ordered specialist tests (standard scratch test on forearm) to which we were told she was allergic to eggs, nuts and shellfish!

So at 1 year of age, she had also had eczema all of her life. Doctors said not to worry about the eczema as she was still putting on weight and developing as per their charts (eventhough I thought it was strange she was vomitting a lot after I would breastfeed her). I questioned them weather it would be because of something I was eating, but they didn't think so. During this time of testing, we were not happy with the specialist really - yep, she's allergic to eggs, nuts and shell fish, so see you in 12 months for another test! It was now I had my first experience with a kinesiologist referred by a friend. He told us she was allergic to eggs, peanuts, shellfish, salicylates, dairy and grain - WOWSERS!!! I cried all the way home as we'd just booked a family trip to Italy! So, we took her off dairy and grain, and within 2 weeks her skin was perfect - no eczema. We continued this for 2 months and it was all good. This is back way before I'd even heard of paleo, and we thought "how can she live like this (no grain, no dairy)?" so we slowly started to add them back into her diet. Everything was 'fine' unless she overdosed on either and would get little hives in the insides of her elbows or on her ankles ... still does today.

She can eat eggs now, along with all other nuts, but not peanuts (and we haven't tried that one ... probably no need to now!). I suspect shellfish are OK too, but haven't actually fed any to her, though she has eaten oyster sauce in stir-frys. And the salicylates, dairy and grain are now back in her diet too. She is one that I would really like to get off grain, especially knowing what I do now!

I've always wondered whether it was my daughter reacting with the seafood I at while pregnant with her, and is it because of me that she's allergic, eventhough I'm not? It must be related somewhere, but no-one has ever said how or why ...

### sooperb — 2014-01-25T00:07:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-106933>

When I was 8 months pregnant with my first I also grew a rash all over my bump that itched like mad. I was told by the doctor in the clinic that it was a general urticaria quite common in pregnancy and that it would go away after delivery. He was right it did and I didn't get it with my next two. Strangely though, that daughter developed chronic asthma at two and a half and has it to this day. She's had a host of other problems too. Her second daughter has eczema although she breast fed her for a long time and the first up indication of allergy problems was her first taste of kiwi at less than a year old which saw her face and ears swell alarmingly. Fortunately that went down on it's own while she was in the hospital.

Lots of things caused her problems, cows milk, cola drinks (both of which brought a red rash up round her mouth), sweets with bright colours. As soon as her mum put two and two together she started being very careful about what she let her eat so her eczema never got too bad but even now, at 14, too much of the wrong thing has her scratching at herself.

### Sandra — 2014-01-29T12:46:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-107292>

I think my Leptin Reset essentially started today as I was starting to eat my BAB's within 1 hr of waking ... as of today, it's 1/2 an hour!!

So, some stats ...

38 year old female

weight - 65kg (143 pds)

height - 165cm (5ft 5in)

a bit of a tummy pudge but otherwise I'm OK with my body shape

2 kids, aged 9 and 7

I'd like to get to the bottom of my tiredness ... not critical, but less than optimal, and the more I read, the more I wonder if I have adrenal or hormone issues too. Again, I'm not feeling they are at a critical level, but something is off (and I guess this means something could be critical at some time in the future!)

no labs back yet ... tracked down some from a few years ago and had a few done last week - shall post results when I get them. I couldn't get all the ones I wanted due to the doctor not feeling they were in relation to my 'tiredness' which is what I went to see her about ... time for a new doc, I know - working on that.

Have been eating Paleo for about 12 months, including a little dairy, but have decided to remove all dairy now to see if that makes any difference.

My work and home life is reasonably stress-free, and although it is constant and I don't have a lot of down-time, I don't feel overwhelmed with anything. I work 2 days a week at home, 3 days away from home, have a few after school activities for the kids, husband works from home and life if pretty flexible and balanced.

Usual daily timeframe ...
5am - wake up
5:30am to 6am - brekky
6am to 3pm - work
11am - lunch
3:30pm - my dinner
3pm to 7pm - kids homework, activities, dinner prep
8:30/9pm - bed

Sleep is pretty good ... if I wake up during the night, I go back to sleep pretty quickly. Usually fall asleep alright and wake just before my 5am alarm. Always yawn and feel tired during the day, even if I get more sleep. I have a magnetico mattress but can't say I've noticed any great difference since having it (around 2 months now).

I use the blue-blocker glasses too, and doing it religiously now while on the Leptin Reset.

Is there anything else I should note?

### Sandra — 2014-01-30T02:18:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-107378>

I doubt I'm peri menopausal as I have hardly any of the symptoms (listed below) but if there's other things you think point to it, I'd love to hear it. I am still on the pill so could that mask it, and I'm 38 ... is that early?

Perhaps I should seek out an ASI test. I mentioned "adrenal" to the GP when I had my blood tests ordered last week and she told me that medicine doesn't deal with adrenal issues and I'd need to talk to the naturopath about that. I want to get the other test results back but really don't feel like I can go back and talk to her - I'm not going to get anywhere!

35 Symptoms of Perimenopause

Hot flashes, hot flushes, night sweats and/or cold flashes, clammy feeling
Irregular heart beat
Irritability
Mood swings, sudden tears
Trouble sleeping through the night (with or without night sweats)
Irregular periods; shorter, lighter periods; heavier periods, flooding; phantom periods, shorter cycles, longer cycles
Loss of libido
Vaginal dryness
Crashing fatigue
Anxiety, feeling ill at ease
Feelings of dread, apprehension, doom
Difficulty concentrating, disorientation, mental confusion
Disturbing memory lapses
Incontinence, especially upon sneezing, laughing; urge incontinence
Itchy, crawly skin
Aching, sore joints, muscles and tendons
Increased tension in muscles
Breast tenderness
Headache change: increase or decrease
Gastrointestinal distress, indigestion, flatulence, gas pain, nausea
Sudden bouts of bloat
Depression
Exacerbation of existing conditions
Increase in allergies
Weight gain
Hair loss or thinning, head, pubic, or whole body; increase in facial hair
Dizziness, vertigo, light-headedness, episodes of loss of balance
Changes in body odor
Electric shock sensation under the skin and in the head
Tingling in the extremities
Gum problems, increased bleeding
Burning tongue, burning roof of mouth, bad taste in mouth, change in breath odor
Osteoporosis (after several years)
Changes in fingernails: softer, crack or break easier
Tinnitus: ringing in ears, bells, 'whooshing,' buzzing etc.

### Sandra — 2014-02-06T21:20:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-108738>

Thought I'd add a mini timeline of events that might relate to some lab changes ...

Nov 2011 - first labs to go off ... while eating my 'normal' 37 year diet ... not overly bad I'd say, but still full of grains and dairy, though not a lot of sugar. Around June of 2011 I scared myself on the scales, and while I've never been overweight in my life, I was 69.9kgs ... and I swore I would no way be 70, so started to cut out snacking on junk then (so about 6 months before first labs)

pre-Oct 2012 - worked from home in our business, very flexible schedule, not a lot of computer time, lots of outside time in the veggie garden , also walked at least 5 times a week ... not power-walking, about 6kms in an hour, and did some swimming

Oct 2013 - started an office job 3 days a week, in front of a computer all day. much less exercise

Jan 2013 - started eating Paleo but still included some dairy, and I was snacking (I know that's not good now!)

June, July, August 2013 - trip to Europe, stuck 95% to eating Paleo, walked a lot, lost 3kgs - down to sub 62kgs (though back to 65kg pretty quickly ... maybe exercise??)

Aug 2013 - changed pill to a lower dose on Dr's advice due to risks associated with cancer and clotting on the one I was taking for so long (considering ditching it altogether now)

Nov 2013 - installed home and work RO water system, and got a 10 gauss magnetico mattress. Love drinking the water, and drink 4-5 Litres a day, but can't say I feel any noticeable difference from the magnetico yet (after 3 months)

Jan 25, 2014 - started Leptin Reset

Soooo, maybe the big change in my BUN/Creatine ratio could be do do with working in an office, change of pill, or lack of exercise???

### Sandra — 2014-02-10T12:01:20+0200

More tests ordered last week ... Vit D, Vit B12, RF, ANA (ENA and DNA if applicable), ELFTs, Coeliac Screen, PCR and M/C/S

Still sticking to the BAB's - no desire to snack, and happy with 2 meals a day. Not really feeling any different to before but was also on pretty low carb and no sugar

No pill (Levlen) as of Friday and I'll be getting some hormone testing done in about 6-8 weeks time, but now I have a headache ... I never get headaches

The funny thing is that I haven't noticed my fingers sore since I stopped the pill ... is that weird?? It's only been a few days, and I can't say I specifically remembered to check my fingers when I woke up, but the fact that I didn't do something to hurt them makes me think that they might be improving.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-02-10T21:02:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-109470>

### Sandra — 2014-05-03T06:58:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124833>

It's been a while since i've been here but have been continuing on.

Still eating epi-paleo and stayed on the lepton reset for 4 weeks. No weight loss and no real physical change but I'm ok with that - 5kgs would have been nice though

Had some hormon blood test and they were all fine, in limits, with nothing the GP is concerned with.

Shoulder is still sore for no apparent reason so went off for another round of xrays ultrasounds and even a neck MRI. Nothing showed up except inflation in one of my shoulder bursas. They suggested a cortisone injection ... It's been sore for three years - so I went for it. Was great for a week or so then back to usual soreness again! Then Nick told me about the shoulder thread on here - whoa!!! That's me  so the next step ... Bone broth and CT - I want to fix this up and want to try anything that might work. The broth tastes awesome and is so filling. CT I'm working up to ... we have a swimming pool and it's 24 deg C at the moment. I know it's no 10 degrees but it's under body temp so I'm working with that at the moment. Have been I'm for 3 days straight for 1/2 hour each time. I shivered and shook the whole first time - was very weird, but never felt cold. 2nd time less shaking, and third time I swam around instead of reading and didn't shake at all. There was a temperature drop last night so hopefully it's getting colder. I'll keep on with the 10deg face dunks too and see what I notice over the next week. No change with my shoulder yet but having great sleeps and dreams the past three nights ... do things happen that quickly??

There's a quick recap and hopefully I'll be around more now - have a lot of reading to catch up on.

### Sandra — 2014-05-03T06:59:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124834>

Oh, and we made a big batch of sauerkraut that I've been munching my way through since the antibiotics. It tastes soooo good! And was very easy to make

### caroline — 2014-05-04T00:33:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124933>

Hellllooooo!

How did you make the cabbage?

### Sandra — 2014-05-04T01:36:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124935>

It was pretty easy actually ... just cabbage and salt, and lots of strong hands

### Sandra — 2014-05-04T01:44:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-124936>

There are a few recipes floating around out there, but basically you slice the cabbage really thinly. For each kg of cabbage, sprinkle over 1 tablespoon of salt - best to do this in lots of 500g we found. I think we scrunched about 5kgs of cabbage in one sitting and filled about 8 1L Mason jars. I was lucky to have 2 sets of big man hands helping me too

Once you have sprinkled the salt on the cabbage, scrunch it with your hands. Really squeeze and squish it. After about 5-10 minutes, you'll notice the juice coming out of it. Once your cabbage has gone really soggy and you have lots of juice, time to jar it.

Sterilise your jars earlier, then (once the jars are cool) fill them with the sauerkraut. Leave about 2cm from the top of the jar and then fill with the juice. Don't seal it yet.
Then you need to ferment it for 3-10 days. I did 3 days as it was reasonably warm. Optimum temp is 20-24 deg C (or about 65-75 F). Push it down every few hours or use a weight to press it down. You'll see bubbles start to form - this is good. Sometimes there might be scum on top, so scoop this off (I didn't get scum, but I don't think it's a bad thing if you do). You can taste as you go too.

Once it's done, seal it up. If you live in a cold area you could store it on the shelf, but I didn't want to risk it, so put it in the fridge.

It's nice and crispy and great with grass fed sausages and mustard

I don't think this is the exact recipe I used, but looks familiar ...

[http://www.thekitchn.com/how-to-mak...on-jar-cooking-lessons-from-the-kitchn-193124](http://www.thekitchn.com/how-to-make-easy-homemade-sauerkraut-in-a-mason-jar-cooking-lessons-from-the-kitchn-193124)

### Sandra — 2014-06-06T00:35:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-129878>

We were talking about the underwire bra issues a few days ago amongst some friends and the question was raised about rings (jewellery). I have been having issues with my fingers for about 6 months ... waking up in the mornings with stiff fingers. After a few minutes of stretching they seem OK, until the next morning. Nothing showed up in x-rays and MRIs of my neck, massage and physio on my arm and shoulders didn't seem to make a difference. Well, after our discussion 3 days ago, I have not been wearing my gold rings (on 3 fingers on one hand and 2 fingers on my left hand) ... and no more stiffness!!

Is that a bit weird, is it coincidence, or is there a good reason for the gold in my rings somehow causing my fingers to have these issues???

Interestingly I also wear a fairly heavy gold fob chain around my neck and I have started to have shoulder issues. I'm wondering if they are all related (amongst other things as well....).

So, I'm experimenting with wearing no jewellery during the day and none to bed anymore, and will take notice of how things go.

### Sandra — 2015-02-21T11:40:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156210>

a few strange observations lately ...
\- 'itchy' throat (inside)
\- itchy knees (on the front surface)

\- still tired and some bloating (situation normal!)

\- shoulders so much better (I put that down to the new latex mattress from last July)

\- started to take a magnesium supplement as advised by naturopath and am also taking some drops to flush out lymph nodes in the armpit area (which showed up on a test as being blocked - maybe due to deodorant or potential link to breast tissue)

\- currently waiting on results from a hair allergy test - not sure how accurate this will be but thought I'd give it a try to see if there are any more clues there. Naturopath thinks tiredness might be due to a food allergy and thinks my stomach area is very inflamed. I will also attempt to keep a food diary.

### caroline — 2015-02-22T00:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156247>

Good morning Sandra! how was your get together? dying for details and pics!

### Sandra — 2015-02-22T13:49:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156277>

It was great!! Jac has some pics which I'm sure Nick will post soon. I even ate prawn shells as Yew told me to  Lots of little clues just keep piecing together and I never would have worked it all out myself... so grateful to have found this forum and the wonderful people who are part of it.

### Sandra — 2015-02-22T14:04:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156278>

Just some other notes for me ...
\- quite tight calf muscles for the last couple of months
\- feel tired tonight but can't sleep. Have been using blue blockers again regularly. Tonight is quite hot so have now put on the ceiling fan and I wonder if it might also have something to do with getting into the pool which was cooler than usual after all the rain??? I tend to get 'hot' after my limited attempt at CT - not that of class today as CT really - maybe it's just a hot night! I also fell and bashed my knee and ankle quite badly as I ungracefully tripped into the pool. Have had it in ice for a few hours so maybe a little extra heat and discomfort there too.

### caroline — 2015-02-22T23:02:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-156313>

Hi Sandra - so glad you had a great time!

### Sandra — 2015-03-04T12:38:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157403>

I've been keeping a good diary for around 10 days now. It's amazing shat you don't want to eat if you have to write it down!! Aside from no grain or sugar which is fairly normal for me now I am also not eating dairy or eggs. Something gives me tummy pains and bloating and I suspect it's eggs or dairy. The good thing is that I have lost cms and maybe even inches from my waist line!! I've never been one for weighing and measuring but when I put ony clothes and notice they are loose, and I feel good, then something must be right!

However I'm also taking some supplements and doing a bit of exercise so I don't know exactly what's working but I guess it's good anyway.

Just had 4 days in Rotorua and a funny observation re my fingers (which are usually a bit tight and sore every morning) ... they weren't in Rotorua! I did have a hot natural spa and sauna every night while away though ... wonder if that has anything to do with it. Also noticed that my period was very heavy just for day 1 and day 2 and then it was over - wonder if the hot spa had anything to do with that! (usually for last 12 months day 1 heavy and day 2-4 light) (and pre paleo and in the pill was always day 1-3 heavy and day 4-7 light).

Also been eating fresh sashimi every day or other day and two meals a day rather than 3 - just don't feel like I need to eat 3 and I seem to have more energy.

I'll keep noting and see what happens

### caroline — 2015-03-04T23:25:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157452>

Good morning Sandra!

### Sandra — 2015-03-05T23:10:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-157572>

I also seem to be eating just two meals a day which is a bit odd for me Breakfast seems to be around 8 or 9 am and is quite a good size (3 sausages and some sauteed cabbage and veggies) and then lunch/dinner is around 3 or 4pm. I seem to have plenty of energy, am getting lots of sleep and feel great, so I guess that's OK. I'll just keep going down this path I think..

I've also noticed that perhaps I have a bit of nut issue going on too. I bake 'paleo' treats in waves, freeze them, eat them (not between meals as snacks but as part of the meal). When I don't do that I feel much better ... lots of little observations that might just add up to something big later on.

### Sandra — 2015-03-24T08:54:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-159343>

Confused ... I had an intolerance/allergy test done with a hair sample. Some people totally disagree with this testing and others seem to see merit in it, and even have the results line up with 'standard' medical tests.

So, I thought I'd give it a go and see it I found any more answers. I already had a couple of suspicions concerning dairy, eggs, red wine and chocolate. I was pretty sure about gluten.

I ran a little experiment on myself over the past few weeks after meeting Yew. I kept a food diary and waited for the results to arrive. During this time I avoided eggs, diary, chocolate and gluten. I was amazed with how I felt - lots more energy and not tired, as well as having far less abdominal bloating, and even some weight loss (about 3-4 kgs out of 69kgs)

I received the results today. I must admit I didn't really do a lot of research into what food types would be tested but just went on the naturopath's advice that perhaps it would be worth getting done.

These are the items that came as having 'tested reactive for your immune system and must be avoided' ...

\- celery
\- olives
\- shallots
\- avocado
\- cucumber
\- onion
\- tomato
\- cashews

\- lavish bread (no surprise there - gluten)
\- white bread (no surprise there - gluten)
\- wholemeal bread (no surprise there - gluten)

\- Jatz biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- milk arrowroot biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- são biscuits (no surprise there - gluten)
\- select chocolate creams (no surprise there - gluten)
\- vita wheat original (no surprise there - gluten)

\- pasta durham wheat (no surprise there - gluten) ... but not couscous - I thought that was made out of the same stuff and contained gluten?
\- white flour (no surprise there - gluten)
\- wholemeal flour (no surprise there - gluten)

\- chocolate (darrell lea brand)
\- chocolate (lindt brand)
\- chocolate (nestle brand)
\- chocolate (cadbury brand)
\- chocolate (dove brand) ..... assuming these commercial chocolates contain a soy product as carob and cocoa were fine
\- Licorice (no surprise there - gluten)

\- quite a few corn, oat, bran and wheat cereals - no major problem for me as I avoid these anyway ... but rice bran, rice bubbles, oat bran and polenta were OK

\- brown sugar
\- white sugar
\- raw sugar

\- beer (no loss)
\- red wine (what!!!!!!!)

\- apricots
\- bananas
\- kiwifruit
\- pawpaw

\- cola
\- drinking chocolate (cadbury)
\- fanta
\- ginger ale
\- milo (contains gluten)
\- ovaltine (contains gluten)

\- garlic
\- gelatine

\- shellfish (what!!!!!!)

\- ayam oyster sauce
\- soy sauce
\- tomato sauce
\- tomato paste
\- worcester sauce

\- butter
\- cows cheese
\- cows yoghurt
\- cream
\- eggs
\- icecream
\- full cream milk (but A2 is OK)
\- skim milk
\- soy milk

\- tofu
\- tempey

\- pork (no!!!! - I love bacon and crackling!!) - funnily enough, by avoiding eggs recently, I also avoided bacon ... and felt great

\- dove deodorant
\- mum deodorant

\- polyester (funny - I've always hated wearing this)

\- cigarette smoke (I've always hated this too - tried smoking once when I was 17 and vomited horribly ... never did it again and now can't stand the smell)

I'm wondering if anyone else has ever had this DNA Hair Allergy testing done, were the results in line with other tests you had done, did you feel better after avoiding the foods they suggested to avoid.

I'm also interested in what the connections are between the various foods, especially the fruit and vegetables. I thought eggplant might be off the list being a nightshade, as tomato is, but eggplant seemed to be fine.

I'm also wondering if this is a 'forever' thing, or if one day I'll be able to eat these foods again. I simply love shellfish and avocado ....

Thanks for any advice in advance.
Sandra

### Sandra — 2018-01-17T12:49:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-232716>

How time flies in the blink of an eye ... nearly three years later and I'm still popping in from time to time to read up on things but rarely commenting. Loving all the inquisitive minds and advice here. Thank you everyone

And the good news is my breast MRI was all clear and I feel and see no effects from the contrast gadolinium.

I am excited to be going on a 3 day comedy cruise to laugh with friends, chill out by the pool and eat seafood.

### Sandra — 2024-02-29T02:31:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332036>

Hi Strangers - it's been a while ... 6 years! Good to see this site is still here. It's like coming back to a wise neighbour. Funny how we put up with things to a point then need them solved. I am still having the tiredness issues - not stopping me doing anything but just not optimal. I have tracked down a functional GP who seems really good. Just one meeting so far. She started talking about circadian biology and I said I knew about that as I had followed a US neurosurgeon for years but just hadn't really implemented it all practically. She asked who it was ... so I saved her telling me all about Uncle Jack - yay!! I felt she'd be the right one after that. So here I go again ... but now incorporating every sunrise with barefoot grounding - 7 days in a row now. She looked at all my symptoms and said thyroid issues for sure so is running the full thyroid panel, as well as hormones. I'm tracking everything I do and eat and how I feel.

I have a question ... if it is my thyroid, and since thyroid controls the hormones, and if I'm getting a big energy slump on day 1-3 of my cycle, how do I fix this and feel better? I just feel like I need to eat something to get some energy, but when I look at all the food I'm eating (100-120g protein a day, good fat, some salad vegetables, the odd piece of fruit), I can't possibly be hungry. Do I actually need energy? And in the past when I felt like this and I ate, it didn't fix the feeling of that energy slump. So if it's hormones, how do I feel better and get rid of the low energy feeling. I've emailed the new doctor but she works on her own and may not get back to me quickly. Next appt is end of April. Any ideas here would be great to manage me through. Thanks

### Sandra — 2024-02-29T02:58:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332037>

Day 1 of new work from home office ... outdoor grounding

### Sandra — 2024-03-23T07:10:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332775>

It's my birthday today ... new year, new me. Started with sunrise on the beach and a swim. Total bliss! I'm reading a book "Younger Next Year" ... mid life crisis time coming up and I'm doing what I can to avoid that! I'm making improvements in my health, redox/circadian biology, food, lifestyle - my gift to me is to continuously improve this. I'm starting to feel better, people are starting to notice - it will be a great year! Whilst I have big gaps in my time spent on this site, I am so grateful for you all for sharing your stories and advice to create an amazing resource, and to Jack for being the light!

### Anthony Smith — 2024-03-23T18:17:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sandras-optimal-journal.9352/post-332785>

### Nick — 2014-01-29T10:10:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/waving-hi-from-australia.9353/post-107269>

Hi Caroline, Dan the 3yo is going well, we got a lot more allergy testing done on him just before christmas and we are still making good progress. In less than 6 months we have brought back a lot of allergies. I am going to type these tests into in spreadsheet that I am happy to share as soon as I pull my finger out.

We have just bought a 10G Magnetico for him and will give the 7yo the 5G version. I am very keen to see what more progress we can make while he is still so young. We just had a terrific trip to Canada, a holiday of a lifetime, he survived that including many days of ski lessons which was a big relief. No problems with any of our energy levels! I have attached a photo of him, hopefully it works.

Sandra is great to talk to and is very smart, I am not sure about co opting her journal though, lol!!!!

### Nick — 2014-02-03T07:50:59+0200

>
> you broke him? were you not optimal when you became pregnant?

We broke him after the pregnancy I think, he was born very healthy in all respects but developed Allergies and Eczema at 6 months. In that time up to when he was three we had him sitting in a 12MGauss field from a fridge through the wall plus he was also next to 2 iPads, 2 iPhones and a wireless home phone right on the other side of the wall to his bed. It was only because of the info from JK about EMF that we cottoned onto this. Up until then the testing was progressively worse every time and the best the Dr's could recommend was to use yet another steroid cream or use wet wraps. My wife is part of a FB eczema group and there are so many kids that are getting worse all the time with many mothers now using chemo to try and reverse the eczema. You can see why I am so grateful to Jack. We have avoid this, managed to fix a fair bit up quickly plus improved the health of the entire family along the way. We don't spend much time with regrets about what we did, can't change that now, but we feel very responsible for coming up for a solution as best as we can.

# Thread: Dairy - beta casein - A1 vs A2 milk.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Da-mo — 2014-01-16T23:38:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105966>

Having read some of the Leptin and Epi-paleo blogs, I see JK seeming to advocated limiting or eliminating dairy unless you can get it from France or New Zealand - I presume that has to do with being pasture fed. I've also seen him mention the beta-casein issue which I interpret as being about the A1 and A2 milk differences.

Here in New Zealand, yes our dairy and indeed most food animals are pasture fed. However, most of the dairy herds produce A1 milk.

Luckily, we can get A2 milk here and our family switched over to it several years ago. Initially it was very expensive compared with non-A2 milk but has now come down to the price of the A1. I read somewhere that the dairy industry here is switching over to A2 herds but that will likely take a decade or so.

As for the effects on my family. . . . the most noticeable was that prior to switching to A2 milk, both my daughters would get debilitating hayfever in the summer to the point they could hardly function without antihistamines up to the eyeballs. Since making the switch, their hayfever is no more noticeable than anyone else that may get dust or pollen in their eyes. Both also were on asthma medication (Ventolin) but since switching to A2 neither has needed any at all.

Unfortunately the other dairy derived products here (cheese, yoghurt etc.) do not distinguish between A1 and A2 feedstocks so are probably still A1 derived.

Thoughts/Comments/Opinion/Discussion?

### margor_j — 2014-01-16T23:44:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105967>

thats interesting..
i recently listened to a podcast by sean croxton (underground wellness) where he was interviewing a dairy farmer in NZ, who said most of our milk here is A2, so i kept taking the raw milk (iv since stopped)
im using a 'red 8' whey protein - trying it.. now im wondering if im using the wrong one, confusion starts all over again.. but to get 50gm of protein at b'fast AND cut back on eggs leaves less choice (where i am right now, its not so easy to get up and cook up 3 steaks etc..)

so youre saying that alot of our milk is A1 now? hmm this guy being interviewed (sorry, dont know name) was saying we are one of the lucky countries, because we have mainly A2...

MG

### Da-mo — 2014-01-17T00:15:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105968>

Last I read, Fonterra was switching the herds over to A2, while simultaneouly refuting any claims that A1 was harmful . . . hahaha!!

THe A2 is now the same price as most A1 so Im sticking with the A2 labelled stuff.

Still reading whats in the forum about it and saw something about no dairy until FBG <85?, need to research that a little more and start thinking about getting some labs done.

Was interesting reading some of your introduction - had a freind who went to Antartica with the Air Force. He and a freind from the Army got in trouble for talking a pool toy, an inflatable seal, out onto the ice and baiting the greenies by beating it with broomsticks at a distance where it looked real.

### Jack Kruse — 2014-01-17T02:40:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105972>

Keith Woodford. Devil in the milk. Also I spoke in some blog comments in the BG series with a Bob Smith about BCM-7 and the casein link to DPPIV in the intestine. I am sure someone has a link to the discussion. It was deep.

### margor_j — 2014-01-17T03:59:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dairy-beta-casein-a1-vs-a2-milk.9364/post-105982>

oh thats funny re antarctica.. that wouldnt have gone down well! yeah, its a great place, but i wonder now about the deeper effects. funny, EVERY summer ppl just balloon and i always assumed it was the carbs and the beer, maybe its more than that. i know 90% of folk there find 'some' issue with sleep.
so look at JKs comment.. sounds like hes not a fan.. :-/ (feck, i have whey protein! i thought it was the right stuff to take.) thats in the brain gut series - we got some homework to do bro! haha

### Sean Waters — 2017-08-02T17:05:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-dr-kruse-use-crystals-in-surgery.9367/post-220724>

I was always sceptical of healing crystals, but since putting the pieces together with Iron and Magnetism, it started to make sense that some of them could deflect or absorb EMF.

I carry Lodeston and Hematite tumble stones, about 10 altogether, in my pocket at all times and I put 2 stones at each corner of my bed before sleep, that is apparently creating an EMF protective tent over the bed.

I was looking to buy some more Lodestone, or Pyrite or Hematite - as I read this article: [http://hibiscusmooncrystalacademy.com/how-to-lower-your-emf-risks-with-crystal-healing/](http://hibiscusmooncrystalacademy.com/how-to-lower-your-emf-risks-with-crystal-healing/)

She explains how some crystals are used for electronics, silicates, and these actually resonate with these frequencies. So quartz and the like are a No-no. But the iron containing stones: Pyrite, Hematite and Lodestone would be beneficial due to their Ferromagnetic properties.

I decided to check the forum and now I need to look into Amethyst Geodes... Either way, for anyone reading - the Iron stones work quite well (my n=1).

### Sean Waters — 2017-08-02T18:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-does-dr-kruse-use-crystals-in-surgery.9367/post-220729>

Yeah I did some more research and I need to Caveat a few parts of the above:
1\. Hematite are often sold as bracelets or in tumblestones (black and shiny), they are always "magnetic", but according to this site: [http://geology.com/minerals/hematite.shtml](http://geology.com/minerals/hematite.shtml), they aren't supposed to be. So they are apparently fakes.
2\. Hematite, pure specimens of the rock are used to provide protection against radiation due to the high iron content.. coincidentally, they can't be found on amazon (UK), it's all the shiny black magnetic bracelets - which I'm not sure about now. However, Ebay (UK) has raw specimens that are about £10 each.
3\. Pyrite is much cheaper than Hematite or Lodestone/Magnetite, but consequently it is only 46% Iron, whereas Hematite is 70% and Lodestone/Magnetite 72.4%. If the Iron (transition metal) is the key player in absorbing the EMF, then we want the latter two rather than Pyrite. It really is Fool's Gold.

Take away (from my research so far):
\- Buy Lodestone tumble stones for your pockets
\-Buy Magnetite/Lodestone and Hematite Raw Rock Specimens for your home
