### ghina — 2014-03-07T03:54:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-113899>

Hi Caroline,

I'm in an older 1955 brick house that is in the middle of a corner lot, which means that I have above ground power lines. The corner of my bedroom is roughly 50 ft from the corner on the street.

Not using grounding sheets, instead I've purchased a grounding cord that plugs into my grounded surge protector and a USB port on my computer. I've also bought a Harapad and an antistatic mat for the computer. I'm not doing anything in particular in my bedroom, but the Magnetico was my move to help with the EMFs in the first place. I only got the lowest gauss pad. And I'm afraid to try a higher gauss pad.

I don't have and had never had cell phones/tvs in my room. The only thing plugged in my clock radio, but it's on the other side of the room and is angled so i have to get up to find the the right angle to read it.

I tried to turn off the wifi in the evening before removing the magnetico and test to see if my symptoms were better and they did not have a noticeable effect. I did call and speak with Dr. Bonlie, I think I was a puzzle to him too as he recommended I speak to a doctor in Baltimore, but that is impractical for me. I listened to two of his podcasts from his site and it makes me very excited, but then very sad. I also bought Pluggz shoes for when I'm out, but that is new and wouldn't have had the initial weird effect. I've also installed FLux on my computer and cell phone.

I have noticed since aerating the water that I need to urinate very soon after drinking the water. I like to interpret that as structured water is replacing bulk water, but I'm not sure. I'm also drinking even when I'm not thirsty. But since I've removed the pad, the neuropathy has really lessened. I'm waking up feeling better, though blood sugar has not yet returned to normal yet.

So I feel like I'm addressing the 3 big things:

1.  Water (aeration, halides removed)
2.  Photoelectric (blocking blue light, resetting my circadian clock using light)
3.  EMF (grounding shoes, grounding cord, laptop shields, ~magnetico~)

Now I have a picture up, so maybe this will be interesting to Jack?!

In the beginning of CT I would get so hot internally, drink two glasses of cold water and still just burn up. I finally had to eat a bowl of ice cream to bring my temperature down. My autonomic systems (temperature, heart rate, blood pressure, etc) do not work well,( poor redox?)

Now I am constantly warm regardless of outside temperature, I am no longer the mother that asks her kid to bundle up when going out in the cold. So he is wearing fewer layers etc. I have heard that any kind of cold differential is technically CTing. So I consider myself to "do" CT, and the feedback from my body seems to confirm it.

But it sounds like I need to do the bath, and that is what might increase redox enough so that I can take advantage of the Magnetico. Did I interpret that correctly?

### ghina — 2014-03-07T15:41:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-113993>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=113921)
>
> People buy beliefs not benefits. This thread is proof positive of this..........

Sorry I don't know what this means?

### ghina — 2014-03-17T00:36:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-115864>

Ok, so I was listening to Dr. Bonlie and he mentioned that mercury is removed from the brain when there is enough of the magnetic field to allow the cell walls to function better and release it. He mentioned taking DMSA just before sleeping on the pad to mop up released mercury. I had already chelated about 100 times (some with DMSA) and was pretty sure there would only be a tiny amount left if any, so it didn't occur to me that this dynamic might be in play. Nonetheless I had some DMSA in my cupboard of thousands of pills, so I tried it. Took some DMSA and slept on the magnetico. Guess what? No tingling or prickliness. I began to suspect that the neuropathy could be from released mercury that I was not removing from my body. My glutathione stores are well below average, and I'm dehydrated. I still have high blood sugar (165), but it's lower than it used to be. I'm going to experiment with the DMSA.

Interestingly I noticed that the sulfur smell was being eliminated from both "number 1" and "number 2". I remember Dr. Tim stating that when your gut is overwhelmed and cannot eliminate that it shunts detox to the kidneys. This could explain the dehydration.

My current stance is do what I was already doing (CT, EpiPaleo, EMF, Water etc) plus: 1) heal the gut, which could put less stress on the kidneys and improve the BUN/Creatinine ratio, which would indicate improved redox. 2)Use DMSA and Magnetico at various amounts to see if there is a correlation over time. 3) continue to monitor blood sugar. If it all trends better, I'll be in the market for a queen Magnetico

I still find the "beliefs not benefits" remark a little negative, unless you want to clarify?

### caroline — 2014-03-17T00:44:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-115865>

Sounds like you are making progress - good job!

### sooperb — 2014-03-17T16:57:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-115938>

My interpretation of what Dr. Jack meant is that people will buy into a belief, a diet or a dogma but you can't gain the benefits if you don't follow the prescription. So, for example, Leptin reset is a strict protocol, eat within 30 mins of rising, big breakfast etc. etc. You can't just follow part of it and expect it to work, you have to follow the instructions to the letter. If you don't, you are fooling yourself into believing that you are doing it and kind of forgetting the bits that don't appeal or substituting your own idea of one of the parts, maybe eating all veggies for breakfast but with lots of fat. When you don't get the results you expected, you blame the prescription and not your lack of adherence. I hope that makes sense even if it a bit long-winded lol.

### Inger — 2014-03-17T21:25:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ghinas-journal.9407/post-116008>

Hi Ghina! That was interesting about the DMSA and the magnetico experience of yours. Do you sleep with a grounding cord on ?

# Thread: Seasonality and Food:  An attempt to deepen my understanding

### Curves — 2014-01-21T19:54:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonality-and-food-an-attempt-to-deepen-my-understanding.9422/post-106485>

Hey Everyone! I've been pondering this question on seasonality and food and looking for some guidance. I know there is a piece missing in my understanding and would appreciate some help in figuring it out. Here goes:

There are two parallel Circadian systems.
One, the master "clock" located in the SCN (suprachiasmatic nucleus) is entrained to light. And not to any other environmental cue.
The other system exists in the peripheral tissues such as the gut and are (or can be) synced to food.
A mismatch occurs when these two are on different pages such as eating carbs in low light cycles.
Here's where I get derailed.
If blue light at night tells your body it's summer and you eat carbs does a mismatch occur?
I'm guessing yes, but wondering what's the missing piece in my understanding.

So nice to be back in this community

### Inger — 2014-01-21T20:34:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonality-and-food-an-attempt-to-deepen-my-understanding.9422/post-106490>

Nice to have you back

IDK but I just think we cannot fool nature... It is just not made to have the same season all year so we would suffer if we artificially make it like that.
and carbs need sunlight... not blue light. They need the real sun and vit D to be healthy for us. So artificial blue light would be no option to make carbs healthy as only the sun makes, when it is strong in summer.

this was very non geeky lol but hope it helps a little still!

PS. I personally think carbs are not even good in summer if we are not tanning a lot, like modern human, indoors all day and much clothing on. I bet carbs are not good in those conditions either

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seasonality-and-food-an-attempt-to-deepen-my-understanding.9422/post-106492>

If you're ill they can also be a bad thing as well if you're not getting outside to spend time to increase your energy potential through the photoelectric effect. as Jack said last night - everything is energy - from the photoelectric effect to magnetism - it all is energy that directly impacts how your body leverages that energy in water and food....

Here's another. You go to MDA and the standard reply to just about any query from about half a dozen people will be "eat more carbs". It must be a Paleosphere meme. Where does it come from? Sisson himself has never pushed carbs.

"I'm overweight" ... "Eat more carbs"
"I've got a fasting bloodsugar of 8.8 mmol/L" ... "Eat more carbs"
"I've got a really complex problem with my hormones even my endocrinologist doesn't understand" ... "Eat more carbs"
"I've a pimple on my ass" ... "Eat more carbs"

### freesia — 2014-01-22T23:09:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/january-2014-webinar.9427/post-106644>

I've been avoiding nuts because avoiding high arginine foods is supposed to (backed up by n=1) help with those experiencing reactivating EBV and other HHVs. Theres likely a bigger picture there I haven't yet got my head around. I do have macadamias and walnuts very occasionally though, and pine nuts (low arginine) regularly but I doubt I eat enough to support a robust bifidobacteria count.
Definitely need to give this some more thought yet and figure out how to best approach given my context.

A couple of interesting (to me anyway lol) things... 2 years implementing protocols here I can now tolerate nightshades...at least some overground ones... tomatoes capsicums and aubergine. No joint swelling at all after eating these this summer! (First summer since 1994!)
I'm game to give potato starch a fair trial personally. I have been making my own potato starch, but that's not sustainable if I want to take bigger doses. 3 medium potatoes makes around 1 Tbsp.
The other thing... I noticed a couple of people, not eating ketogenically, say they can now tolerate potatoes where they couldn't before due to salicylate sensitivity. Possibly they've built up bifidobacteria to where they can degrade salicylates effectively? I'm just guessing there.

Whole nuts would be my preference long term, over _part_ of a potato. Just my initial thoughts....

Jack has stated many times that nut intake should only be done "when inflammation is low". ### Hope — 2014-01-23T01:05:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/january-2014-webinar.9427/post-106650>

> [Paul S said:](/goto/post?id=106643)
>
> For those fixing a bad gut, or with an AI, on one hand it sounds possibly helpful to add resistant starches (avoiding nightshades) and IF'ing to improve gut health based on Jack's biohack. What about raw nuts and cocoa/seeds in those circumstances, however? Recommended, or dependent on each person's N=1 experience with those foods?

Hi Paul, I don't have (based on thyroid AB anyway) any thyroid AI issues. I do have a bad gut that I have been working on for years now. I do eat some nuts. I prefer the soaked kind, but I also do some roasted. I get them in little packets from one brand online and it is great for traveling. THough the actual nut is awesome too. I have tested my n-1 and seem to be fine in some ways, with some nuts. It is my only source of carbs outside veggies (no starchy ones unless you consider carrots starchy - I have not listened to the webinar) but I love them for the fat. I do use raw cocoa powder. I found the most amazing brand after trying a few. This one is totally mild. No bitter taste, no heavy metals, nothing. I gave some to my mom and she said she doesn't believe there is no sugar, but there isn't. I don't eat much of it - you don't need much, but I mix it with the nuts or nut butter as a treat...and wow. Amazing. I don't need ANY fruit, or starch....I do realize I gotta watch it, be careful of getting allergic.....or addicted.

I have eaten some white potatoes in summer and do not tolerate them. I can totally tell I get angry after I eat them. It is crazy I know but true. I have had sweet potatoes in the past but find I don't sleep as well when I eat them. I sleep WAY better without any carbs, besides the nuts (don't see anything bad there it appears). It is really amazing to me, and so worth it. And I thought maybe it was the magnetico and darkness. But no. It is more than that. Definitely fun to hack it.

And so fun to read everyone else's story. I do need to have a period again of not eating nuts at all. Just to see what happens. I am so thankful Dr K shared his results with us, just so cool!!! I wish I could do labs as often too. I really need to be a NP. LOL.

[http://www.wildernessfamilynaturals...oducts-raw-cacao-powder-fermented/CACAOP5.php](http://www.wildernessfamilynaturals.com/product/chocolate-products-raw-cacao-powder-fermented/CACAOP5.php)

The cacao powder is created by cold-pressing raw, organic cacao beans into a pressed cake separating the oil from the protein and fiber. The temperature is never allowed to exceed 120Â° F and is usually 104Â° F (all other cocoa powders are exposed to temperatures as high as 300Â° F!). A high-accuracy thermometer monitors the process. The remaining dry 'cake-like' material is then cold-ground and fine-milled so that only the finest raw cacao particles become the finished product.

This raw cacao powder has antioxidant activity as exhibited by an ORAC score of 1377 μmol TE/g.

Oh and thanks for sharing about the cornmeal. My DH thinks I am nuts....when I say this.

Jeff Leach from the Human Food Project paints a very convincing picture regarding plant fiber/resistant starches with his forray into "aquiring the healthiest gut microbiome in the world" - the guys at American Gut have gone so far as to \*daily\* take DNA samples of their flora - their babies diapers - their wives even carry a q-tip "just in case" - they have tracked what antibiotics does to the flora (twice in a short period of time decimates the gut flora) - so that's when I started up the daily smoothie with everything under the sun in it - haven't gone the potato starch route - they talk about plant fiber so I go with kale and lots of fruits - it's 80 degrees here in so. Cal, and I go outside a lot so what the heck...

[http://humanfoodproject.com/going-f...cquire-healthiest-gut-microbiome-world-heard/](http://humanfoodproject.com/going-feral-one-year-journey-acquire-healthiest-gut-microbiome-world-heard/)

[http://aem.asm.org/content/74/14/4264.full](http://aem.asm.org/content/74/14/4264.full)

Probiotics have been an utter failure - am working on increasing vitamin D to seal the gut wall and prebiotics that feed firmicutes - and methylation, as that's why the gut wall is permeable in the first place...

### Josh — 2014-01-23T07:42:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/january-2014-webinar.9427/post-106693>

Things to consider when gardening....

1\. Soil is built on the right substrates, organic and inorganic. I consider the fiber as a substrate to grow the bugs on.
2\. The fiber, wherever it comes from of whatever type is a substrate and in some cases a food for the microbiome.
3\. Fibers such as Arabinogalactan, typically sourced from Birch as I recall, support butyrate production if the right bugs are there. In my experience, this is the most useful fiber for most people with bad guts.
4\. Protein digestion, especially animal sources is shockingly dependent on manganese and molybdenum for many people. Almost overnight, I went from not being able to digest pork, eggs and any excess of other types and meats with high amounts of fat to be able to handle normal portions. I eat fat of various animal types by the spoon now. However, if I get more inflamed due to stress, a cold, whatever, I may lose the ability to digest these for a few days. I suspect that hormones may play a significant role for many people, especially women as well.
5\. If I have the right cofactors such as micronutrients and whatever else my liver may need, I seem to be able to shift my diet without disturbing my guts as evidenced by Bristol stool chart quality and regularity. This is a big if. When people are more inflamed and adrenal stressed, it seems that they handle a plant based diet with low fat protein such as fish in or chicken in moderate quantities better than the higher fat and protein paleo and epi-paleo prescriptions.
6\. Don't forget seasons and travel to other places with other electromagnetics and temperatures, etc...
7\. Parasites will throw the whole thing off quickly and most people have no clue as to whether they have them. They become active and a big deal when people get stressed from other things, you name it. I find they come out to play on the tail of acute respiratory infections in many people.

### freesia — 2014-01-23T23:50:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/january-2014-webinar.9427/post-106831>

I have actually had a mouldy pumpkin seed - years ago. Horrible, and I didn't touch them again for ages! I'm sensitive to mould (though CT makes me stronger in the face of accidental exposures).
The ones I've been buying seem fine....no ill effects. I don't use them as a regular part of my diet, more intermittently.
